title: Kabuli Pulao
summary: Afghanistan's national rice dish with tender lamb, caramelized carrots and raisins
description: Kabuli Pulao is the crown jewel of Afghan cuisine, a fragrant rice pilaf layered with slow-cooked lamb, sweet caramelized carrots and plump raisins.
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice|500g lamb shoulder, cubed|2 large carrots, julienned|1/2 cup raisins|1/4 cup vegetable oil|1 large onion, thinly sliced|1 tsp cumin seeds|4 green cardamom pods|1/2 tsp ground cinnamon|1 tsp salt|3 cups water
instructions: Rinse rice and soak in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain.|Heat oil in a heavy pot, fry onion slices until deep golden brown, remove half for garnish.|Add lamb cubes to the pot, brown on all sides, then add cumin, cardamom, cinnamon, salt and water.|Cover and simmer on low heat for 45 minutes until lamb is very tender.|In a separate pan, fry julienned carrots in a little oil until caramelized, add raisins and cook 1 minute.|Par-boil the soaked rice in salted boiling water until half cooked, about 5 minutes, then drain.|Layer the par-boiled rice over the lamb, top with caramelized carrots and raisins, cover tightly and steam on very low heat for 25 minutes.

title: Mantu
summary: Afghan steamed dumplings filled with spiced ground beef and onions, topped with yogurt and tomato sauce
description: Mantu are delicate steamed dumplings that showcase Afghan cuisine at its finest, filled with seasoned beef and onions, served with a garlic yogurt and a spiced tomato-lentil sauce.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup water for dough|1 tsp salt|400g ground beef|2 large onions, finely diced|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp black pepper|1/2 tsp ground cumin|1 cup plain yogurt|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 cup crushed tomatoes|1/2 cup yellow split peas, cooked
instructions: Mix flour, salt and water to form a smooth dough, knead 10 minutes, rest covered 30 minutes.|Combine ground beef, diced onions, coriander, cumin, pepper and salt for the filling.|Roll dough very thin, cut into 3-inch squares, place a tablespoon of filling in each, fold and pinch edges to seal.|Lightly oil a steamer basket, arrange dumplings without touching, steam for 40 minutes.|Make yogurt sauce: stir garlic into yogurt with a pinch of salt.|Make tomato sauce: simmer crushed tomatoes with cooked split peas, salt and a pinch of turmeric for 15 minutes.|Arrange steamed mantu on a platter, drizzle yogurt sauce then tomato-lentil sauce on top, and serve.

title: Bolani
summary: Crispy Afghan flatbread stuffed with seasoned potato or leek filling
description: Bolani is a popular Afghan street food — thin flatbread stuffed with spiced mashed potatoes, pan-fried until golden and crispy on both sides.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|3/4 cup warm water|1 tsp salt|3 large potatoes, boiled and mashed|1 bunch green onions, chopped|1/2 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp ground cumin|1/4 tsp chili flakes|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|vegetable oil for frying
instructions: Mix flour, salt and water into a soft dough, knead 5 minutes, cover and rest 20 minutes.|Combine mashed potatoes with green onions, coriander, cumin, chili flakes, cilantro and salt.|Divide dough into 8 balls, roll each very thin into an oval shape.|Spread potato filling on one half of each oval, fold the other half over and press edges to seal.|Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat, fry each bolani 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crispy.|Drain on paper towels and serve warm with yogurt or chutney.

title: Aushak
summary: Afghan leek-filled dumplings served with meat sauce and garlic yogurt
description: Aushak are tender boiled dumplings filled with seasoned leeks, served in layers with a garlicky yogurt and a spiced ground meat sauce.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup water|1 tsp salt|3 cups leeks, finely chopped|1 tsp ground coriander|1/4 tsp red pepper flakes|250g ground beef|1 cup crushed tomatoes|1 tsp turmeric|1 cup plain yogurt|2 cloves garlic, minced|dried mint for garnish
instructions: Make dough with flour, water and salt, knead until smooth, rest 20 minutes.|Toss chopped leeks with coriander, red pepper flakes and a pinch of salt for filling.|Roll dough thin, cut into 3-inch circles, fill each with a tablespoon of leek mixture, fold and seal edges.|Boil dumplings in salted water for 8-10 minutes until they float and dough is tender.|Brown ground beef in a pan, add tomatoes and turmeric, simmer 15 minutes into a thick sauce.|Mix yogurt with minced garlic and salt.|Layer dumplings on a plate, spoon yogurt over them, then meat sauce, and sprinkle dried mint.

title: Shorwa
summary: Hearty Afghan lamb and vegetable soup with warming spices
description: Shorwa is a comforting Afghan soup made with bone-in lamb, potatoes, carrots and tomatoes, seasoned with cumin and coriander.
ingredients: 500g lamb shank or bone-in pieces|1 large onion, diced|2 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|2 tomatoes, chopped|1 tsp ground cumin|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp turmeric|2 tbsp vegetable oil|6 cups water|salt and black pepper|fresh cilantro for garnish
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot, brown lamb pieces on all sides.|Add diced onion, cook until softened.|Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, stir 1 minute.|Add tomatoes, potatoes, carrots and water, bring to a boil.|Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 1.5 hours until lamb is falling off the bone.|Season with salt and pepper, garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with naan.

title: Qabili Chai
summary: Traditional Afghan green tea with cardamom
description: Qabili Chai is the everyday green tea of Afghanistan, brewed strong and flavored with green cardamom, served without milk.
ingredients: 2 tsp loose green tea leaves|4 cups water|3 green cardamom pods, lightly crushed|sugar to taste
instructions: Bring water to a boil in a teapot.|Add green tea leaves and crushed cardamom pods.|Steep for 5 minutes with the lid on.|Strain into cups, add sugar to taste and serve.

title: Lawand
summary: Refreshing Afghan yogurt drink with mint and cucumber
description: Lawand is a cooling Afghan beverage made from diluted yogurt blended with fresh mint and cucumber, perfect for hot days.
ingredients: 2 cups plain yogurt|1 cup cold water|1 small cucumber, peeled and grated|2 tbsp fresh mint, chopped|1/2 tsp salt|ice cubes
instructions: Whisk yogurt and cold water together until smooth.|Stir in grated cucumber, chopped mint and salt.|Pour over ice cubes in glasses and serve chilled.

title: Naan-e-Afghani
summary: Traditional Afghan flatbread baked in a tandoor or oven
description: Naan-e-Afghani is the daily bread of Afghanistan, a soft yet chewy flatbread with a signature oblong shape, traditionally baked slapped against the walls of a clay tandoor.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup whole wheat flour|1 packet active dry yeast|1 tsp sugar|1 tsp salt|1 1/4 cups warm water|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tbsp nigella seeds
instructions: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water, let stand 10 minutes until foamy.|Mix flours and salt, add yeast mixture and oil, knead into a soft elastic dough for 10 minutes.|Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled.|Divide into 4 portions, shape each into an oval about 10 inches long.|Press nigella seeds into the surface, score lines with fingers lengthwise.|Bake on a preheated baking stone at 260°C (500°F) for 6-8 minutes until puffed and lightly charred.

title: Qorma-e-Sabzi
summary: Afghan spinach and lamb stew with aromatic spices
description: Qorma-e-Sabzi is a richly spiced Afghan stew of tender lamb simmered with spinach, leeks and fresh herbs, served over white rice.
ingredients: 500g lamb shoulder, cubed|300g fresh spinach, chopped|1 bunch leeks, sliced|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp turmeric|1/4 tsp cinnamon|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 cup water|salt and pepper
instructions: Heat oil, brown lamb cubes on all sides, remove and set aside.|In the same pot, sauté onion until golden, add garlic and cook 1 minute.|Add coriander, turmeric and cinnamon, stir 30 seconds.|Return lamb, add leeks, spinach and water.|Cover and simmer on low for 1 hour until lamb is tender and sauce is thick.|Season with salt and pepper, serve over steamed basmati rice.

title: Firni
summary: Creamy Afghan milk pudding flavored with rosewater and cardamom
description: Firni is a silky Afghan dessert pudding made from ground rice, milk, sugar and rosewater, garnished with crushed pistachios.
ingredients: 1/2 cup rice flour or finely ground rice|4 cups whole milk|1/2 cup sugar|2 tbsp rosewater|1/4 tsp ground cardamom|2 tbsp crushed pistachios|1 tbsp slivered almonds
instructions: Dissolve rice flour in 1 cup of cold milk, stir until no lumps remain.|Heat remaining milk in a saucepan until simmering.|Slowly pour the rice flour mixture into the hot milk, stirring constantly.|Cook on medium-low heat, stirring continuously, for 15-20 minutes until thickened to a pudding consistency.|Remove from heat, stir in sugar, rosewater and cardamom.|Pour into serving bowls, chill for at least 2 hours.|Garnish with crushed pistachios and slivered almonds before serving.

title: Sheer Yakh
summary: Afghan ice cream made with milk, rosewater and salep
description: Sheer Yakh is Afghanistan's traditional ice cream, distinctively chewy and stretchy thanks to salep powder, flavored with rosewater and pistachios.
ingredients: 4 cups whole milk|1 cup heavy cream|3/4 cup sugar|2 tbsp rosewater|1 tsp salep powder (or 1 tbsp cornstarch)|1/4 cup crushed pistachios|1/4 tsp ground cardamom
instructions: Heat milk and cream in a saucepan over medium heat.|Dissolve salep powder in a little cold milk and stir into the pot.|Add sugar, stir until dissolved and mixture thickens slightly, about 10 minutes.|Remove from heat, stir in rosewater and cardamom, cool to room temperature.|Churn in an ice cream maker according to manufacturer instructions.|Fold in crushed pistachios, freeze for at least 4 hours until firm.

title: Lamb Kebab Afghani
summary: Marinated Afghan lamb skewers grilled over charcoal
description: Afghan lamb kebabs are chunks of lamb marinated in yogurt, onion juice and spices, then skewered and grilled over hot charcoal until charred and juicy.
ingredients: 800g boneless lamb leg, cut into 1.5 inch cubes|1 large onion, grated|1/2 cup plain yogurt|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp ground cumin|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp black pepper|1 tsp salt|1/4 tsp cayenne pepper|metal or soaked wooden skewers
instructions: Squeeze grated onion to extract juice, discard pulp.|Mix onion juice, yogurt, oil, cumin, coriander, pepper, cayenne and salt in a bowl.|Add lamb cubes, toss to coat evenly, marinate in the fridge for at least 4 hours or overnight.|Thread marinated lamb onto skewers.|Grill over hot charcoal or a very hot grill for 3-4 minutes per side until nicely charred but still juicy inside.|Serve immediately with naan, sliced onions and fresh cilantro.

title: Pakowra
summary: Afghan vegetable fritters in a spiced chickpea batter
description: Pakowra are crispy deep-fried fritters made with sliced potatoes and onions dipped in a spiced chickpea flour batter, a beloved Afghan street snack.
ingredients: 1 cup chickpea flour (besan)|1/2 cup water|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp ground cumin|1/2 tsp ground coriander|1/4 tsp cayenne pepper|1/4 tsp baking soda|2 large potatoes, thinly sliced|1 large onion, sliced into rings|vegetable oil for deep frying
instructions: Whisk chickpea flour, water, salt, cumin, coriander, cayenne and baking soda into a smooth batter.|Heat oil to 180°C (350°F) in a deep pot.|Dip potato slices and onion rings into the batter, coating well.|Fry in batches for 3-4 minutes until golden brown and crispy.|Drain on paper towels and serve hot with green chutney.

title: Mastawa
summary: Afghan rice and mung bean soup with yogurt
description: Mastawa is a warming Afghan soup that combines rice, mung beans and chunks of lamb in a tomato-based broth, finished with a tangy yogurt topping.
ingredients: 250g lamb, cubed|1/2 cup short-grain rice|1/2 cup mung beans, soaked 2 hours|1 large onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 tsp turmeric|1 tsp ground coriander|6 cups water|2 tbsp oil|salt and pepper|1 cup plain yogurt|dried mint
instructions: Heat oil, brown lamb and onion until golden.|Add tomatoes, turmeric and coriander, cook 3 minutes.|Add water, soaked mung beans, bring to a boil.|Simmer 30 minutes, then add rice and cook another 20 minutes until rice and beans are tender.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve in bowls topped with a generous spoon of yogurt and a sprinkle of dried mint.

title: Kofta Chalao
summary: Afghan meatballs in a spiced tomato sauce served over rice
description: Kofta Chalao features well-seasoned beef meatballs simmered in a rich tomato and onion sauce, traditionally ladled over fluffy white rice.
ingredients: 500g ground beef|1 onion, grated|1 tsp ground cumin|1/2 tsp ground coriander|1/4 tsp cinnamon|salt and pepper|2 tbsp oil|1 onion, diced|3 tomatoes, pureed|1/2 tsp turmeric|1 cup water|cooked basmati rice for serving
instructions: Mix ground beef with grated onion, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt and pepper, form into walnut-sized balls.|Heat oil in a pan, brown meatballs on all sides, remove.|In the same pan, sauté diced onion until soft, add pureed tomatoes and turmeric.|Add water, bring to a simmer, return meatballs to the sauce.|Cover and cook on low heat for 30 minutes until sauce thickens.|Serve meatballs and sauce ladled over steamed basmati rice.

title: Borani Banjan
summary: Afghan eggplant dish with tomato sauce and garlic yogurt
description: Borani Banjan layers pan-fried eggplant slices with a spiced tomato sauce and tangy garlic yogurt, a classic Afghan vegetable dish.
ingredients: 2 large eggplants, sliced 1cm thick|1/4 cup vegetable oil|1 large onion, sliced|3 tomatoes, diced|1 green bell pepper, diced|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp turmeric|salt and pepper|1 cup plain yogurt|2 cloves garlic, minced|dried mint
instructions: Salt eggplant slices and let sit 20 minutes, pat dry.|Fry eggplant slices in oil until golden on both sides, drain on paper towels.|In the same pan, sauté onion until golden, add bell pepper, tomatoes, coriander and turmeric.|Cook sauce 10 minutes until thick.|Layer fried eggplant in a serving dish, spoon tomato sauce over top.|Mix yogurt with garlic and salt, drizzle over the dish.|Garnish with dried mint and serve warm with rice or naan.

title: Sholeh Zard
summary: Afghan saffron rice pudding topped with cinnamon and nuts
description: Sholeh Zard is a vibrant yellow rice pudding made with saffron, rosewater and cardamom, decorated with cinnamon, almonds and pistachios.
ingredients: 1 cup short-grain rice|6 cups water|1 cup sugar|1/4 tsp saffron threads, dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water|2 tbsp rosewater|1/4 tsp ground cardamom|2 tbsp butter|2 tbsp slivered almonds|2 tbsp crushed pistachios|ground cinnamon for garnish
instructions: Rinse rice and boil in water until very soft and broken down, about 40 minutes.|Add sugar, saffron water and butter, stir well.|Continue cooking on low heat, stirring frequently, for another 20 minutes until thick and pudding-like.|Remove from heat, stir in rosewater and cardamom.|Pour into a serving dish or individual bowls, smooth the top.|Garnish with slivered almonds, pistachios and cinnamon designs, chill before serving.

title: Do Pyaza
summary: Afghan lamb stew with a double quantity of onions
description: Do Pyaza means "two onions" — this Afghan stew uses a generous amount of onions cooked in two stages, creating a deeply sweet and savory lamb dish.
ingredients: 600g lamb, cubed|4 large onions, 2 sliced and 2 diced|3 tbsp oil|2 tomatoes, chopped|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp coriander|1/2 tsp turmeric|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1 cup water|salt and pepper|fresh cilantro
instructions: Heat oil, fry sliced onions until deep brown and caramelized, remove half and set aside.|Add lamb to the pot, brown well on all sides.|Add diced onions, tomatoes and all spices, stir.|Add water, bring to boil, then reduce to a simmer.|Cover and cook 1.5 hours until lamb is very tender.|Stir in the reserved caramelized onions, cook 5 more minutes.|Garnish with cilantro and serve with rice or naan.

title: Tashkent Salad
summary: Afghan-style salad of beef tongue, radish and egg
description: Tashkent Salad is a Central Asian-influenced Afghan salad combining tender boiled beef tongue, sliced radishes, boiled eggs, dressed in a creamy mayo dressing.
ingredients: 300g beef tongue, boiled and sliced|4 radishes, thinly sliced|3 hard-boiled eggs, sliced|1 small onion, thinly sliced|1/2 cup mayonnaise|2 tbsp fresh dill, chopped|1 tbsp lemon juice|salt and pepper
instructions: Boil beef tongue in salted water for 2 hours until tender, peel skin and slice thinly.|Arrange sliced tongue, radishes and eggs on a platter.|Scatter sliced onion over the top.|Mix mayonnaise with lemon juice, salt and pepper.|Drizzle dressing over the salad, garnish with dill and serve chilled.

title: Sambosa
summary: Afghan fried pastry triangles filled with spiced potatoes and ground beef
description: Sambosa are crispy triangular pastries filled with a savory mix of spiced ground beef, potatoes and herbs, deep-fried until golden — a favorite Afghan appetizer.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/4 cup vegetable oil|1/2 cup warm water|1/2 tsp salt|200g ground beef|2 potatoes, boiled and diced small|1 small onion, finely diced|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp ground cumin|1/4 tsp chili flakes|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|oil for deep frying
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, oil, salt and water, knead until smooth, rest 20 minutes.|Brown ground beef with onion, add spices, stir in diced potatoes and cilantro, cool.|Roll dough thin, cut into strips about 3 inches wide.|Place a spoonful of filling at one end, fold into triangles, seal edges with water.|Heat frying oil to 175°C (350°F), fry sambosas until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.|Drain on paper towels, serve hot with mint chutney.

title: Tavë Kosi
summary: Albanian baked lamb and rice casserole with a tangy egg-yogurt custard
description: Tavë Kosi is Albania's national dish — tender lamb pieces baked in a creamy egg and yogurt custard over rice, golden and set like a savory flan.
ingredients: 600g lamb shoulder, cubed|2 cups plain yogurt|4 eggs|3 tbsp butter|3 tbsp all-purpose flour|1 cup rice, par-boiled|1 tsp dried oregano|salt and pepper|1/2 tsp garlic powder
instructions: Season lamb with salt, pepper and oregano, sear in butter until browned on all sides.|Spread par-boiled rice in a buttered baking dish, arrange lamb on top.|Whisk eggs, yogurt, flour, garlic powder and salt until smooth.|Pour the yogurt mixture over the lamb and rice.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for about 50-60 minutes until the top is golden and custard is set.|Let rest 10 minutes before cutting into squares and serving.

title: Fërgesë Tirane
summary: Tirana-style baked peppers, tomatoes and feta cheese
description: Fërgesë Tirane is a hearty Albanian dish from the capital, combining roasted peppers, ripe tomatoes and crumbled feta baked until bubbly.
ingredients: 4 red bell peppers, roasted and peeled|4 ripe tomatoes, peeled and chopped|200g feta cheese, crumbled|3 tbsp butter|2 tbsp all-purpose flour|1 cup milk|salt and pepper|1/2 tsp paprika
instructions: Roast peppers over a flame or under the broiler, peel, seed and chop.|Melt butter in a saucepan, stir in flour and cook 1 minute, then gradually add milk stirring until thick.|Add chopped tomatoes, peppers and paprika, cook 5 minutes.|Stir in crumbled feta, season with salt and pepper.|Transfer to a baking dish, bake at 200°C (400°F) for 20 minutes until bubbly and golden on top.|Serve hot with crusty bread.

title: Byrek
summary: Albanian layered filo pastry pie filled with cheese or spinach
description: Byrek is a flaky Albanian pie made with thin layers of pastry dough filled with a mixture of feta cheese and eggs, or spinach and onion.
ingredients: 500g filo pastry sheets|400g feta cheese, crumbled|3 eggs|200g cottage cheese|1/4 cup vegetable oil|1/4 cup melted butter|1/2 cup plain yogurt|1 tsp baking powder|salt and pepper
instructions: Mix feta, cottage cheese, eggs, yogurt, baking powder, salt and pepper in a bowl.|Brush a large baking pan with oil.|Layer 3-4 filo sheets in the pan, brushing each with oil-butter mixture.|Spread half the cheese filling evenly.|Add another 3-4 layers of filo, brushing each, then the rest of the filling.|Top with remaining filo layers, brush top generously with butter.|Score the top into squares, bake at 180°C (350°F) for 35-40 minutes until golden and flaky.

title: Jani Me Fasule
summary: Albanian white bean soup with tomatoes and herbs
description: Jani Me Fasule is a comforting Albanian bean soup simmered with tomatoes, onion, paprika and olive oil — hearty, simple and deeply satisfying.
ingredients: 2 cups dried white beans, soaked overnight|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 tbsp tomato paste|1/4 cup olive oil|1 tsp sweet paprika|1/2 tsp dried oregano|6 cups water|salt and pepper|fresh parsley for garnish
instructions: Drain soaked beans, place in a pot with fresh water, boil 10 minutes then drain.|Heat olive oil, sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add tomato paste and paprika, stir 1 minute.|Add beans, diced tomatoes, oregano and 6 cups water.|Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 1-1.5 hours until beans are very tender.|Season with salt and pepper, garnish with parsley, serve with crusty bread.

title: Qofte Fërguara
summary: Albanian pan-fried meatballs with herbs and feta
description: Qofte Fërguara are savory Albanian meatballs made with a mix of beef, bread, feta and fresh herbs, pan-fried until crispy outside and moist within.
ingredients: 500g ground beef|1 slice bread, soaked in milk and squeezed|50g feta cheese, crumbled|1 egg|1 small onion, grated|2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|2 tbsp fresh mint, chopped|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper|1/4 cup flour for coating|vegetable oil for frying
instructions: Combine ground beef, squeezed bread, feta, egg, onion, parsley, mint, salt and pepper.|Knead the mixture well, shape into flat oval patties.|Lightly coat each patty in flour.|Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.|Fry patties 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.|Drain on paper towels, serve with lemon wedges and salad.

title: Trilece
summary: Albanian three-milk cake soaked in a sweet caramel sauce
description: Trilece is Albania's beloved version of tres leches — a sponge cake soaked in three kinds of milk and topped with a layer of burnt caramel.
ingredients: 6 eggs|1 cup sugar|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 tsp baking powder|1 cup whole milk|1 cup heavy cream|1 can (400ml) sweetened condensed milk|1 cup sugar for caramel|3 tbsp water for caramel
instructions: Beat eggs and 1 cup sugar until thick and pale, about 5 minutes.|Fold in flour and baking powder gently.|Pour into a buttered 9x13 baking pan, bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25 minutes until golden.|Mix whole milk, heavy cream and condensed milk together.|Poke the warm cake all over with a fork, pour the milk mixture evenly over it.|Refrigerate for at least 4 hours until fully absorbed.|Make caramel: heat 1 cup sugar with water until dark amber, pour over the chilled cake.|Let caramel set, then cut into squares and serve cold.

title: Pite me Mish
summary: Albanian meat pie with hand-stretched dough layers
description: Pite me Mish is a traditional Albanian savory pie featuring layers of hand-stretched thin dough filled with seasoned ground meat and onions.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup warm water|1 tsp salt|1/4 cup vegetable oil|500g ground lamb or beef|2 large onions, diced|1 tsp black pepper|1/2 tsp paprika|3 tbsp butter, melted|1 egg yolk for glazing
instructions: Mix flour, salt, water and oil into a soft dough, knead 5 minutes, rest covered 30 minutes.|Brown ground meat with onions, season with salt, pepper and paprika, cool.|Divide dough into 6 balls, roll each very thin on an oiled surface.|Layer 3 thin sheets in a buttered round pan, brushing each with melted butter.|Spread meat filling evenly over the layers.|Cover with remaining 3 dough sheets, brushing each with butter.|Brush top with egg yolk, score into portions.|Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 35-40 minutes until golden and crispy.

title: Shishqebab
summary: Albanian-style grilled meat skewers with vegetables
description: Shishqebab Albanian style features cubes of marinated lamb or beef alternated with peppers and onions on skewers, grilled over charcoal.
ingredients: 600g lamb or beef, cut into 2cm cubes|2 bell peppers, cut into chunks|2 onions, quartered|3 tbsp olive oil|1 tbsp red wine vinegar|1 tsp dried oregano|1 tsp paprika|salt and pepper|metal skewers
instructions: Marinate meat in olive oil, vinegar, oregano, paprika, salt and pepper for at least 2 hours.|Thread meat, peppers and onion pieces alternately onto skewers.|Grill over hot coals or a very hot grill, turning every 2-3 minutes.|Cook about 10-12 minutes total for medium, adjusting to your preference.|Serve immediately with flatbread and a tomato-cucumber salad.

title: Flija
summary: Albanian layered crepe cake baked with cream between each layer
description: Flija is a unique Albanian specialty — dozens of thin crepe-like layers, each brushed with cream or butter, slowly baked in a sač (domed lid) until golden.
ingredients: 4 cups all-purpose flour|4 cups milk|4 eggs|1 tsp salt|1 cup heavy cream|1/2 cup melted butter|1 cup plain yogurt
instructions: Whisk flour, milk, eggs and salt into a smooth thin batter.|Heat a large round baking pan (traditionally under a sač), brush with butter.|Pour a thin layer of batter, cook until just set.|Spread a thin layer of cream-yogurt mixture over it.|Repeat: add another thin layer of batter, cook, add cream, for at least 15 layers.|For oven method: bake at 200°C (400°F) adding one layer at a time, returning to oven between each layer for 5 minutes.|When all layers are done, bake a final 15 minutes until top is golden.|Cut into wedges like a cake and serve warm.

title: Speca të Mbushur
summary: Albanian stuffed peppers filled with rice and ground meat
description: Speca të Mbushur are sweet bell peppers stuffed with a savory mixture of rice, ground meat, tomatoes and herbs, baked until tender.
ingredients: 6 bell peppers, tops cut and seeds removed|300g ground beef|1 cup rice, rinsed|1 onion, finely diced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|1 tsp paprika|1/2 tsp allspice|salt and pepper|1 cup tomato passata|1 cup water|2 tbsp olive oil
instructions: Mix ground beef, rice, diced onion, tomatoes, parsley, paprika, allspice, salt and pepper.|Stuff each pepper generously with the filling, place upright in a baking dish.|Mix tomato passata with water and olive oil, pour around the peppers.|Cover dish with foil, bake at 180°C (350°F) for 50 minutes.|Remove foil, bake another 15 minutes until peppers are soft and tops are lightly charred.|Serve warm with yogurt on the side.

title: Komposto Albania
summary: Albanian fruit compote dessert with seasonal fruits
description: Komposto is a traditional Albanian dessert of seasonal fruits poached in a light sugar syrup, served chilled as a refreshing end to a meal.
ingredients: 4 ripe pears or peaches, peeled and halved|1 cup sugar|4 cups water|1 cinnamon stick|3 whole cloves|1 strip lemon zest|1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: Combine water, sugar, cinnamon stick, cloves and lemon zest in a saucepan.|Bring to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves.|Add fruit halves, reduce heat and simmer gently for 15-20 minutes until fruit is tender but holds its shape.|Remove from heat, stir in lemon juice.|Cool to room temperature, then chill in the refrigerator.|Serve the fruit in bowls with plenty of the chilled syrup.

title: Lakror
summary: Albanian leek and cornmeal pie from southern Albania
description: Lakror is a rustic Albanian pie from the Korçë region, featuring a filling of leeks and onions encased in a cornmeal and wheat flour crust.
ingredients: 1 cup cornmeal|2 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup warm water|1/4 cup olive oil|1 tsp salt|4 cups leeks, thinly sliced|2 onions, sliced|3 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
instructions: Mix cornmeal, flour, salt, water and olive oil into a dough, knead briefly, rest 20 minutes.|Sauté leeks and onions in butter until very soft, season with salt, pepper and red pepper flakes.|Divide dough in two, roll the first half to fit a greased baking pan.|Spread the leek filling evenly over the dough.|Roll the second half and place on top, pinch edges to seal.|Brush top with a little olive oil, poke a few holes for steam.|Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 40 minutes until golden and crusty.

title: Paçe Koke
summary: Albanian trotter soup traditionally eaten for breakfast
description: Paçe Koke is a rich Albanian soup made from slow-cooked lamb or veal trotters, seasoned with garlic and vinegar — a hearty breakfast dish.
ingredients: 1 kg lamb or veal trotters, cleaned|1 large onion, halved|4 cloves garlic, crushed|2 bay leaves|1 tsp black peppercorns|8 cups water|3 tbsp white wine vinegar|salt|crusty bread for serving
instructions: Place trotters in a large pot with onion, bay leaves and peppercorns.|Cover with water, bring to a boil, skim off any foam.|Reduce heat and simmer for 4-5 hours until meat falls off the bones.|Remove trotters, shred meat, discard bones.|Strain broth, return to pot with shredded meat.|Stir in crushed garlic and vinegar, season with salt.|Serve very hot in deep bowls with crusty bread for dipping.

title: Reçel Fiku
summary: Albanian fig jam preserve made with whole figs
description: Reçel Fiku is a traditional Albanian preserve of whole figs simmered in sugar syrup until glossy and jammy, often served with coffee.
ingredients: 1 kg fresh figs|600g sugar|1 cup water|juice of 1 lemon|1/4 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: Wash figs, trim stems but leave whole.|Make syrup: dissolve sugar in water over medium heat, add lemon juice.|Add figs to the syrup, bring to a gentle boil.|Reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes, stirring gently occasionally, until figs are translucent and syrup thickens.|Stir in vanilla extract.|Let cool, then transfer to sterilized jars.|Serve with bread, cheese or alongside Albanian coffee.

title: Sufllaqe
summary: Albanian pita wrap with grilled meat, fries and tzatziki
description: Sufllaqe is Albania's popular street food — grilled meat wrapped in warm pita bread with french fries, fresh vegetables and a garlic yogurt sauce.
ingredients: 500g chicken thigh or lamb, sliced thin|2 tbsp olive oil|1 tsp paprika|1 tsp dried oregano|salt and pepper|4 pita breads|2 cups french fries, cooked|2 tomatoes, sliced|1 onion, sliced|1 cup yogurt|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: Marinate sliced meat in olive oil, paprika, oregano, salt and pepper for 1 hour.|Grill meat on high heat until charred and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes.|Mix yogurt with garlic, lemon juice and salt for the sauce.|Warm pita breads on the grill briefly.|Assemble: lay meat on pita, add fries, tomato, onion and drizzle sauce.|Roll up tightly and serve immediately.

title: Gjellë me Perime
summary: Albanian mixed vegetable stew with olive oil
description: Gjellë me Perime is a traditional Albanian vegetable stew combining seasonal vegetables slow-cooked in olive oil and tomatoes.
ingredients: 2 zucchini, cubed|2 bell peppers, cubed|2 potatoes, cubed|1 eggplant, cubed|3 tomatoes, chopped|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1/4 cup olive oil|1 tsp paprika|1/2 tsp dried oregano|salt and pepper|fresh parsley
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot, sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add all cubed vegetables, stir to coat with oil.|Add chopped tomatoes, paprika, oregano, salt and pepper.|Cover and cook on low heat for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all vegetables are very tender.|Adjust seasoning, garnish with parsley and serve with crusty bread.

title: Ashure Albania
summary: Albanian wheat berry and dried fruit pudding
description: Ashure is a sweet dessert pudding of boiled wheat berries, chickpeas, dried fruits and nuts, enjoyed in Albania during special occasions.
ingredients: 1 cup wheat berries, soaked overnight|1/4 cup chickpeas, soaked overnight|1/2 cup sugar|6 cups water|1/4 cup raisins|1/4 cup dried apricots, chopped|1/4 cup walnuts, chopped|2 tbsp rosewater|1 tsp cinnamon|pomegranate seeds for garnish
instructions: Boil soaked wheat berries and chickpeas in water until very soft, about 1 hour.|Add sugar, raisins and dried apricots, cook another 15 minutes.|Stir in rosewater and cinnamon.|If too thick, add a little water; if too thin, cook a bit longer.|Pour into bowls, let cool to room temperature.|Garnish with walnuts and pomegranate seeds before serving.

title: Raki Albania
description: Raki is Albania's beloved grape brandy, distilled from fermented grape pomace — clear, strong and traditionally homemade across the country.
ingredients: 10 kg grape pomace (skins, seeds, stems from winemaking)|5 kg sugar|20 liters water|distillation equipment
instructions: Place grape pomace in a large fermentation vessel.|Dissolve sugar in water, pour over the pomace.|Cover loosely and let ferment for 2-3 weeks, stirring daily, until bubbling stops.|Transfer fermented mash to a traditional copper still.|Distill slowly, collecting the clear spirit that flows out, discarding the first 50ml (foreshots).|Collect the heart of the distillation, stopping when strength drops below 40%.|Dilute with water if needed to reach desired strength (typically 40-45% ABV).

title: Misër me Djathë
summary: Albanian cornbread baked with feta cheese
description: Misër me Djathë is a rustic Albanian cornbread studded with chunks of salty feta cheese, baked until golden with a crispy crust.
ingredients: 2 cups fine cornmeal|1 cup all-purpose flour|200g feta cheese, crumbled|2 eggs|1 cup plain yogurt|1/2 cup milk|1/4 cup butter, melted|1 tsp baking powder|1 tsp salt
instructions: Mix cornmeal, flour, baking powder and salt in a large bowl.|In another bowl, whisk eggs, yogurt, milk and melted butter.|Combine wet and dry ingredients, stir until just mixed.|Fold in crumbled feta cheese.|Pour batter into a greased baking pan.|Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 30-35 minutes until golden brown and a toothpick comes out clean.|Let cool slightly, cut into squares and serve warm.

title: Kabuni
summary: Albanian sweet rice with caramelized sugar and raisins
description: Kabuni is a festive Albanian rice dessert from Elbasan, made by cooking rice in a rich caramel sauce with butter, raisins and cinnamon.
ingredients: 2 cups short-grain rice|1 cup sugar|4 cups water|1/4 cup butter|1/2 cup raisins|1/4 tsp cinnamon|1/4 tsp ground cloves|2 tbsp slivered almonds
instructions: Boil rice in water until almost tender, drain.|In a heavy pot, melt sugar over medium heat without stirring until it turns deep amber caramel.|Carefully add butter (it will sizzle), stir until combined.|Add drained rice, raisins, cinnamon and cloves, stir gently.|Cook on very low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.|Pack into a mold or bowl, press firmly, let rest 5 minutes.|Invert onto a plate, garnish with slivered almonds, serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Khorovats
summary: Armenian charcoal-grilled meat skewers, the king of Armenian BBQ
description: Khorovats is the Armenian art of grilling marinated pork, lamb or beef over hot charcoal — the centerpiece of every Armenian gathering.
ingredients: 1 kg pork shoulder or lamb, cut into 2-inch cubes|2 large onions, grated|1/4 cup dry red wine|3 tbsp olive oil|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp salt|1 tsp dried basil|1 tsp ground sumac|metal skewers|lavash bread for serving
instructions: Squeeze grated onions to extract juice, mix juice with wine, oil, pepper, basil, sumac and salt.|Add meat cubes, toss well, marinate refrigerated for at least 6 hours or overnight.|Thread meat onto flat metal skewers, shaking off excess marinade.|Grill over very hot charcoal, turning every 3-4 minutes, for 12-15 minutes total.|Remove from skewers onto a bed of lavash bread.|Serve with grilled tomatoes, peppers and fresh herbs.

title: Dolma Armenia
summary: Armenian grape leaf rolls stuffed with spiced lamb and rice
description: Armenian Dolma features tender grape leaves wrapped around a savory filling of ground lamb, rice, herbs and warm spices, simmered until meltingly soft.
ingredients: 40 grape leaves (jarred or fresh, blanched)|500g ground lamb|1 cup short-grain rice|1 large onion, finely diced|3 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|2 tbsp fresh dill, chopped|1 tbsp fresh mint, chopped|1 tsp allspice|1/2 tsp cinnamon|salt and pepper|2 tbsp tomato paste|juice of 1 lemon|2 cups water
instructions: Mix ground lamb, rice, onion, parsley, dill, mint, allspice, cinnamon, salt and pepper.|Lay a grape leaf flat, shiny side down, place a tablespoon of filling near the stem end.|Fold in sides and roll up tightly into a small cylinder.|Pack dolma snugly seam-side down in a heavy pot lined with a few extra grape leaves.|Mix tomato paste, lemon juice and water, pour over the dolma.|Place a plate on top to keep them from unrolling.|Cover and simmer on low heat for 1 hour until rice is cooked and leaves are tender.

title: Harissa Armenia
summary: Armenian porridge of slow-cooked wheat and chicken
description: Harissa is a beloved Armenian comfort dish — hulled wheat cooked for hours with chicken until it breaks down into a thick, creamy porridge.
ingredients: 2 cups hulled wheat (dzedzadz), soaked overnight|1 whole chicken (about 1.5 kg)|8 cups water|1 tsp salt|1/2 cup butter|ground cumin for serving
instructions: Place soaked wheat and whole chicken in a large heavy pot with water and salt.|Bring to a boil, reduce heat to very low, cover.|Cook for 3-4 hours, stirring occasionally, until wheat is completely broken down.|Remove chicken, shred all meat off the bones, return meat to the pot.|Beat vigorously with a wooden spoon until the mixture becomes a thick, smooth porridge.|Serve in bowls with a generous pat of butter melting on top and a sprinkle of cumin.

title: Ghapama
summary: Armenian stuffed pumpkin with rice, dried fruits and nuts
description: Ghapama is a festive Armenian dish — a whole pumpkin hollowed out and filled with rice, dried fruits, nuts and honey, then baked until tender.
ingredients: 1 medium pumpkin (about 2 kg)|2 cups cooked rice|1/2 cup dried apricots, chopped|1/2 cup raisins|1/2 cup prunes, chopped|1/2 cup walnuts, chopped|1/2 cup almonds, chopped|1/4 cup honey|1 tsp cinnamon|1/4 tsp ground cloves|2 tbsp butter
instructions: Cut the top off the pumpkin to make a lid, scoop out seeds and fibers.|Mix cooked rice with apricots, raisins, prunes, walnuts, almonds, honey, cinnamon, cloves and butter.|Stuff the pumpkin with the rice mixture, replace the lid.|Place on a baking sheet, bake at 180°C (350°F) for 1.5-2 hours until the pumpkin flesh is very tender.|Bring the whole pumpkin to the table.|Cut into wedges, scooping stuffing and pumpkin flesh together onto plates.

title: Manti Armenia
summary: Tiny Armenian boat-shaped dumplings baked in tomato broth
description: Armenian Manti are small open-topped dumplings filled with spiced ground meat, baked until crisp then simmered in tomato broth and served with garlic yogurt.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup water|1 egg|1/2 tsp salt|300g ground beef or lamb|1 onion, very finely diced|1/4 tsp allspice|salt and pepper|2 cups tomato broth (tomato paste + water)|1 cup yogurt|2 cloves garlic, minced|dried mint|2 tbsp butter
instructions: Make dough from flour, water, egg and salt, knead until smooth, rest 30 minutes.|Mix meat with onion, allspice, salt and pepper for the filling.|Roll dough thin, cut into 1.5-inch squares.|Place a small ball of filling in each square, pinch opposite corners together leaving top open like a boat.|Place on a greased baking sheet, bake at 200°C (400°F) for 15 minutes until dough is golden.|Pour hot tomato broth over the baked manti in the pan, bake 10 more minutes.|Mix yogurt with garlic and salt.|Serve manti in bowls with broth, top with garlic yogurt, drizzle melted butter mixed with dried mint.

title: Spas
summary: Armenian yogurt and wheat berry soup
description: Spas is a tangy, creamy Armenian soup made with yogurt, hulled wheat and fresh herbs — served hot in winter or cold in summer.
ingredients: 4 cups plain yogurt|1/2 cup hulled wheat berries, soaked and pre-cooked|1 egg|2 tbsp all-purpose flour|4 cups water|1 large onion, diced|2 tbsp butter|3 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|1 tbsp fresh mint, chopped|salt to taste
instructions: Whisk yogurt, egg, flour and water together until smooth in a large pot.|Heat gently over medium-low heat, stirring constantly in one direction to prevent curdling.|Once it begins to simmer, add the cooked wheat berries.|In a small pan, sauté diced onion in butter until golden.|Add the sautéed onion, cilantro and mint to the soup.|Simmer gently for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, season with salt.|Serve hot, or cool and refrigerate to serve cold in summer.

title: Gata
summary: Armenian sweet bread pastry with a buttery filling
description: Gata is a beloved Armenian pastry — soft sweet bread encasing a rich filling of butter, sugar and flour called khoriz.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup warm milk|1 packet dry yeast|1/4 cup sugar|1/4 cup melted butter|1 egg|1/2 tsp salt|For filling: 1/2 cup butter softened|1/2 cup sugar|1 cup flour|1/2 tsp vanilla|1 egg yolk for glaze
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm milk with a pinch of sugar, let foam 10 minutes.|Mix flour, sugar and salt, add yeast mixture, melted butter and egg, knead into a soft dough.|Cover and let rise 1 hour until doubled.|Make filling (khoriz): mix softened butter, sugar, flour and vanilla into a crumbly paste.|Roll dough into a rectangle, spread filling evenly over it.|Roll up like a jelly roll, coil into a round, or cut into individual portions.|Place on a baking sheet, brush with egg yolk.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 30-35 minutes until deep golden brown.

title: Eech
summary: Armenian bulgur salad with tomatoes, peppers and herbs
description: Eech is a vibrant Armenian bulgur salad mixed with tomato paste, fresh vegetables, lemon juice and herbs — similar to kisir or tabbouleh.
ingredients: 1 cup fine bulgur wheat|1 cup boiling water|3 tbsp tomato paste|1/4 cup olive oil|2 tomatoes, finely diced|1 green bell pepper, finely diced|4 green onions, sliced|1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped|2 tbsp lemon juice|1 tsp red pepper paste|salt and pepper
instructions: Pour boiling water over bulgur, cover and let stand 20 minutes until water is absorbed.|Fluff with a fork, add tomato paste and olive oil, mix well.|Add diced tomatoes, bell pepper, green onions and parsley.|Season with lemon juice, red pepper paste, salt and pepper.|Toss everything together, let sit 15 minutes for flavors to meld.|Serve at room temperature as a side dish or wrapped in lettuce leaves.

title: Lahmajoun Armenia
summary: Armenian thin flatbread topped with spiced ground meat
description: Lahmajoun is a paper-thin crispy flatbread spread with a layer of seasoned ground lamb, tomatoes, peppers and herbs, baked at high heat.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup warm water|1 tbsp olive oil|1/2 tsp salt|300g ground lamb|1 onion, very finely diced|2 tomatoes, finely diced|1 green pepper, finely diced|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 tsp paprika|1/2 tsp cumin|1/4 tsp cayenne|fresh parsley|lemon wedges
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, water, oil and salt, knead until smooth, rest 30 minutes.|Mix ground lamb with onion, tomatoes, pepper, tomato paste, paprika, cumin, cayenne, parsley and salt into a spreadable paste.|Divide dough into 8 balls, roll each very thin (almost translucent).|Spread a thin layer of meat mixture over each round, edge to edge.|Bake on a hot baking stone at 250°C (480°F) for 5-7 minutes until edges are crisp.|Squeeze lemon juice over top, roll up and eat.

title: Borani Armenia
summary: Armenian spinach and yogurt side dish
description: Borani is a simple Armenian appetizer of sautéed spinach mixed with thick yogurt and garlic, served chilled with flatbread.
ingredients: 500g fresh spinach, washed|1 cup thick yogurt (strained)|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 small onion, diced|2 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|pinch of cayenne
instructions: Wilt spinach in a pot with just the water clinging to the leaves, drain well and chop.|Sauté onion in butter until golden.|Mix chopped spinach, sautéed onion, yogurt and garlic.|Season with salt, pepper and cayenne.|Chill for at least 1 hour before serving.|Serve cold with lavash bread.

title: Pasuts Tolma
summary: Armenian Lenten grape leaf rolls with beans, lentils and bulgur
description: Pasuts Tolma is the vegan version of Armenian dolma, filled with a mixture of chickpeas, lentils, bulgur and spices, traditionally made during Lent.
ingredients: 30 grape leaves|1/2 cup red lentils|1/2 cup chickpeas, cooked|1/2 cup fine bulgur|1 onion, finely diced|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 tsp cumin|1/2 tsp allspice|3 tbsp olive oil|salt and pepper|juice of 1 lemon|2 cups water
instructions: Cook lentils until just tender (about 10 min), drain and cool.|Mix lentils, chickpeas, bulgur, onion, tomato paste, cumin, allspice, olive oil, salt and pepper.|Place a spoonful of filling on each grape leaf, fold and roll tightly.|Pack rolls seam-side down in a pot.|Mix lemon juice with water, pour over rolls.|Place a plate on top, cover and simmer 45 minutes.|Serve at room temperature or cold, drizzled with olive oil.

title: Khash
summary: Armenian slow-simmered cow foot soup eaten at dawn
description: Khash is an ancient Armenian tradition — cow feet simmered overnight until the broth is rich and gelatinous, eaten at early morning gatherings with garlic and lavash.
ingredients: 2 kg cow feet, cleaned and split|1 head garlic, peeled|10 cups cold water|salt|dried lavash bread|white vinegar
instructions: Place cow feet in a large pot, cover with cold water.|Bring to a boil, skim all foam and fat from the surface.|Reduce heat to the lowest setting, simmer uncovered for 8-10 hours (overnight) until broth is thick and milky.|The meat should be falling off the bones.|Serve in deep bowls with the hot broth.|Guests crush raw garlic into their bowl, tear dried lavash into it, and season with salt and vinegar to taste.

title: Lavash
summary: Armenian paper-thin soft flatbread baked in a tonir
description: Lavash is Armenia's iconic UNESCO-heritage flatbread — paper-thin, soft and pliable, traditionally slapped against the hot walls of a clay tonir oven.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup warm water|1 tsp salt|1 tsp sugar|1 tbsp vegetable oil
instructions: Mix flour, salt and sugar, add water and oil, knead into a smooth elastic dough.|Cover and rest for 30 minutes.|Divide into 8 balls, roll each ball out as thin as possible on a floured surface.|Heat a large skillet or inverted wok over medium-high heat.|Drape the thin dough over the hot surface, cook 30-40 seconds until bubbles form.|Flip and cook another 20 seconds.|Stack cooked lavash under a damp towel to keep soft.

title: Anoushabour
summary: Armenian Christmas wheat berry pudding with dried fruits and nuts
description: Anoushabour is a festive Armenian dessert served at Christmas — creamy wheat berry pudding topped with walnuts, pomegranate, dried fruits and cinnamon.
ingredients: 1 cup hulled wheat berries, soaked overnight|6 cups water|1/2 cup sugar|1/4 cup raisins|1/4 cup dried apricots, chopped|1/4 cup walnuts, chopped|pomegranate seeds|1 tsp cinnamon|1 tsp rosewater
instructions: Simmer soaked wheat berries in water for 1.5-2 hours until very tender and starting to burst.|Add sugar, raisins and dried apricots, cook 15 more minutes.|Stir in rosewater, remove from heat.|Pour into serving bowls, let cool to room temperature.|Top generously with walnuts, pomegranate seeds and a dusting of cinnamon.|Serve at room temperature or chilled.

title: Zhingyalov Hats
summary: Armenian flatbread stuffed with 20+ wild herbs and greens
description: Zhingyalov Hats is a specialty of Artsakh — thin flatbread stuffed with a mixture of over 20 different wild mountain herbs and greens, dry-cooked on a griddle.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup warm water|1/2 tsp salt|1 tbsp oil|4 cups mixed fresh greens and herbs (spinach, sorrel, parsley, cilantro, dill, chives, purslane, tarragon, mint, nettles)|2 green onions, chopped|1 small hot pepper, minced|salt and pepper|2 tbsp vegetable oil for cooking
instructions: Make dough with flour, water, salt and oil, knead smooth, rest 20 minutes.|Wash and finely chop all the greens and herbs, mix with green onions, hot pepper, salt and pepper.|Divide dough into 8 balls, roll each into a thin circle.|Place a generous handful of the herb mixture on one half.|Fold dough over and press edges to seal like a half-moon.|Cook on a dry hot griddle or skillet, 2-3 minutes per side until golden spots appear.|Serve warm, the bread should be soft with the herbs steaming inside.

title: Tzhvzhik
summary: Armenian pan-fried organ meat with onions and tomatoes
description: Tzhvzhik is a quick Armenian dish of sautéed liver, heart and lungs with plenty of onion, tomatoes and herbs — rich and satisfying.
ingredients: 300g beef or lamb liver, cubed|200g beef heart, cubed|2 large onions, sliced|2 tomatoes, diced|3 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp paprika|1/2 tsp black pepper|salt|fresh cilantro and basil
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat.|Add cubed heart first (it takes longer), cook 5 minutes.|Add liver cubes, cook 3 minutes until browned.|Add sliced onions, cook until softened, about 5 minutes.|Add tomatoes and paprika, cook 3 more minutes.|Season with salt, pepper, garnish with cilantro and basil.|Serve hot with lavash bread.

title: Khinkali Armenia
summary: Armenian soup dumplings filled with spiced meat
description: Armenian Khinkali are large dumplings with a twisted top knob, filled with juicy seasoned meat that creates broth inside as they cook.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup water|1 tsp salt|500g ground beef and pork mix|1 large onion, finely diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1/2 cup cold water|1 tsp black pepper|1/2 tsp cumin|1/2 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|salt
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, water and salt, knead until smooth, rest 30 minutes covered.|Mix ground meat with onion, garlic, cold water, pepper, cumin, cilantro and salt into a juicy filling.|Roll dough thin, cut into 4-inch circles.|Place 2 tablespoons filling in the center of each circle.|Gather edges and pleat them together at the top, twisting to seal into a knob.|Boil in salted water for 12-15 minutes until they float and dough is cooked.|Serve immediately — eat by holding the knob, biting the bottom and sipping the broth inside.

title: Nazook
summary: Armenian flaky rolled pastry with a sweet walnut filling
description: Nazook is a beautiful Armenian pastry — layers of tender yeast dough rolled with a sweet buttery filling, sliced to show a swirl pattern.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup butter, cold and cubed|1/2 cup sour cream|1 egg|1/4 tsp salt|For filling: 1/2 cup sugar|1/2 cup flour|1/4 cup butter, softened|1/2 cup walnuts, finely chopped|1 tsp vanilla|1 egg yolk for glaze
instructions: Pulse flour and cold butter in a food processor until crumbly.|Add sour cream, egg and salt, mix until dough forms.|Wrap and chill for 1 hour.|Make filling: mix sugar, flour, softened butter, walnuts and vanilla.|Roll chilled dough into a rectangle, spread filling evenly.|Roll up tightly into a log, cut into 2-inch pieces, place cut-side up on a baking sheet.|Brush tops with egg yolk.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25-30 minutes until golden.

title: Matsun
summary: Armenian homemade yogurt thick and tangy
description: Matsun is traditional Armenian yogurt — thick, tangy and creamy, made by fermenting warm milk with a live culture starter, a staple of Armenian kitchens.
ingredients: 1 liter whole milk|2 tbsp matsun or plain yogurt with live cultures
instructions: Heat milk in a saucepan to 82°C (180°F), then remove from heat.|Cool the milk to 43°C (110°F) — warm to the touch but not hot.|Stir in the yogurt starter until well mixed.|Pour into a clean glass jar or ceramic bowl.|Wrap in a thick towel or blanket and keep in a warm place undisturbed for 8-12 hours.|The matsun is ready when it has thickened and has a tangy flavor.|Refrigerate and use within a week — reserve 2 tbsp as starter for next batch.

title: Tolma with Quince
summary: Armenian grape leaf dolma served with quince for autumn celebrations
description: This autumn variation of Armenian tolma pairs the classic stuffed grape leaves with slices of tart quince tucked between the rolls, adding a sweet-sour dimension.
ingredients: 30 grape leaves|500g ground lamb|1 cup short-grain rice|1 onion, finely diced|3 tbsp fresh herbs (parsley, dill, mint), chopped|1 tsp allspice|salt and pepper|2 quinces, peeled, cored and sliced|2 tbsp tomato paste|juice of 1 lemon|2 cups water|1 tbsp sugar
instructions: Mix lamb, rice, onion, herbs, allspice, salt and pepper.|Roll filling in grape leaves as for standard tolma.|Arrange a layer of quince slices in the bottom of a heavy pot.|Pack tolma rolls on top of the quince, layering more quince between them.|Mix tomato paste, lemon juice, sugar and water, pour over everything.|Press a plate on top, cover and simmer on low heat for 1 hour.|Serve the tolma with the softened quince slices alongside.

title: Plov Azerbaijan
summary: Azerbaijani saffron rice with dried fruits, chestnuts and lamb
description: Azerbaijani Plov is the national dish — fragrant saffron-infused rice topped with a gazmag crust, served with dried fruit, chestnut and herb-wrapped lamb.
ingredients: 3 cups basmati rice, soaked 2 hours|500g lamb shoulder, cubed|1/2 tsp saffron threads, dissolved in 3 tbsp hot water|1/2 cup dried apricots|1/2 cup dried plums|1/2 cup chestnuts, peeled|1/2 cup butter|1 large onion, diced|1 tsp turmeric|salt|1 sheet lavash for gazmag crust
instructions: Par-boil soaked rice in salted water until half done (about 5 min), drain.|Sauté lamb with onion until browned, season with salt and turmeric, cook through.|Line the bottom of a heavy pot with lavash bread brushed with butter (this becomes the crispy gazmag).|Layer half the rice over the lavash, then the lamb, dried fruits and chestnuts, then remaining rice.|Pour saffron water and melted butter over the top.|Cover tightly with a towel under the lid, cook on very low heat for 45 minutes.|Serve rice on a platter with the golden gazmag crust on top, fruits and lamb arranged alongside.

title: Dushbara
summary: Tiny Azerbaijani dumplings in a clear lamb broth
description: Dushbara are miniature dumplings — so small that a spoonful holds several — filled with spiced lamb and simmered in a clear aromatic broth.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 egg|1/4 cup water|1/2 tsp salt|250g ground lamb|1 small onion, very finely minced|1/2 tsp black pepper|1/4 tsp turmeric|6 cups lamb or beef broth|2 tbsp white vinegar|dried mint|fresh cilantro
instructions: Make dough: flour, egg, water, salt — knead smooth, rest 20 minutes.|Mix lamb, onion, pepper, turmeric and salt for filling.|Roll dough paper-thin, cut into 1-inch squares.|Place a tiny pinch of filling on each square, fold into triangles and press edges.|Bring broth to a simmer, drop in dumplings, cook 6-8 minutes until they float.|Add vinegar to the broth.|Serve in bowls with broth, sprinkle dried mint and cilantro.

title: Lavangi
summary: Azerbaijani whole fish or chicken stuffed with walnut-onion filling
description: Lavangi is a specialty of southern Azerbaijan — whole fish or chicken stuffed with a rich paste of ground walnuts, onions and aromatic spices, then baked.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken (1.5 kg) or large fish|2 cups walnuts, ground|3 large onions, finely diced|1 tbsp pomegranate molasses|1 tsp turmeric|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp cinnamon|salt and pepper|2 tbsp butter|juice of 1 lemon
instructions: Sauté onions until deeply golden and caramelized, about 20 minutes.|Mix caramelized onions with ground walnuts, pomegranate molasses, turmeric, coriander, cinnamon, salt and pepper.|Clean the chicken or fish, pat dry, season inside and out with salt and lemon juice.|Stuff the cavity with the walnut-onion mixture.|Sew or skewer the opening closed.|Place in a greased baking dish, dot with butter.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 1.5 hours (chicken) or 40 minutes (fish) until cooked through and skin is golden.

title: Dolma Azerbaijan
summary: Azerbaijani grape leaf dolma with lamb and split peas
description: Azerbaijani dolma features grape leaves wrapped around a filling of ground lamb enriched with yellow split peas and aromatic herbs.
ingredients: 40 grape leaves|500g ground lamb|1/2 cup yellow split peas, soaked 1 hour|1 cup onion, finely diced|3 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|3 tbsp fresh dill, chopped|2 tbsp fresh mint, chopped|1 tsp allspice|salt and pepper|juice of 2 lemons|1 cup water
instructions: Boil split peas for 15 minutes until just tender, drain.|Mix lamb, split peas, onion, cilantro, dill, mint, allspice, salt and pepper.|Blanch grape leaves in boiling water 1 minute, drain.|Place filling on each leaf, fold sides and roll tightly.|Pack snugly in a pot, seam-side down.|Pour lemon juice and water over, place a plate on top.|Simmer on low 50 minutes until cooked through.

title: Buglama
summary: Azerbaijani braised lamb with tomatoes, peppers and onions
description: Buglama is a simple Azerbaijani braise where lamb chops cook in their own juices with layers of onions, tomatoes and peppers — no added water needed.
ingredients: 1 kg lamb chops or shoulder, bone-in|3 large onions, sliced into rings|3 tomatoes, sliced|2 bell peppers, sliced|4 cloves garlic, sliced|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|1/4 cup fresh dill, chopped|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp salt|2 tbsp butter
instructions: Layer sliced onions on the bottom of a heavy pot.|Place lamb pieces over the onions.|Layer tomatoes, peppers and garlic over the lamb.|Scatter herbs, salt and pepper on top, dot with butter.|Cover tightly (seal lid with dough if needed) — do not add any water.|Cook on very low heat for 2-2.5 hours until lamb is falling off the bone.|Serve directly from the pot with bread.

title: Qutab
summary: Azerbaijani thin stuffed flatbread with herbs or meat
description: Qutab are paper-thin half-moon flatbreads filled with either fresh herbs and cheese or spiced ground lamb, cooked on a dry griddle.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup water|1/2 tsp salt|For herb filling: 2 cups mixed herbs (cilantro, dill, spinach, sorrel), chopped|100g feta cheese, crumbled|1 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|sumac for serving
instructions: Make dough: flour, water, salt — knead smooth, rest 20 minutes.|Mix chopped herbs with crumbled cheese and season.|Divide dough into 8 balls, roll each very thin into a circle.|Place herb filling on one half, fold into a half-moon, press edges.|Cook on a dry hot griddle 2 minutes per side until golden spots appear.|Brush with butter while hot.|Cut into strips, sprinkle with sumac and serve.

title: Piti
summary: Azerbaijani lamb and chickpea stew cooked in individual clay pots
description: Piti is an Azerbaijani stew traditionally cooked in small clay pots — lamb, chickpeas, chestnuts and saffron slowly meld into a deeply flavored meal.
ingredients: 400g lamb shoulder, cubed|1 cup chickpeas, soaked overnight|8 chestnuts, peeled|1 large onion, quartered|1 large potato, cubed|2 tomatoes, halved|1/4 tsp saffron|1 tsp turmeric|salt and pepper|4 cups water|dried plums (optional)
instructions: Divide all ingredients among 4 individual clay pots or use one large pot.|Add water to each pot, cover tightly with lids or foil.|Place in an oven at 160°C (325°F) and cook for 3-4 hours.|The broth becomes rich, the lamb tender, the chickpeas creamy.|Traditionally, first pour off the broth and drink it as soup with bread.|Then mash the remaining solids together and eat as a second course.

title: Kyukyu
description: Kyukyu is an Azerbaijani egg dish dense with fresh herbs — a thick frittata of eggs, cilantro, dill and spring onions, baked until set.
ingredients: 8 eggs|1 cup fresh cilantro, finely chopped|1 cup fresh dill, finely chopped|1/2 cup spring onions, sliced|2 tbsp butter|1/2 tsp turmeric|salt and pepper
instructions: Beat eggs with salt, pepper and turmeric.|Stir in all chopped herbs and spring onions.|Melt butter in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat.|Pour in the egg mixture, cook on stovetop for 3 minutes until bottom sets.|Transfer to an oven at 180°C (350°F), bake 15 minutes until fully set and golden on top.|Flip onto a plate, cut into wedges and serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Ovdukh
summary: Cold Azerbaijani yogurt and herb soup for summer
description: Ovdukh is a refreshing chilled Azerbaijani soup of yogurt, herbs, cucumber and hard-boiled eggs — the perfect summer meal.
ingredients: 2 cups plain yogurt|2 cups cold water|1 cucumber, finely diced|3 hard-boiled eggs, chopped|1/2 cup fresh dill, chopped|1/2 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|4 spring onions, sliced|salt and pepper|ice cubes
instructions: Whisk yogurt and cold water until smooth.|Add diced cucumber, chopped eggs, dill, cilantro and spring onions.|Season with salt and pepper.|Chill for at least 1 hour.|Serve in bowls with ice cubes floating on top.

title: Shekerbura
summary: Azerbaijani crescent-shaped pastries filled with sweet almonds and cardamom
description: Shekerbura are beautifully decorated crescent pastries filled with ground almonds, sugar and cardamom — a festive treat for Novruz celebrations.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup butter, melted|1/2 cup warm milk|1 egg|1 tbsp sugar|1/4 tsp salt|1 tsp dry yeast|For filling: 1 cup almonds, finely ground|1/2 cup sugar|1/2 tsp ground cardamom|1 egg white for sealing
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm milk with sugar, let foam.|Mix flour and salt, add yeast mixture, egg and melted butter, knead smooth dough, rest 30 minutes.|Mix ground almonds, sugar and cardamom for filling.|Roll dough thin, cut into 3-inch circles.|Place a spoon of filling in the center, fold into a crescent and pinch edges with a decorative pattern.|Place on a lined baking sheet.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 18-20 minutes until lightly golden (they should stay pale).

title: Govurma
summary: Azerbaijani slow-braised lamb with onions and herbs
description: Govurma is a rich Azerbaijani lamb braise — chunks of lamb slowly cooked with onions and spices until incredibly tender and deeply flavored.
ingredients: 800g lamb shoulder, cubed|3 large onions, sliced|3 tbsp butter or tail fat|1 tsp turmeric|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1/2 tsp black pepper|salt|1/2 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|1/2 cup water
instructions: Melt butter in a heavy pot, add lamb and brown on all sides.|Add sliced onions, cook until they soften and start to caramelize.|Add turmeric, cinnamon, pepper and salt.|Add water, cover and simmer on very low heat for 1.5 hours.|Check occasionally, the sauce should reduce to a thick glaze coating the lamb.|Stir in cilantro at the end, serve with rice or bread.

title: Pomidor Dolmasi
summary: Azerbaijani tomatoes stuffed with spiced lamb and rice
description: Pomidor Dolmasi are ripe tomatoes hollowed out and filled with a seasoned lamb and rice mixture, baked until the tomatoes are soft and jammy.
ingredients: 8 large ripe tomatoes|300g ground lamb|1/2 cup rice, rinsed|1 onion, finely diced|2 tbsp fresh basil, chopped|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|1 tsp allspice|salt and pepper|2 tbsp butter|1/2 cup water
instructions: Cut tops off tomatoes, scoop out pulp (reserve it).|Mix lamb, rice, onion, basil, cilantro, allspice, salt and pepper.|Stuff each tomato with the filling, replace the tops as lids.|Place stuffed tomatoes in a baking dish.|Chop reserved tomato pulp, pour around the tomatoes with water.|Dot with butter, cover with foil.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 50 minutes, remove foil for last 10 minutes.

title: Saj Qovurma
summary: Azerbaijani lamb and vegetables cooked on a convex iron saj
description: Saj Qovurma is lamb and seasonal vegetables quickly stir-fried on a domed saj griddle over open flame, giving smoky charred flavor.
ingredients: 500g lamb tenderloin, sliced thin|2 bell peppers, sliced|2 tomatoes, wedged|2 onions, sliced|2 eggplants, sliced|3 tbsp butter or tail fat|1 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|fresh herbs (cilantro, basil, tarragon)
instructions: Heat the saj or a large flat griddle over high heat with butter.|Add lamb slices, sear quickly until browned, push to the edges.|Add onions and eggplant to the center, cook 5 minutes.|Add peppers and tomatoes, toss everything together.|Season with salt and pepper, cook 3-5 more minutes until vegetables are charred but still have bite.|Scatter fresh herbs over the top and serve directly from the saj with bread.

title: Pakhlava Azerbaijan
summary: Azerbaijani diamond-shaped baklava with cardamom and saffron
description: Azerbaijani Pakhlava is a rich layered pastry of thin dough, ground almonds and walnuts spiced with cardamom, soaked in saffron-honey syrup.
ingredients: 500g filo pastry|2 cups walnuts, ground|1 cup almonds, ground|1 cup sugar|1 tsp ground cardamom|1/2 tsp saffron, dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water|1 cup butter, melted|For syrup: 1 cup sugar|1/2 cup water|1/4 cup honey|1 tbsp rosewater|1 egg yolk for glaze
instructions: Mix ground nuts, sugar and cardamom for filling.|Layer 5-6 filo sheets in a buttered pan, brushing each with melted butter.|Spread a layer of nut filling.|Add 2 filo sheets, butter, more filling. Repeat until filling is used.|Top with 5-6 filo sheets, brushing each with butter.|Cut into diamond shapes, brush top with egg yolk mixed with saffron water.|Bake at 170°C (340°F) for 40-45 minutes until golden.|Make syrup: boil sugar and water, add honey and rosewater, simmer 5 minutes.|Pour warm syrup over hot pakhlava, let soak for several hours.

title: Chigirtma
summary: Azerbaijani chicken braised with eggs, onions and saffron
description: Chigirtma is a comforting Azerbaijani dish of chicken pieces braised with caramelized onions and saffron, finished with beaten eggs poured over the top.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces|3 large onions, sliced|3 tbsp butter|1/4 tsp saffron, dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water|4 eggs, beaten|juice of 1 lemon|salt and pepper|fresh cilantro
instructions: Season chicken with salt and pepper, brown in butter on all sides, remove.|In the same pot, cook sliced onions until deeply caramelized, about 20 minutes.|Return chicken, add saffron water and lemon juice.|Cover and cook on low heat for 30 minutes until chicken is done.|Pour beaten eggs over the chicken, cover and cook 3-4 minutes until eggs are just set.|Garnish with cilantro and serve from the pot.

title: Syudlu Siyig
description: Syudlu Siyig is a creamy Azerbaijani rice pudding cooked in milk with butter and sugar, simple and comforting.
ingredients: 1 cup short-grain rice|4 cups whole milk|1/3 cup sugar|2 tbsp butter|1/4 tsp salt|1 tsp vanilla|cinnamon for garnish
instructions: Rinse rice well.|Bring milk to a simmer in a heavy saucepan.|Add rice, sugar, butter and salt.|Cook on very low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30-35 minutes until rice is very soft and mixture is creamy.|Stir in vanilla, pour into serving bowls.|Sprinkle with cinnamon, serve warm or chilled.

title: Toyuq Shorbasi
summary: Azerbaijani chicken soup with vermicelli and saffron
description: Toyuq Shorbasi is a golden saffron-scented chicken soup with thin vermicelli noodles — Azerbaijan's classic comfort soup.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken|8 cups water|1 large onion, quartered|2 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|1/2 cup vermicelli noodles|1/4 tsp saffron, dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water|salt and pepper|fresh dill and cilantro
instructions: Place chicken in a pot with water, bring to boil, skim foam.|Add onion and carrots, simmer 45 minutes until chicken is cooked.|Remove chicken, shred the meat.|Strain broth, return to pot, add potatoes and cook 15 minutes.|Add vermicelli and saffron water, cook 5 more minutes.|Return shredded chicken to the soup, season with salt and pepper.|Serve garnished with fresh dill and cilantro.

title: Pomegranate Sharbat
description: Pomegranate Sharbat is a traditional Azerbaijani cold drink made from fresh pomegranate juice, sugar and rosewater — ruby-red and refreshing.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh pomegranate juice (from about 8 pomegranates)|1/4 cup sugar|1 tbsp rosewater|2 cups cold water|ice cubes|fresh mint leaves
instructions: Stir sugar into pomegranate juice until dissolved (or heat briefly if needed).|Add rosewater and cold water, stir.|Chill thoroughly in the refrigerator.|Serve over ice cubes, garnished with mint leaves.

title: Qara Meyvə Kompotu
summary: Azerbaijani dark fruit compote with plums and cherries
description: A traditional Azerbaijani compote made by poaching dark fruits — plums, cherries and cornelian cherries — in a light sugar syrup, served cold.
ingredients: 2 cups dark plums, halved and pitted|1 cup cherries, pitted|1 cup cornelian cherries or sour cherries|3/4 cup sugar|6 cups water|1 cinnamon stick|2 whole cloves
instructions: Bring water and sugar to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves.|Add cinnamon stick and cloves.|Add plums and cook 5 minutes.|Add cherries and cook another 5 minutes until fruit is tender but not mushy.|Remove from heat, cool to room temperature.|Chill in the refrigerator, serve cold in glasses with the fruit.

title: Kuku Sabzi
summary: Azerbaijani herb frittata with walnuts and barberries
description: Kuku Sabzi is a dense herb frittata packed with fresh herbs, walnuts and tart barberries, sliced into wedges — popular for Novruz celebrations.
ingredients: 8 eggs|2 cups fresh parsley, finely chopped|1 cup fresh cilantro, finely chopped|1 cup fresh dill, finely chopped|1/2 cup walnuts, chopped|2 tbsp dried barberries|4 spring onions, sliced|1/2 tsp turmeric|1/2 tsp baking powder|salt and pepper|3 tbsp butter
instructions: Beat eggs with turmeric, baking powder, salt and pepper.|Stir in all chopped herbs, spring onions, walnuts and barberries.|Melt butter in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet.|Pour in the mixture, cook on stovetop over medium-low heat for 5 minutes until bottom sets.|Transfer to oven at 180°C (350°F), bake 20 minutes until firm and golden.|Let cool slightly, flip onto a plate, cut into wedges.|Serve warm or at room temperature with yogurt and flatbread.

title: Machboos
summary: Bahraini spiced rice with chicken or lamb, the national dish
description: Machboos is Bahrain's beloved rice dish — meat or chicken simmered with onions, tomatoes and a fragrant baharat spice blend, then cooked with the rice.
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice, soaked 30 min|500g chicken pieces or lamb|2 large onions, diced|3 tomatoes, diced|2 dried limes (loomi), pierced|2 tbsp baharat spice mix|1 tsp turmeric|1 tbsp tomato paste|3 tbsp vegetable oil|3 cups chicken broth|salt|fresh cilantro|fried onions for garnish
instructions: Heat oil, fry onions until golden, add chicken or lamb and brown.|Add baharat, turmeric, and tomato paste, stir 1 minute.|Add tomatoes and dried limes, cook 5 minutes.|Add broth, bring to boil, reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes.|Remove meat, add drained rice to the broth.|Cook rice until liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes.|Return meat on top, cover tightly and steam on low 10 minutes.|Serve garnished with fried onions and cilantro.

title: Harees Bahrain
summary: Bahraini slow-cooked wheat and chicken porridge
description: Harees is a comforting Bahraini dish of crushed wheat and chicken cooked for hours until they meld into a silky, savory porridge topped with ghee.
ingredients: 1 cup jareesh (crushed wheat)|500g chicken breast|1 large onion, quartered|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp cinnamon|6 cups water|4 tbsp ghee|ground cinnamon for garnish
instructions: Soak crushed wheat for 2 hours, drain.|Boil chicken with onion and salt until cooked, shred the meat.|Combine wheat, shredded chicken and cooking liquid in a pot.|Simmer on very low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.|Beat with a wooden spoon until smooth and porridge-like.|Serve in bowls with a generous drizzle of hot ghee and cinnamon on top.

title: Balaleet
summary: Bahraini sweet saffron vermicelli topped with a savory egg omelet
description: Balaleet is a unique Bahraini breakfast dish — sweet vermicelli noodles flavored with saffron, cardamom and rosewater, topped with a thin savory omelet.
ingredients: 200g vermicelli noodles|3 tbsp ghee|1/4 cup sugar|1/4 tsp saffron threads in 2 tbsp hot water|1/2 tsp ground cardamom|1 tbsp rosewater|4 eggs|salt and pepper|1 tbsp butter
instructions: Lightly toast vermicelli in ghee until golden.|Add enough water to cover, cook until tender, drain any excess.|Stir in sugar, saffron water, cardamom and rosewater.|Beat eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper.|Make a thin omelet in butter, cook until just set.|Mound the sweet vermicelli on a plate, lay the omelet on top.|Serve warm for breakfast.

title: Muhammar
description: Muhammar is a traditional Bahraini rice dish sweetened with date syrup and perfumed with cardamom and rosewater, often served with grilled fish.
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice, soaked 30 min|3 tbsp date syrup (dibs)|3 tbsp ghee|1/2 tsp ground cardamom|1 tbsp rosewater|1/4 tsp saffron in 1 tbsp hot water|pinch of salt|3 cups water
instructions: Par-boil rice in salted water until half cooked, drain.|Heat ghee in a pot, add date syrup and stir.|Add par-boiled rice, saffron water, cardamom and rosewater.|Stir gently, cover tightly and cook on very low heat for 20 minutes.|Fluff with a fork — the rice should be golden and sweet.|Serve alongside grilled fish or seafood.

title: Samboosa Bahrain
summary: Bahraini spiced meat or cheese pastry triangles
description: Samboosa are crispy fried pastry triangles filled with spiced ground meat or cream cheese, a staple appetizer during Ramadan in Bahrain.
ingredients: 1 pack spring roll wrappers|300g ground beef|1 onion, finely diced|1 tsp baharat spice|1/2 tsp ground cumin|2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|salt and pepper|oil for deep frying|flour-water paste for sealing
instructions: Cook ground beef with onion until browned, add spices and parsley, season.|Cut spring roll wrappers into strips about 3 inches wide.|Place a spoon of filling at one end, fold into a triangle shape repeatedly.|Seal the edge with flour-water paste.|Deep fry at 175°C (350°F) until golden and crispy, about 3 minutes.|Drain on paper towels, serve hot.

title: Ghouzi
summary: Bahraini whole roasted lamb on a bed of spiced rice
description: Ghouzi is a celebratory Bahraini dish of a whole lamb slow-roasted until falling apart, served on a mountain of fragrant spiced rice with nuts and raisins.
ingredients: 2 kg lamb leg|3 cups basmati rice|2 onions, sliced|2 tbsp baharat spice|1 tsp turmeric|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1/4 cup raisins|1/4 cup almonds, toasted|1/4 cup pine nuts, toasted|3 tbsp ghee|salt|4 cups broth
instructions: Rub lamb with baharat, salt and turmeric, sear in ghee until browned.|Place in a roasting pan, add 1 cup broth, cover with foil.|Roast at 160°C (325°F) for 3 hours until meat falls off the bone.|Cook rice in remaining broth with cinnamon and onions.|Toast almonds, pine nuts and raisins in ghee.|Mound rice on a large platter, place the lamb on top.|Scatter toasted nuts and raisins over everything.

title: Jireesh
summary: Bahraini cracked wheat porridge with chicken
description: Jireesh is a traditional Bahraini dish of cracked wheat cooked with chicken into a thick, comforting porridge, seasoned with baharat and topped with caramelized onions.
ingredients: 1 cup jareesh (cracked wheat)|500g chicken thighs|1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 tbsp baharat spice|1/2 tsp turmeric|2 tbsp ghee|5 cups water|salt|fried onions for topping
instructions: Soak cracked wheat 1 hour.|Brown chicken in ghee, add onion and cook until soft.|Add tomatoes, baharat, turmeric and salt, cook 3 minutes.|Add drained wheat and water, bring to boil.|Reduce heat, simmer 1.5 hours stirring occasionally until thick.|Shred chicken into the porridge, stir.|Serve topped with crispy fried onions and extra ghee.

title: Thareed Bahrain
summary: Bahraini bread-based lamb and vegetable stew
description: Thareed is a Bahraini stew of lamb and vegetables served over torn pieces of thin flatbread (regag), which soak up the flavorful broth.
ingredients: 500g lamb, cubed|2 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|1 zucchini, cubed|2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp baharat spice|1 tsp turmeric|2 tbsp tomato paste|4 cups water|4 sheets regag bread (or thin flatbread)|2 tbsp oil|salt
instructions: Brown lamb in oil, add onion and cook until soft.|Add baharat, turmeric and tomato paste, stir 1 minute.|Add all vegetables and water, bring to boil.|Simmer 45 minutes until lamb is tender and sauce is rich.|Tear regag bread into pieces and line a deep serving dish.|Ladle the stew over the bread, let it soak 5 minutes.|Serve family-style directly from the dish.

title: Hammour Fish Curry
summary: Bahraini grouper fish curry with coconut and spices
description: This Bahraini fish curry features local hammour (grouper) simmered in a fragrant sauce of coconut milk, tomatoes, curry leaves and fenugreek.
ingredients: 600g hammour or grouper fillets, cubed|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp ginger, grated|2 tomatoes, diced|1 cup coconut milk|1 tbsp curry powder|1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds|8 curry leaves|1/2 tsp turmeric|2 tbsp oil|salt|fresh cilantro
instructions: Heat oil, toast fenugreek seeds and curry leaves 30 seconds.|Add onion, garlic and ginger, sauté until fragrant.|Add curry powder and turmeric, stir 1 minute.|Add tomatoes, cook until softened.|Pour in coconut milk, bring to a gentle simmer.|Add fish cubes, cook gently 10-12 minutes until fish is flaky.|Season with salt, garnish with cilantro, serve with rice.

title: Shawarma Bahrain
summary: Bahraini-style chicken shawarma wrap with garlic sauce
description: Bahraini shawarma features thinly sliced marinated chicken wrapped in fresh Arabic bread with pickles, fries and a creamy garlic toum sauce.
ingredients: 500g chicken thighs, sliced thin|2 tbsp yogurt|1 tbsp lemon juice|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp paprika|1/2 tsp turmeric|1/2 tsp cinnamon|salt and pepper|4 Arabic flatbreads|For toum: 1/2 cup garlic cloves|1 cup vegetable oil|3 tbsp lemon juice|salt|pickles and french fries for serving
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon juice and all spices for at least 2 hours.|Make toum: blend garlic with salt, slowly drizzle in oil while blending, add lemon juice until creamy.|Cook marinated chicken on a hot griddle or skillet until charred and cooked through, slice thin.|Warm flatbreads on the grill.|Assemble: spread toum on bread, add chicken, pickles and a few fries.|Roll up tightly, cut in half and serve.

title: Khabeesa
summary: Bahraini sweet flour and saffron dessert
description: Khabeesa is a quick Bahraini sweet treat — toasted flour cooked with sugar, saffron and cardamom into a soft, fragrant pudding.
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour|1/2 cup sugar|2 tbsp ghee|1/4 tsp saffron in 1 tbsp hot water|1/2 tsp ground cardamom|1 tbsp rosewater|1.5 cups water|crushed pistachios for garnish
