instructions: Toast flour in a dry pan over medium heat, stirring constantly, until golden and fragrant (about 5 min).|Add ghee, stir to combine.|In a separate pot, dissolve sugar in water, bring to a boil.|Pour sugar syrup into the flour mixture gradually, stirring constantly.|Add saffron water, cardamom and rosewater.|Cook stirring for 5 minutes until thick and pulling from the sides.|Spread on a plate, garnish with pistachios, cut into squares when cool.

title: Dango
summary: Bahraini chickpea flour dumplings in a sweet syrup
description: Dango are small Bahraini dumplings made from chickpea flour, deep-fried and soaked in a cardamom-saffron syrup.
ingredients: 1 cup chickpea flour|1/4 cup rice flour|1/4 tsp baking powder|1/2 tsp ground cardamom|pinch of saffron in 1 tbsp warm water|1/4 cup water to make dough|oil for frying|For syrup: 1 cup sugar|1/2 cup water|1 tbsp rosewater|1/4 tsp cardamom
instructions: Mix chickpea flour, rice flour, baking powder and cardamom.|Add saffron water and enough water to make a soft dough.|Shape into small balls the size of a marble.|Deep fry in oil at 160°C (320°F) until golden brown, about 4 minutes.|Make syrup: boil sugar and water until slightly thick, add rosewater and cardamom.|Soak fried dumplings in warm syrup for 10 minutes.|Serve at room temperature.

title: Rubyan
summary: Bahraini dried shrimp and rice dish
description: Rubyan is a traditional Bahraini recipe using dried shrimp cooked with onions and spices, served over fragrant rice — a taste of Bahrain's pearl-diving heritage.
ingredients: 1 cup dried shrimp|2 cups basmati rice|2 onions, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 tbsp baharat spice|1 tsp turmeric|2 dried limes, pierced|3 tbsp oil|3 cups water|salt|fresh cilantro
instructions: Soak dried shrimp in warm water for 30 minutes, drain.|Heat oil, fry onions until golden.|Add dried shrimp, baharat, turmeric, stir 2 minutes.|Add tomatoes and dried limes, cook 5 minutes.|Add water and salt, bring to boil.|Add soaked rice, stir once, cover and cook on low 18 minutes.|Fluff, garnish with cilantro and serve.

title: Lugaimat
summary: Bahraini sweet fried dough balls drizzled with date syrup
description: Lugaimat are irresistible Bahraini fried dough balls — light, fluffy and golden, generously drizzled with date syrup and sesame seeds.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 tbsp sugar|1 tsp dry yeast|1/2 tsp ground cardamom|pinch of saffron in 1 tbsp warm water|1 cup warm water|1/4 tsp salt|oil for deep frying|date syrup (dibs) for drizzling|sesame seeds
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, yeast, cardamom and salt.|Add saffron water and warm water, mix to a thick batter.|Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour until bubbly.|Heat oil to 175°C (350°F).|Drop tablespoonfuls of batter into hot oil, fry until golden on all sides, about 3 minutes.|Drain on paper towels.|Pile on a plate, drizzle generously with date syrup and sprinkle sesame seeds.

title: Bahraini Saffron Tea
summary: Traditional Bahraini tea brewed with saffron and cardamom
description: Bahraini Saffron Tea is the signature hot drink — black tea infused with saffron, cardamom and sometimes rosewater, served sweet in small glass cups.
ingredients: 2 tbsp loose black tea|4 cups water|1/4 tsp saffron threads|4 cardamom pods, cracked|2 whole cloves|sugar to taste|1 tsp rosewater (optional)
instructions: Bring water to a boil with saffron, cardamom and cloves.|Add tea leaves, simmer 3-5 minutes to desired strength.|Strain into a teapot.|Add sugar and rosewater if desired.|Serve in small glass cups.

title: Margoog
summary: Bahraini thin dough and vegetable stew
description: Margoog is a hearty Bahraini stew of meat and vegetables with pieces of thin flat dough torn and cooked in the broth until silky.
ingredients: 500g lamb or chicken|2 potatoes, cubed|1 zucchini, cubed|2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp baharat|1 tsp turmeric|2 tbsp tomato paste|5 cups water|For dough: 2 cups flour|1/2 cup water|1/2 tsp salt|1 tbsp oil
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, water, salt and oil, knead smooth, rest 30 minutes.|Brown meat with onion in a pot, add spices and tomato paste.|Add all vegetables and water, simmer 30 minutes.|Roll dough very thin, tear into rough 3-inch pieces.|Drop dough pieces into the simmering stew.|Cook 15 more minutes until dough is soft and silky.|Serve in deep bowls.

title: Kebab Dajaj
summary: Bahraini grilled chicken kebabs with Arabic spices
description: Kebab Dajaj are juicy Bahraini chicken kebabs marinated in yogurt, garlic and baharat, grilled until charred and smoky.
ingredients: 600g chicken breast, cubed|1/2 cup yogurt|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp baharat spice|1 tsp paprika|1 tbsp lemon juice|2 tbsp olive oil|salt|skewers|Arabic bread for serving
instructions: Mix yogurt, garlic, baharat, paprika, lemon juice, oil and salt.|Add chicken cubes, coat well, marinate 2-4 hours.|Thread onto skewers.|Grill over high heat, turning, for 10-12 minutes until charred and cooked through.|Serve with Arabic bread, hummus and pickled vegetables.

title: Awwameh
description: Awwameh are small crispy dough balls fried until golden and immediately drenched in sugar syrup scented with orange blossom water.
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour|1/2 cup cornstarch|1 tsp dry yeast|1 tsp sugar|3/4 cup warm water|pinch of salt|oil for frying|For syrup: 1 cup sugar|1/2 cup water|1 tbsp lemon juice|1 tbsp orange blossom water
instructions: Make syrup first: boil sugar, water and lemon juice until slightly thick, add orange blossom water, cool.|Mix flour, cornstarch, yeast, sugar and salt.|Add warm water, mix to a smooth batter, cover and rest 1 hour until bubbly.|Heat oil to 175°C (350°F).|Drop small spoonfuls of batter into hot oil, fry until puffed and golden.|Immediately dunk fried puffs in cold syrup, toss to coat.|Serve at room temperature.

title: Muhallabia
summary: Bahraini rosewater milk pudding with pistachios
description: Muhallabia is a silky Bahraini milk pudding set with cornstarch, perfumed with rosewater and topped with ground pistachios.
ingredients: 4 cups whole milk|1/2 cup sugar|4 tbsp cornstarch|2 tbsp rosewater|2 tbsp ground pistachios|1 tbsp slivered almonds|ground cinnamon for garnish
instructions: Dissolve cornstarch in 1/2 cup of the cold milk.|Heat remaining milk with sugar until simmering.|Pour cornstarch mixture into the hot milk, stirring constantly.|Cook on medium-low, stirring, for 8-10 minutes until thick enough to coat the back of a spoon.|Remove from heat, stir in rosewater.|Pour into individual serving bowls.|Chill at least 3 hours until set.|Top with pistachios, almonds and a dusting of cinnamon before serving.

title: Fatayer
summary: Bahraini baked pastries with spinach or meat filling
description: Fatayer are triangular baked pastries popular in Bahrain — soft dough wrapped around a filling of spinach with sumac, or spiced ground meat.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 tsp dry yeast|1 tsp sugar|1 cup warm water|3 tbsp oil|1 tsp salt|For spinach filling: 300g spinach, chopped|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp lemon juice|1 tbsp sumac|2 tbsp olive oil|salt and pepper
instructions: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water, let foam.|Mix flour and salt, add yeast mixture and oil, knead until smooth, rise 1 hour.|Sauté onion, add spinach, cook until wilted, season with lemon, sumac, salt and pepper, cool.|Divide dough into small balls, roll into circles.|Place filling in center, bring up three edges and pinch into a triangle shape.|Place on a lined baking sheet, brush with oil.|Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 15-18 minutes until golden.

title: Cou-Cou and Flying Fish
summary: The national dish of Barbados — cornmeal and okra pudding with fried flying fish
description: Cou-Cou and Flying Fish is Barbados' signature dish — creamy cornmeal stirred with sliced okra, served alongside battered and fried flying fish in a tomato Creole sauce.
ingredients: 2 cups fine cornmeal|6 okras, sliced|4 cups water|2 tbsp butter|salt|For fish: 4 flying fish fillets (or other white fish)|2 tbsp lime juice|1 clove garlic, minced|1 tsp fresh thyme|1/2 cup flour for dredging|oil for frying|For sauce: 1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 scotch bonnet pepper, minced|1 tbsp butter
instructions: Boil okra slices in 4 cups salted water until tender, about 10 minutes.|Slowly pour in cornmeal, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.|Cook on low heat, stirring continuously, for 15-20 minutes until very thick and pulling from the pot.|Stir in butter, shape into a mound on a plate.|Season fish with lime juice, garlic, thyme and salt, let sit 15 minutes.|Dredge in flour, fry in hot oil until golden on both sides.|Make sauce: sauté onion, tomatoes and scotch bonnet in butter until thick.|Serve cou-cou topped with fish and sauce.

title: Pudding and Souse
summary: Barbadian steamed sweet potato pudding with pickled pork
description: Pudding and Souse is a Bajan Saturday tradition — spiced sweet potato pudding steamed in a casing, served with pickled pork in lime and cucumber.
ingredients: For pudding: 3 large sweet potatoes, grated|1/4 cup butter, melted|2 tbsp brown sugar|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1/4 tsp nutmeg|salt|sausage casing or foil|For souse: 500g pork shoulder, cubed|1 pig trotter|1 cucumber, sliced|1 onion, sliced|juice of 3 limes|1 scotch bonnet pepper|salt|fresh parsley
instructions: Mix grated sweet potato with melted butter, sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg and salt.|Stuff into sausage casings or roll in foil cylinders.|Steam for 1 hour until firm.|Boil pork and trotter in salted water until very tender, about 1.5 hours, drain and cool.|Slice the pork thinly.|Make pickling liquid: mix lime juice, sliced cucumber, onion, scotch bonnet, salt and a little water.|Toss pork in the pickling liquid, chill 1 hour.|Slice the pudding into rounds, serve alongside the cold souse.

title: Macaroni Pie Barbados
summary: Bajan-style baked macaroni and cheese with mustard and ketchup
description: Bajan Macaroni Pie is a denser, more flavorful cousin of American mac and cheese — baked with sharp cheddar, mustard and a touch of ketchup.
ingredients: 500g elbow macaroni|3 cups sharp cheddar cheese, grated|3 eggs, beaten|2 cups whole milk|2 tbsp butter|1 tbsp yellow mustard|2 tbsp ketchup|1 onion, finely diced|salt and pepper|1/4 tsp paprika
instructions: Cook macaroni until al dente, drain.|Mix beaten eggs, milk, mustard, ketchup, most of the cheese, onion, salt and pepper.|Stir in cooked macaroni.|Pour into a buttered baking dish, top with remaining cheese and paprika.|Dot with butter.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 40-45 minutes until golden and set.|Let rest 10 minutes before cutting into squares.

title: Fish Cakes
summary: Bajan salt cod fritters, a beloved street snack
description: Bajan Fish Cakes are crispy deep-fried fritters made with salt cod, herbs and scotch bonnet pepper — sold at every rum shop and street corner in Barbados.
ingredients: 250g salt cod, soaked overnight and flaked|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 egg|1/2 cup water|1 small onion, finely diced|2 green onions, sliced|1 tbsp fresh marjoram, chopped|1/2 scotch bonnet pepper, minced|1/2 tsp baking powder|salt and pepper|oil for deep frying
instructions: Flake the soaked and drained salt cod, removing any bones.|Mix flour, baking powder, egg and water into a thick batter.|Add flaked cod, onion, green onions, marjoram and scotch bonnet.|Season with pepper (taste before adding salt — the cod is salty).|Drop tablespoons of batter into 175°C (350°F) oil.|Fry until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes.|Drain on paper towels, serve hot with pepper sauce.

title: Pepperpot Barbados
summary: Bajan slow-cooked stew with cassareep and mixed meats
description: Bajan Pepperpot is a rich, dark stew of various meats slow-cooked with cassareep (cassava syrup), cinnamon and hot peppers — a dish for celebrations.
ingredients: 500g beef shin, cubed|500g pork shoulder, cubed|1/4 cup cassareep|2 tbsp brown sugar|1 cinnamon stick|3 whole cloves|1 scotch bonnet pepper, whole|1 large onion, diced|4 cups water|salt|2 tbsp vegetable oil
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy pot, brown beef and pork in batches.|Add onion, cook until softened.|Add cassareep, brown sugar, cinnamon, cloves, scotch bonnet and water.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to very low.|Simmer covered for 3-4 hours until meat is meltingly tender.|The sauce should be dark, thick and glossy.|Season with salt, serve with bread or rice.

title: Bajan Chicken
summary: Barbadian marinated and baked chicken with herbs and lime
description: Bajan Chicken is marinated overnight in a Bajan seasoning of lime, thyme, marjoram, garlic and scotch bonnet, then baked until golden and succulent.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces|juice of 2 limes|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 onion, grated|2 green onions, chopped|1 tbsp fresh thyme|1 tbsp fresh marjoram|1/2 scotch bonnet, minced|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper|1 tbsp browning sauce|2 tbsp vegetable oil
instructions: Blend or mix garlic, onion, green onions, thyme, marjoram, scotch bonnet, lime juice, salt, pepper and browning sauce.|Rub the seasoning all over chicken pieces, marinate overnight.|Place chicken in a baking dish, drizzle with oil.|Cover with foil, bake at 190°C (375°F) for 30 minutes.|Remove foil, bake another 25-30 minutes until skin is golden and crispy.|Let rest 5 minutes before serving.

title: Rice and Peas Barbados
summary: Bajan rice and pigeon peas cooked in coconut milk
description: Rice and Peas is a Bajan staple — fluffy rice cooked with pigeon peas, coconut milk, thyme and scotch bonnet pepper.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|1 can (400ml) coconut milk|1 can pigeon peas, drained|1 cup water|1 sprig fresh thyme|1 whole scotch bonnet pepper|1 clove garlic, minced|1 tbsp butter|salt and pepper
instructions: Rinse rice until water runs clear.|In a pot, combine coconut milk, water, garlic, thyme, whole scotch bonnet and salt.|Bring to a boil, add rice and peas.|Stir once, reduce to low heat, cover tightly.|Cook 18-20 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed.|Remove thyme and scotch bonnet (do not burst the pepper).|Fluff with a fork, stir in butter and serve.

title: Conkies
summary: Barbadian steamed pumpkin and coconut parcels wrapped in banana leaves
description: Conkies are traditional Bajan treats made for Independence Day — a sweet mixture of cornmeal, pumpkin, coconut and spices steamed in banana leaves.
ingredients: 1 cup cornmeal|1 cup pumpkin, grated|1 cup fresh coconut, grated|1/2 cup all-purpose flour|1/2 cup brown sugar|1/4 cup butter, melted|1/4 cup milk|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1/2 tsp nutmeg|1/2 tsp almond extract|1/4 cup raisins|banana leaves, cut into 8-inch squares
instructions: Mix cornmeal, flour, sugar, cinnamon and nutmeg.|Add grated pumpkin, coconut, melted butter, milk, almond extract and raisins.|Stir until well combined into a thick batter.|Soften banana leaves over an open flame or in hot water.|Place 2-3 tablespoons of mixture on each banana leaf, fold into a parcel and tie.|Steam parcels for 45-50 minutes until firm.|Unwrap and serve warm.

title: Jug Jug
summary: Bajan Christmas dish of pigeon peas and guinea corn with salt meat
description: Jug Jug is Barbados' traditional Christmas dish — a thick mixture of pigeon peas, ground guinea corn (millet) and salt meat, similar to Scottish haggis in concept.
ingredients: 2 cups pigeon peas, cooked|1 cup guinea corn flour (or millet flour)|250g salt beef, boiled and shredded|250g salt pork, boiled and shredded|1 large onion, diced|2 green onions, sliced|1 sprig fresh thyme|1 tbsp butter|3 cups stock (from boiling the meats)|salt and pepper
instructions: Boil salt meats until tender (about 1.5 hours), shred, reserve stock.|Grind cooked pigeon peas coarsely in a food processor.|Sauté onion in butter, add thyme and green onions.|Add ground peas, guinea corn flour, shredded meats and stock.|Stir and cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes until thick and porridge-like.|Season with pepper (careful with salt — the meats are salty).|Serve hot as a side with baked ham.

title: Breadfruit Pie
summary: Bajan savory pie with breadfruit, cheese and onion
description: Breadfruit Pie is a Barbadian comfort dish — sliced breadfruit layered with cheese, butter and onions, baked until bubbly and golden.
ingredients: 1 large breadfruit, peeled, boiled and sliced|200g cheddar cheese, grated|1 large onion, sliced into rings|3 tbsp butter|1 cup milk|salt and pepper|1/2 tsp paprika
instructions: Boil breadfruit until tender, slice into 1/2-inch rounds.|Layer breadfruit slices in a buttered baking dish.|Top with onion rings and half the cheese.|Repeat layers.|Pour milk over, dot with butter, season with salt, pepper and paprika.|Top with remaining cheese.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25-30 minutes until golden and bubbly.

title: Sweet Bread Barbados
summary: Bajan coconut sweet bread with dried fruits and spices
description: Bajan Sweet Bread is a dense, aromatic loaf packed with coconut, dried fruit, cherries and warm spices — a beloved snack throughout Barbados.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup sugar|1 cup desiccated coconut|1/2 cup mixed dried fruit|1/4 cup glacé cherries|2 eggs|1/2 cup butter, melted|1 cup milk|2 tsp baking powder|1 tsp vanilla|1/2 tsp nutmeg|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1/4 tsp almond extract
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, coconut, nutmeg and cinnamon.|Add dried fruit and cherries, toss to coat.|Beat eggs with milk, melted butter, vanilla and almond extract.|Pour wet into dry, stir until just combined.|Pour into a greased loaf pan.|Bake at 170°C (340°F) for 60-70 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.|Cool in pan 10 minutes, then turn out and cool completely.

title: Rum Punch
summary: Classic Bajan rum punch following the island's famous recipe
description: Barbados Rum Punch follows the old rhyme: one of sour, two of sweet, three of strong, four of weak — lime, sugar, rum and water with nutmeg on top.
ingredients: 1 cup fresh lime juice|2 cups simple syrup|3 cups Barbadian rum|4 cups water|Angostura bitters|freshly grated nutmeg|ice
instructions: Combine lime juice, simple syrup, rum and water in a large pitcher.|Add a few dashes of Angostura bitters, stir well.|Fill glasses with ice, pour the punch.|Grate fresh nutmeg on top of each glass.|Serve immediately.

title: Souse
summary: Bajan pickled pork in lime and cucumber
description: Souse is boiled pickled pork served cold in a tangy lime and cucumber broth — a refreshing Bajan dish often eaten on Saturdays.
ingredients: 1 kg pork loin or pig head meat|2 cucumbers, sliced|1 large onion, sliced|juice of 4 limes|1 scotch bonnet pepper, sliced|3 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|salt|4 cups water for boiling
instructions: Boil pork in salted water until very tender, about 1 hour.|Drain and let meat cool, slice thinly.|Mix lime juice, sliced cucumber, onion, scotch bonnet and parsley with a cup of the cooking liquid.|Add sliced pork to the lime mixture, toss.|Season with salt, chill for at least 2 hours.|Serve cold, spooning the cucumber-lime liquid over the meat.

title: Bajan Black Cake
summary: Rich Barbadian rum fruit cake for Christmas celebrations
description: Bajan Black Cake is an intensely rich, dark fruit cake — dried fruits soaked in rum for months, blended and baked into a dense, moist cake for the holidays.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed dried fruits (prunes, raisins, currants, cherries)|1 cup dark rum|1/2 cup cherry brandy|1 cup brown sugar|1 cup butter|4 eggs|2 cups all-purpose flour|1 tsp baking powder|2 tbsp browning sauce|1 tsp vanilla|1 tsp mixed spice (cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice)|1 tsp almond extract
instructions: Soak dried fruits in rum and brandy for at least 2 weeks (ideally months), blend into a paste.|Cream butter and sugar until fluffy.|Beat in eggs one at a time.|Add browning sauce, vanilla and almond extract.|Fold in flour, baking powder and mixed spice.|Stir in the blended fruit mixture.|Pour into a lined 9-inch cake pan.|Bake at 150°C (300°F) for 2 hours until a skewer comes out clean.|Brush top with extra rum while still hot, let cool completely.

title: Bajan Fried Plantains
summary: Sweet ripe plantains pan-fried in butter until caramelized
description: Fried plantains are a simple Bajan side dish — ripe sweet plantains sliced and fried in butter until golden and caramelized on the edges.
ingredients: 3 ripe plantains (skin should be mostly black)|3 tbsp butter|pinch of salt|pinch of cinnamon (optional)
instructions: Peel plantains and slice diagonally into 1/2-inch pieces.|Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat.|Fry plantain slices 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and caramelized.|Sprinkle with a tiny pinch of salt and cinnamon if desired.|Serve warm as a side dish.

title: Soup Saturday
summary: Bajan Saturday tradition of hearty split pea and vegetable soup
description: Soup Saturday is a Barbadian tradition — a thick, hearty soup packed with split peas, root vegetables, dumplings and salt meat, cooked every Saturday.
ingredients: 1 cup green split peas|250g salt beef or pig tail, soaked overnight|1 large sweet potato, cubed|2 potatoes, cubed|1 carrot, diced|1 onion, diced|2 cups pumpkin, cubed|1 sprig fresh thyme|1 scotch bonnet pepper, whole|8 cups water|For dumplings: 1 cup flour, 1/4 cup water, pinch salt
instructions: Boil soaked salt meat in water for 30 minutes.|Add split peas, cook another 30 minutes until starting to soften.|Add all vegetables, thyme and whole scotch bonnet.|Make dumplings: mix flour, water and salt into a stiff dough, break into small pieces.|Drop dumplings into the soup.|Simmer everything for 30 more minutes until thick and vegetables are tender.|Remove scotch bonnet, season with pepper and serve hot.

title: Tamarind Balls
summary: Bajan sweet and sour tamarind candy rolled in sugar
description: Tamarind Balls are a popular Barbadian candy — tangy tamarind pulp mixed with sugar, shaped into balls and rolled in granulated sugar.
ingredients: 1 cup tamarind pulp (seedless)|1/2 cup brown sugar|1/4 tsp salt|1/4 tsp hot pepper sauce (optional)|granulated sugar for rolling
instructions: Mix tamarind pulp with brown sugar and salt until well combined.|Add a dash of pepper sauce if you want a sweet-hot version.|Roll the mixture into small balls about 1 inch in diameter.|Roll each ball in granulated sugar to coat.|Place on parchment paper and let firm up for a few hours.|Store in an airtight container at room temperature.

title: Corn Soup
summary: Thick Bajan corn soup with dumplings and coconut
description: Bajan Corn Soup is a thick, creamy soup made from fresh corn kernels blended with coconut milk and spices, with flour dumplings dropped in.
ingredients: 6 ears fresh corn, kernels cut off|1 can (400ml) coconut milk|1 onion, diced|2 green onions, sliced|1 sprig thyme|1 scotch bonnet pepper, whole|4 cups water|salt and pepper|For dumplings: 1 cup flour, pinch salt, 1/4 cup water
instructions: Blend half the corn kernels with 1 cup water until smooth, set aside.|Sauté onion until soft, add whole corn kernels, blended corn, remaining water and coconut milk.|Add thyme and scotch bonnet.|Make dumplings: mix flour, salt and water into a stiff dough, pinch off small pieces.|Drop dumplings into the simmering soup.|Cook 20-25 minutes until corn is tender and soup is thick.|Remove scotch bonnet, season with salt and pepper.|Serve hot sprinkled with sliced green onions.

title: Bajan Saltfish Salad
summary: Cold salad of salt cod with vegetables and lime dressing
description: This refreshing Bajan salad combines flaked salt cod with tomatoes, cucumbers, onions and peppers in a zesty lime and olive oil dressing.
ingredients: 300g salt cod, soaked overnight and cooked|2 tomatoes, diced|1 cucumber, diced|1 bell pepper, diced|1 small onion, thinly sliced|2 green onions, sliced|juice of 2 limes|3 tbsp olive oil|1/2 scotch bonnet, minced|salt and pepper|fresh parsley
instructions: Flake the cooked salt cod into large pieces, discarding skin and bones.|Combine with diced tomatoes, cucumber, bell pepper and onion.|Whisk lime juice, olive oil, scotch bonnet, salt and pepper for dressing.|Pour dressing over the salad, toss gently.|Let sit 15 minutes for flavors to meld.|Garnish with green onions and parsley, serve.

title: Baked Breadnuts
summary: Roasted breadnuts served as a Barbadian snack
description: Breadnuts are the seeded variety of breadfruit, roasted until their chestnut-like flesh is tender and nutty — a traditional Barbadian foraging snack.
ingredients: 2 cups fresh breadnuts|water for boiling|1 tbsp butter|salt
instructions: Score each breadnut shell with a small cut.|Boil in salted water for 20 minutes.|Drain, then spread on a baking sheet.|Roast at 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes until shells crack open.|Peel off shells, toss the soft nut flesh with butter and salt.|Serve warm as a snack — they taste similar to chestnuts.

title: Draniki
summary: Belarusian crispy grated potato pancakes, the national dish
description: Draniki are golden, crispy potato pancakes made from coarsely grated raw potatoes fried in oil — the most iconic dish of Belarusian cuisine.
ingredients: 6 large potatoes, peeled and coarsely grated|1 onion, grated|1 egg|2 tbsp all-purpose flour|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper|vegetable oil for frying|sour cream for serving
instructions: Grate potatoes on the coarse side of a grater, squeeze out excess liquid through cheesecloth.|Mix with grated onion, egg, flour, salt and pepper.|Heat a generous amount of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.|Drop large spoonfuls of the mixture, flatten into 3-inch pancakes.|Fry 3-4 minutes per side until deeply golden and crispy.|Drain on paper towels, serve hot with sour cream.

title: Machanka
summary: Belarusian pork rib stew served with thick pancakes
description: Machanka is a rich Belarusian stew of pork ribs and sausage simmered in a creamy gravy, traditionally served with thick buckwheat or potato pancakes for dipping.
ingredients: 500g pork spare ribs|200g smoked sausage, sliced|1 large onion, diced|2 tbsp all-purpose flour|2 tbsp butter|1 cup sour cream|1 cup pork or chicken broth|2 bay leaves|salt and pepper|thick pancakes (bliny) for serving
instructions: Brown pork ribs in butter on all sides, remove.|Sauté onion in the same pot until golden.|Add flour, stir 1 minute to make a roux.|Pour in broth, stir until smooth and simmering.|Return ribs, add sliced sausage and bay leaves.|Cover and simmer on low heat for 1 hour until ribs are tender.|Stir in sour cream, heat through 5 minutes.|Serve the stew over thick pancakes, spooning the creamy sauce generously.

title: Khaladnik
summary: Belarusian cold beet soup for summer
description: Khaladnik is Belarus's refreshing cold beet soup — vibrant pink, made with kefir or sour cream, diced beets, cucumbers, eggs and plenty of fresh dill.
ingredients: 3 medium beets, boiled and diced|2 cups kefir or buttermilk|1 cup sour cream|2 cups cold water|2 cucumbers, diced|4 hard-boiled eggs, chopped|4 green onions, sliced|1/2 cup fresh dill, chopped|juice of 1 lemon|salt and pepper
instructions: Whisk kefir, sour cream and cold water until smooth.|Add diced beets, cucumbers, eggs, green onions and dill.|Season with lemon juice, salt and pepper.|Chill for at least 1 hour.|Serve ice-cold in bowls, with an extra spoon of sour cream if desired.

title: Borscht Belarusian
summary: Belarusian-style hearty beet and cabbage soup with pork
description: Belarusian borscht is a hearty version with pork ribs, beets, cabbage, potatoes and a generous amount of dill, served with sour cream.
ingredients: 500g pork ribs|2 medium beets, grated|3 potatoes, cubed|1/4 head cabbage, shredded|1 large carrot, grated|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp tomato paste|2 cloves garlic, minced|8 cups water|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tbsp vinegar|salt and pepper|sour cream and dill for serving
instructions: Simmer pork ribs in water for 1 hour, skim foam.|Sauté onion and carrot in oil, add tomato paste and grated beets, cook 10 minutes.|Add potatoes to the broth, cook 10 minutes.|Add the beet-onion mixture and shredded cabbage.|Simmer 15 minutes until all vegetables are tender.|Add garlic, vinegar, salt and pepper.|Serve hot with a generous dollop of sour cream and fresh dill.

title: Kletski
summary: Belarusian potato dumplings stuffed with meat
description: Kletski are Belarusian potato dumplings — grated potato dough wrapped around a seasoned meat filling, boiled until tender and served with sour cream.
ingredients: 6 large potatoes, half grated raw, half boiled and mashed|1 egg|3 tbsp potato starch|salt|For filling: 250g ground pork|1 onion, finely diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|salt and pepper|sour cream for serving|fried onions for garnish
instructions: Grate raw potatoes, squeeze out all liquid, reserve the starch that settles.|Mix grated raw potato with mashed potato, egg, potato starch and salt into a pliable dough.|Cook ground pork with onion and garlic until browned, season.|Flatten a portion of dough in your palm, place a spoon of filling in the center.|Fold dough around filling, seal and shape into a smooth ball.|Boil in salted water for 15-20 minutes until they float and are cooked through.|Serve topped with sour cream and fried onions.

title: Vereshchaka
summary: Historic Belarusian sausage and beet leaf stew
description: Vereshchaka is a historic Belarusian dish of sliced homemade sausage simmered in kvass with beet leaves — hearty and tangy.
ingredients: 500g homemade pork sausage or kielbasa, sliced|1 onion, diced|2 cups beet greens or chard, chopped|1 cup kvass (bread drink) or light beer|1 tbsp all-purpose flour|2 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|fresh dill
instructions: Brown sliced sausage in butter, remove.|Sauté onion in the same pan until soft.|Add flour, stir 1 minute.|Pour in kvass, stir until smooth.|Return sausage, add beet greens.|Simmer 15 minutes until greens are wilted and sauce is thickened.|Season, garnish with dill, serve with draniki or bread.

title: Babka Belarus
summary: Belarusian baked potato pudding with bacon and onion
description: Babka is a beloved Belarusian potato casserole — grated potatoes mixed with bacon, onions and egg, baked until golden with a crispy top and creamy center.
ingredients: 8 large potatoes, peeled and grated|150g smoked bacon, diced|1 large onion, diced|2 eggs|3 tbsp sour cream|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper|2 tbsp butter for greasing
instructions: Grate potatoes, squeeze out excess liquid.|Fry diced bacon with onion until bacon is crispy and onion golden.|Mix grated potato with eggs, sour cream, bacon-onion mixture, salt and pepper.|Pour into a well-buttered baking dish.|Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 1 hour until top is deeply golden and edges crispy.|Let rest 10 minutes, cut into squares.|Serve with sour cream.

title: Kolduny
summary: Belarusian filled potato dumplings similar to Lithuanian zeppelin
description: Kolduny are Belarusian potato dumplings stuffed with a savory mushroom or meat filling, pan-fried after boiling for a crispy exterior.
ingredients: 6 large potatoes, grated and squeezed dry|1 egg|2 tbsp potato starch|salt|For filling: 200g mushrooms, finely chopped|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|sour cream for serving
instructions: Sauté mushrooms and onion in butter until golden and dry, season.|Mix grated potato with egg, starch and salt into a smooth dough.|Flatten portions of dough, fill with mushroom mixture, seal into round dumplings.|Boil in salted water 12-15 minutes until they float.|Optionally pan-fry in butter after boiling for a crispy crust.|Serve with sour cream.

title: Kvass Belarus
description: Belarusian Kvass is a mildly fermented drink made from dark rye bread, lightly sweet and tangy — the traditional summer thirst-quencher.
ingredients: 300g dark rye bread, sliced and toasted|3 liters boiling water|100g sugar|5g dry yeast|handful of raisins|1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: Toast bread slices in the oven until very dark and crispy.|Place in a large container, pour boiling water over, let steep for 4 hours.|Strain out the bread, pressing to extract all liquid.|Dissolve sugar in the warm liquid, cool to lukewarm.|Add yeast, stir, cover loosely and ferment at room temperature for 12-24 hours.|Strain again, add lemon juice and a few raisins to each bottle.|Refrigerate and drink within 3-4 days, it gets more sour with time.

title: Pyachista
summary: Belarusian whole roasted pork with garlic and caraway
description: Pyachista is a festive Belarusian roast — a whole piece of pork studded with garlic, rubbed with caraway and slow-roasted until the outside is crackling.
ingredients: 2 kg pork shoulder or loin, bone-in|8 cloves garlic, slivered|1 tbsp caraway seeds|1 tbsp coarse salt|1 tsp black pepper|2 bay leaves|1 cup water
instructions: Make deep incisions all over the pork, stuff each with a sliver of garlic.|Rub entire surface with salt, pepper and caraway seeds.|Place in a roasting pan, tuck bay leaves underneath, add water to the pan.|Roast uncovered at 220°C (430°F) for 30 minutes to crisp the surface.|Reduce to 160°C (325°F), cover with foil and roast 2.5 hours until internal temp reaches 75°C.|Let rest 15 minutes, slice thickly and serve with pan juices.

title: Kindziuk
description: Kindziuk is a homemade Belarusian sausage made from coarsely ground pork seasoned with garlic, pepper and coriander, stuffed into natural casings and cold-smoked.
ingredients: 2 kg pork shoulder, coarsely ground|500g pork back fat, cubed|6 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ground coriander|1/2 tsp caraway seeds|natural hog casings|wood chips for smoking
instructions: Mix ground pork with cubed fat, garlic, salt, pepper, coriander and caraway.|Stuff into natural casings, tie into 12-inch links.|Hang in a cool dry place for 24 hours to form a pellicle.|Cold-smoke at 20-25°C (70-77°F) for 3-5 days using hardwood chips.|Alternatively, hang in a well-ventilated cool space to dry-cure for 2-3 weeks.|Slice thinly and serve as a cold appetizer with bread and mustard.

title: Solyanka Belarus
summary: Belarusian sour and savory meat soup with pickles and olives
description: Belarusian Solyanka is a thick, tangy soup loaded with multiple meats, pickled cucumbers, olives, capers and lemon — hearty and complex.
ingredients: 300g beef, cubed|200g smoked sausage, sliced|100g ham, diced|3 pickled cucumbers, diced|2 tbsp pickle brine|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp tomato paste|1/4 cup olives|1 tbsp capers|1 lemon, sliced|6 cups beef broth|2 tbsp butter|sour cream|fresh dill
instructions: Simmer beef in broth until tender, about 1 hour.|Sauté onion in butter, add tomato paste, cook 2 minutes.|Add pickled cucumbers and pickle brine, cook 5 minutes.|Add the onion mixture, sausage and ham to the broth.|Add olives and capers, simmer 10 minutes.|Serve with a lemon slice and dollop of sour cream in each bowl, sprinkle dill.

title: Kasha Belarus
summary: Belarusian buckwheat porridge with mushrooms and onion
description: Buckwheat kasha is a Belarusian staple — toasted buckwheat groats cooked until fluffy, topped with sautéed mushrooms and caramelized onions.
ingredients: 2 cups buckwheat groats|4 cups water|1 tsp salt|200g mushrooms, sliced|1 large onion, sliced|3 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|fresh parsley
instructions: Toast buckwheat in a dry pan until fragrant, about 3 minutes.|Add water and salt, bring to a boil, reduce heat and cover.|Cook 15 minutes until water is absorbed and groats are tender.|Meanwhile, sauté mushrooms and onion in butter until golden brown.|Fluff buckwheat with a fork.|Top with mushroom-onion mixture, garnish with parsley.

title: Derun
summary: Belarusian thick potato pancake baked in the oven
description: Derun is a thick, oven-baked version of draniki — a large potato cake baked until crusty on the outside and soft inside, often with bacon layered through.
ingredients: 8 large potatoes, coarsely grated|1 onion, grated|2 eggs|3 tbsp flour|150g smoked bacon, diced|salt and pepper|3 tbsp butter|sour cream for serving
instructions: Squeeze excess liquid from grated potatoes.|Mix with onion, eggs, flour, salt and pepper.|Fry bacon until crispy.|Grease a baking dish with butter, layer half the potato mixture, scatter bacon, then remaining potato.|Dot top with butter.|Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 45-50 minutes until deeply golden and crusty.|Cut into wedges, serve with sour cream.

title: Bliny Belarus
summary: Belarusian thin buckwheat crepes served with fillings
description: Belarusian bliny are thin, lacy crepes made with buckwheat flour, served with sour cream, smoked fish, mushrooms or jam.
ingredients: 1 cup buckwheat flour|1/2 cup all-purpose flour|2 eggs|2 cups milk|1 tbsp sugar|1/2 tsp salt|2 tbsp melted butter|butter for cooking
instructions: Whisk flours, eggs, milk, sugar, salt and melted butter into a thin, smooth batter.|Let rest 30 minutes.|Heat a lightly buttered crepe pan over medium heat.|Pour a thin layer of batter, swirl to coat the pan.|Cook 1-2 minutes until edges lift, flip and cook 30 seconds more.|Stack crepes, keep warm.|Serve with sour cream, mushrooms, smoked fish or berry jam.

title: Solonina
summary: Belarusian cured pork belly with garlic and bay leaf
description: Solonina is a traditional Belarusian salt-cured pork belly — rubbed with garlic, bay leaf and peppercorns, cured for weeks, then sliced thin and eaten raw.
ingredients: 1 kg pork belly, skin on|3 tbsp coarse salt|1 tbsp sugar|6 cloves garlic, sliced|4 bay leaves|1 tsp black peppercorns|1/2 tsp allspice berries|1/2 tsp caraway seeds
instructions: Mix salt, sugar, peppercorns, allspice and caraway.|Score the skin side of the pork belly in a crosshatch pattern.|Rub the salt mixture all over, pressing into the scores.|Tuck garlic slices and bay leaves between layers.|Place in a container, cover and refrigerate for 7-10 days, flipping daily.|Rinse off excess salt, pat dry.|Slice paper-thin and serve as a cold appetizer with black bread and mustard.

title: Kisiel
summary: Belarusian fruit jelly drink made with starch
description: Kisiel is a traditional Belarusian dessert drink — fresh berries cooked into a sweet, thick jelly-like consistency with potato starch.
ingredients: 3 cups fresh or frozen berries (cranberries, strawberries, or blueberries)|4 cups water|1/2 cup sugar|3 tbsp potato starch|1/4 cup cold water
instructions: Boil berries in water for 10 minutes until soft.|Strain through a sieve, pressing to extract all juice, discard pulp.|Return juice to pot, add sugar, bring to simmer.|Dissolve potato starch in cold water.|Pour starch mixture into the simmering juice, stirring constantly.|Cook 2-3 minutes until thickened to a drinkable consistency.|Serve warm or chilled in glasses or bowls.

title: Bulba Grill
summary: Belarusian grilled potato skewers with herbs
description: Bulba Grill celebrates Belarus's potato obsession — whole baby potatoes par-boiled, skewered, brushed with garlic-herb butter and grilled until crispy.
ingredients: 1 kg baby potatoes|4 tbsp butter, melted|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp fresh dill, chopped|1 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|1 tsp paprika|salt and pepper|wooden skewers, soaked
instructions: Boil baby potatoes in salted water until just tender, about 12 minutes, drain.|Mix melted butter with garlic, dill, parsley, paprika, salt and pepper.|Thread potatoes onto skewers.|Brush generously with garlic-herb butter.|Grill over medium heat, turning and basting, for 8-10 minutes until charred and crispy outside.|Serve hot straight from the grill.

title: Zhur Soup
description: Zhur is an ancient Belarusian soup made from fermented oat flour broth — sour, creamy and unique, traditionally served with boiled eggs and sausage.
ingredients: 1 cup oat flour|3 cups warm water|1 clove garlic|4 cups chicken broth|200g smoked sausage, sliced|4 hard-boiled eggs, halved|2 tbsp butter|1 onion, diced|salt and pepper|fresh dill
instructions: Mix oat flour with warm water and garlic, cover loosely and let ferment in a warm place for 2-3 days until sour.|Strain the fermented liquid (this is the zhur base).|Sauté onion in butter, add to chicken broth and bring to a simmer.|Slowly stir in the zhur liquid.|Add sliced sausage, simmer 10 minutes.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve with halved boiled eggs and dill in each bowl.

title: Kulaga
summary: Belarusian traditional berry and flour dessert
description: Kulaga is an old Belarusian dessert — fresh berries cooked with rye flour and honey into a thick, sweet-tart pudding.
ingredients: 3 cups mixed berries (raspberries, blueberries, lingonberries)|1/4 cup rye flour|1/4 cup honey|1 cup water|pinch of salt
instructions: Simmer berries in water until they break down, about 10 minutes.|Mix rye flour with a little cold water into a smooth paste.|Stir flour paste into the berries, cook on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring constantly.|Add honey, stir until incorporated.|Cook until thick and pudding-like.|Serve warm or cold with cream or as a topping for bliny.

title: Kuli-Kuli
description: Kuli-Kuli are popular Beninese snacks made from ground roasted peanuts shaped and deep-fried into crunchy bites — sold as street food everywhere.
ingredients: 2 cups roasted peanuts, skinned|1/2 tsp salt|1/4 tsp cayenne pepper|1/4 cup water|oil for deep frying
instructions: Grind roasted peanuts in a food processor until they form a thick paste.|Add salt, cayenne and a little water, knead until the oil starts to separate.|Squeeze out excess peanut oil (save it for cooking).|Shape the paste into small balls, sticks or flat rounds.|Deep fry at 160°C (320°F) for 4-5 minutes until golden and crunchy.|Drain on paper towels, serve as a snack or crumbled over salads.

title: Ablo
summary: Beninese steamed rice and corn cakes
description: Ablo are soft, slightly sour steamed cakes made from fermented rice and corn batter — a staple street food in Benin served with spicy sauces.
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour|1 cup corn flour|1.5 cups warm water|1/2 tsp dry yeast|1 tsp sugar|1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Mix rice flour and corn flour in a bowl.|Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water, let foam.|Pour yeast mixture into flour, mix into a smooth batter.|Cover and let ferment in a warm place for 6-8 hours or overnight until bubbly and slightly sour.|Stir batter, add salt.|Pour into greased small ramekins or molds.|Steam for 20-25 minutes until firm and cooked through.|Serve warm with pepper sauce or stew.

title: Amiwo
summary: Beninese tomato cornmeal paste served with chicken
description: Amiwo is a Beninese dish of cornmeal cooked in a rich tomato sauce until thick and smooth, served alongside grilled or fried chicken.
ingredients: 2 cups fine cornmeal|4 ripe tomatoes, blended|1 onion, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp tomato paste|3 tbsp palm oil or vegetable oil|1 scotch bonnet pepper, minced|2 cups water|salt|grilled chicken for serving
instructions: Heat oil, sauté onion and garlic until soft.|Add blended tomatoes, tomato paste and scotch bonnet, cook until oil separates, about 15 minutes.|Add water, bring to a simmer.|Gradually sprinkle in cornmeal, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.|Cook on low heat, stirring frequently, for 15-20 minutes until thick and pulling from the sides.|Shape into a mound on a plate.|Serve with grilled or fried chicken and pepper sauce.

title: Akassa
description: Akassa is a fermented corn paste that is a dietary staple in Benin — corn dough is fermented for days then cooked into a smooth, tangy ball served with soups and stews.
ingredients: 2 cups dry corn kernels or corn flour|4 cups water|banana leaves or plastic wrap for wrapping
instructions: If using whole corn: soak kernels for 2 days, then grind to a smooth paste.|Mix corn paste with water, let ferment covered in a warm place for 2-3 days until sour.|Strain through a fine cloth, collecting the starchy liquid.|Cook the starch liquid over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens into a smooth, stretchy paste.|Shape into balls, wrap in banana leaves.|Serve alongside soups, stews or spicy sauces.

title: Gbegiri Soup Benin
summary: Beninese bean soup with palm oil
description: Gbegiri is a smooth, creamy bean soup made from peeled black-eyed peas cooked with palm oil and pepper — often served mixed with ewedu leaf soup.
ingredients: 2 cups black-eyed peas, soaked and skinned|1 onion, diced|3 tbsp palm oil|2 scotch bonnet peppers|1 tsp ground crayfish|4 cups water|salt|iru (locust beans) for flavor (optional)
instructions: Boil skinned black-eyed peas until very soft, about 45 minutes.|Blend until completely smooth.|Heat palm oil, sauté onion until soft.|Add blended peppers, cook 10 minutes.|Add the bean puree, ground crayfish and iru.|Simmer 15 minutes, adding water to reach desired consistency.|Season with salt, serve over amala or fufu.

title: Wagashi
summary: Beninese fried cheese made from cow's milk
description: Wagashi is a West African fresh cheese traditional to Benin's Fulani people — cow's milk curded and formed into blocks, often fried and eaten as a protein-rich snack.
ingredients: 2 liters fresh whole cow's milk|juice of 2 lemons or 1/4 cup vinegar|1/2 tsp salt|oil for frying|chili powder for serving
instructions: Heat milk in a pot until just about to boil.|Remove from heat, add lemon juice or vinegar, stir gently.|Let sit 10 minutes until curds separate from whey.|Strain through cheesecloth, squeeze out whey.|Press curds into a mold or shape by hand into blocks.|Let firm up for 1 hour.|Slice and fry in oil until golden on each side.|Serve sprinkled with chili powder, often with peanut sauce.

title: Yovo Doko
description: Yovo Doko are popular Beninese street snacks — sweet dough balls made from flour, sugar and nutmeg, deep-fried until golden and fluffy.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/3 cup sugar|1 tsp baking powder|1/2 tsp nutmeg|1/4 tsp salt|1 egg|3/4 cup warm water|1 tsp vanilla|oil for deep frying
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, nutmeg and salt.|Add egg, water and vanilla, stir into a thick batter.|Let rest 15 minutes.|Heat oil to 170°C (340°F).|Drop tablespoons of batter into hot oil.|Fry 3-4 minutes, turning, until golden brown and cooked through.|Drain on paper towels, serve warm.

title: Ata Din Din
description: Ata Din Din is a fried chili pepper paste from Benin — habaneros, tomatoes and onions slowly fried in palm oil until concentrated and deeply flavorful.
ingredients: 10 scotch bonnet or habanero peppers|4 ripe tomatoes|2 large onions, diced|1/4 cup palm oil|1 tsp ground crayfish|1 tsp iru (locust bean) or bouillon|salt
instructions: Blend peppers and tomatoes into a rough paste.|Heat palm oil in a pan until shimmering.|Fry diced onions until golden.|Add the pepper-tomato paste, reduce heat to low.|Fry slowly for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until oil floats on top and paste is deep red.|Add ground crayfish and iru, cook 5 more minutes.|Season with salt, use as a condiment with everything.

title: Gboma Dessi
summary: Beninese spinach stew with tomatoes and crab
description: Gboma Dessi is a Beninese green stew of large-leaf spinach (gboma) cooked with tomatoes, onions and crab or smoked fish in palm oil.
ingredients: 500g large-leaf spinach or Swiss chard, chopped|3 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|200g crab meat or smoked fish|3 tbsp palm oil|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 scotch bonnet pepper|1 tsp ground crayfish|salt|cooked rice or akassa for serving
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion and garlic until fragrant.|Add diced tomatoes and scotch bonnet, cook 10 minutes until thick.|Add crab or smoked fish, stir.|Add chopped spinach, cover and cook 10 minutes until wilted.|Add ground crayfish, season with salt.|Serve over rice or alongside akassa.

title: Gari Foto
summary: Beninese fried cassava granules with vegetables
description: Gari Foto is a quick Beninese dish of gari (toasted cassava granules) stir-fried with tomatoes, onions, eggs and vegetables — a popular fast meal.
ingredients: 2 cups gari (cassava granules)|3 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|2 eggs|1/4 cup palm oil|1 bell pepper, diced|1 scotch bonnet pepper, minced|salt|water as needed
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion and peppers until soft.|Add tomatoes, cook until soft and saucy.|Crack in eggs, scramble with the vegetables.|Gradually add gari, stirring to mix evenly.|Sprinkle water as needed to reach a fluffy but not wet consistency.|Season with salt, cook 3 more minutes stirring.|Serve hot as a complete meal.

title: Tôh
summary: Beninese stiff millet or corn porridge
description: Tôh is a thick, stiff porridge made from millet or corn flour — a fundamental staple in northern Benin, eaten by hand with soups and sauces.
ingredients: 2 cups millet flour or fine corn flour|4 cups water|1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Bring 3 cups of water to a boil.|Mix flour with remaining 1 cup cold water into a smooth paste.|Pour the paste into the boiling water, stirring vigorously.|Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon for 15-20 minutes.|The mixture should become very thick and pull away from the sides.|Wet a bowl, scoop the tôh into it to shape.|Invert onto a plate, serve with soup or stew alongside.

title: Pâte Noire
description: Pâte Noire is a traditional Beninese starchy staple — corn dough that's been fermented longer than akassa, giving it a darker color and deeper sour taste.
ingredients: 2 cups corn flour|3 cups water|banana leaves for wrapping
instructions: Mix corn flour with water into a smooth paste.|Let ferment in a covered container at room temperature for 3-4 days until sour and darkened.|Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly for 15 minutes until thick.|Shape into balls, wrap in banana leaves.|Serve with any Beninese soup or stew.

title: Palm Nut Soup Benin
summary: Beninese rich soup made from palm fruit extract
description: Palm Nut Soup is a rich, orange-red Beninese soup made by boiling and pressing palm fruits to extract their cream, cooked with meat, fish and crab.
ingredients: 2 cups palm fruit pulp or 1 can palm cream|500g goat meat or chicken, cut into pieces|200g smoked fish|1 onion, diced|2 scotch bonnet peppers|1 tbsp ground crayfish|4 cups water|salt|fufu for serving
instructions: If using fresh palm fruits: boil, pound and strain to extract cream.|Brown meat pieces in a pot, add water and simmer until tender (30-40 min).|Add palm cream, onion, scotch bonnet peppers and smoked fish.|Simmer 20 minutes until oil rises to the surface and soup thickens.|Add ground crayfish, season with salt.|Serve hot with fufu or rice.

title: Koklo Meme
description: Koklo Meme is Beninese grilled chicken marinated in a paste of onion, ginger, garlic and chili, grilled over charcoal until smoky and charred.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, spatchcocked|2 onions, roughly chopped|4 cloves garlic|2-inch piece ginger|3 scotch bonnet peppers|2 tbsp vegetable oil|juice of 2 limes|1 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 bouillon cube
instructions: Blend onions, garlic, ginger, scotch bonnet peppers, oil, lime juice, salt, pepper and bouillon into a smooth paste.|Score the chicken deeply all over.|Rub the paste all over and into the scores, marinate 4 hours or overnight.|Grill over medium charcoal, turning frequently, for 40-50 minutes until charred and cooked through.|Rest 5 minutes, chop into pieces.|Serve with attieke or fried plantains.

title: Fakoye
summary: Beninese smoked fish in peanut sauce
description: Fakoye is a Beninese dish of smoked fish simmered in a rich, thick peanut sauce with tomatoes and peppers, served with rice or tôh.
ingredients: 4 pieces smoked fish (mackerel or catfish)|1 cup smooth peanut butter|3 tomatoes, blended|1 onion, diced|2 scotch bonnet peppers|2 cups water|2 tbsp palm oil|salt|fresh basil leaves
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion until golden.|Add blended tomatoes and peppers, cook 15 minutes.|Dissolve peanut butter in water, add to the pot.|Stir well, simmer 15 minutes until sauce thickens.|Add smoked fish pieces (handle gently so they don't break).|Cook 10 more minutes.|Season with salt, garnish with basil.|Serve with rice, tôh or yam.

title: Djenkoumé
description: Djenkoumé is similar to amiwo — cornmeal cooked in a spiced tomato sauce, but with more vegetables and fish, a staple comfort food in southern Benin.
ingredients: 2 cups cornmeal|5 ripe tomatoes, blended|1 onion, diced|200g smoked shrimp|3 tbsp palm oil|1 scotch bonnet pepper|1 tsp ground ginger|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Heat palm oil, fry onion until soft.|Add blended tomatoes, scotch bonnet and ginger, cook 20 minutes.|Add smoked shrimp, stir.|Add water, bring to a simmer.|Gradually add cornmeal, stirring constantly.|Cook on low heat 15-20 minutes, stirring often, until thick and smooth.|Season with salt, serve as a complete meal.

title: Kpowo
summary: Beninese spicy stewed tomatoes with onions
description: Kpowo is a Beninese tomato condiment — tomatoes and onions stewed slowly with chili and palm oil until thick, served as a side sauce with everything.
ingredients: 6 ripe tomatoes, roughly chopped|2 onions, sliced|3 tbsp palm oil|2 scotch bonnet peppers, minced|1 clove garlic, minced|1 tsp ground crayfish|salt
instructions: Heat palm oil in a saucepan.|Add onions and garlic, cook until softened.|Add tomatoes and scotch bonnet peppers.|Cook on medium-low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and jammy.|Add ground crayfish, season with salt.|Serve as a condiment alongside rice, yam, or tôh.

title: Sodabi
description: Sodabi is Benin's national spirit — a potent liquor distilled from fermented palm wine, clear and fiery, drunk neat or mixed with herbs.
ingredients: 5 liters fresh palm wine|traditional still or distillation equipment
instructions: Collect fresh palm wine from raffia or oil palm trees.|Let the palm wine ferment naturally for 2-3 days in a covered container until very sour and alcoholic.|Pour into a traditional clay or copper still.|Heat gently — the alcohol evaporates and condenses through the still's coil into a collection vessel.|Discard the first small amount (foreshots).|Collect the clear spirit that flows — this is sodabi, typically 40-60% alcohol.|Serve in small glasses neat, or infuse with local herbs and roots.

title: Koliko
description: Koliko are Beninese fried plantain chips — ripe plantains sliced thin and deep-fried until crispy, sold as a popular street snack.
ingredients: 4 ripe plantains|oil for deep frying|salt
instructions: Peel plantains and slice diagonally into thin chips.|Heat oil to 175°C (350°F).|Fry plantain slices in batches until golden and crispy, 3-4 minutes.|Drain on paper towels, sprinkle with salt.|Serve immediately as a snack.

title: Mashed Yam Benin
summary: Beninese pounded yam, smooth and stretchy
description: Mashed Yam (iyan) is a Beninese staple — boiled white yam pounded until smooth, stretchy and dough-like, served with soups and stews.
ingredients: 1 kg white yam, peeled and cubed|water for boiling|pinch of salt
instructions: Boil yam cubes in salted water until very tender, about 20 minutes.|Drain most of the water.|Pound in a mortar with a pestle until completely smooth and stretchy (or use a stand mixer).|Alternatively, mash with a heavy wooden spoon, adding splashes of hot water as needed.|Shape into a smooth mound.|Serve with any Beninese soup — egusi, gbegiri or palm nut.

title: Ema Datshi
summary: Bhutan's fiery national dish of chili peppers and cheese
description: Ema Datshi is Bhutan's beloved national dish — fresh green and red chilies stewed with local yak or cow cheese into a spicy, creamy stew.
ingredients: 10 large green chilies, halved lengthwise|4 red chilies, sliced|200g farmer cheese or feta, crumbled|1 onion, sliced|2 tomatoes, chopped|2 cloves garlic, sliced|2 tbsp butter|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Melt butter in a pot, add onions and garlic, cook 3 minutes.|Add green and red chilies, stir.|Add tomatoes and water, bring to a simmer.|Cook 15 minutes until chilies are tender.|Add crumbled cheese, stir gently until cheese melts into a creamy sauce.|Season with salt, serve hot with red rice.

title: Phaksha Paa
summary: Bhutanese pork with red dried chilies and radish
description: Phaksha Paa is strips of pork belly stir-fried with dried red chilies, daikon radish and Sichuan pepper — one of Bhutan's most popular meat dishes.
ingredients: 500g pork belly, sliced into strips|1 cup dried radish or fresh daikon, sliced|6 dried red chilies, soaked and sliced|1 onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, sliced|1 tbsp Sichuan peppercorns|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy pot, add pork strips and brown well.|Add onion and garlic, cook 3 minutes.|Add dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns, stir.|Add radish and water, bring to boil.|Reduce heat, simmer 30 minutes until pork is tender and sauce is reduced.|Season with salt, serve with red rice.

title: Jasha Maru
summary: Bhutanese spicy minced chicken with tomatoes and chilies
description: Jasha Maru is Bhutanese spiced chicken stew with tomatoes, ginger, chilies and fresh cilantro — simpler than ema datshi but equally flavorful.
ingredients: 500g chicken, cut into small pieces|3 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|3 green chilies, sliced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1-inch ginger, minced|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1/2 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Heat oil, add onion, garlic and ginger, sauté 3 minutes.|Add chicken pieces, brown lightly.|Add tomatoes, green chilies and water.|Simmer 20 minutes until chicken is cooked and sauce is thick.|Stir in cilantro, season with salt.|Serve with Bhutanese red rice.

title: Kewa Datshi
summary: Bhutanese potato and cheese stew with chilies
description: Kewa Datshi is a milder cousin of ema datshi — potatoes sliced and simmered with cheese and chilies into a comforting, creamy stew.
ingredients: 4 large potatoes, peeled and sliced thin|200g farmer cheese or feta, crumbled|3 green chilies, sliced|1 onion, sliced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 tbsp butter|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Melt butter, sauté onion and chilies 3 minutes.|Add potato slices, tomatoes and water.|Simmer 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender.|Add cheese, stir gently until melted and creamy.|Season with salt, serve with red rice.

title: Shakam Datshi
summary: Bhutanese dried beef with cheese and chili sauce
description: Shakam Datshi combines strips of Bhutanese dried beef (shakam) with the classic datshi cheese sauce and chilies — rich and intensely savory.
ingredients: 300g dried beef (or beef jerky), sliced thin|200g farmer cheese, crumbled|4 green chilies, sliced|1 onion, sliced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 tbsp butter|1.5 cups water|salt
instructions: Soak dried beef in warm water for 30 minutes to soften, drain.|Melt butter, sauté onion and chilies.|Add dried beef, stir-fry 5 minutes.|Add tomatoes and water, simmer 20 minutes until beef is tender.|Add cheese, stir until melted and creamy.|Season with salt, serve with red rice.

title: Hoentoe
summary: Bhutanese buckwheat dumplings with cheese and turnip filling
description: Hoentoe are Bhutanese dumplings from Haa Valley — buckwheat dough wrapped around a filling of cheese, turnip greens and chilies, steamed until tender.
ingredients: 2 cups buckwheat flour|3/4 cup hot water|For filling: 1 cup turnip greens, finely chopped|100g farmer cheese, crumbled|2 green chilies, minced|1 small onion, diced|1 tbsp butter|salt
instructions: Mix buckwheat flour with hot water, knead into a pliable dough, rest 15 minutes.|Sauté onion in butter, add turnip greens and chilies, cook until wilted.|Mix with crumbled cheese and salt.|Pinch off pieces of dough, flatten into circles, fill and fold into half-moon shapes.|Steam in a bamboo steamer for 15-20 minutes until dough is cooked.|Serve hot with chili sauce.

title: Suja Butter Tea
description: Suja is Bhutanese butter tea — strong tea churned with yak butter and salt into a rich, warming, savory drink essential for life at high altitude.
ingredients: 2 tbsp loose black tea or 4 tea bags|4 cups water|2 tbsp yak butter or regular butter|1/4 tsp salt|1/4 cup milk
instructions: Boil water and steep tea for 5 minutes, strain.|Pour hot tea into a traditional churn (or blender).|Add butter, salt and milk.|Churn or blend for 2-3 minutes until frothy and well mixed.|Serve immediately in small cups — it should be warm, salty and buttery.

title: Zow Shungo
summary: Bhutanese mixed vegetable and rice stew
description: Zow Shungo is a Bhutanese one-pot meal of leftover rice cooked with mixed vegetables, butter and cheese — the ultimate comfort food.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked red or white rice|2 cups mixed vegetables (spinach, turnips, radish, beans), chopped|100g cheese, cubed|2 tbsp butter|2 green chilies, sliced|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Melt butter in a pot, add chilies and vegetables.|Add water, cook 10 minutes until vegetables are tender.|Add cooked rice, stir to combine.|Add cheese cubes, stir until melted.|Cook 5 more minutes until everything is creamy and combined.|Season with salt, serve hot.

title: Puta Noodles
summary: Bhutanese buckwheat noodles served with sauce
description: Puta are traditional Bhutanese noodles pressed from buckwheat dough through a special mold, served with a chili-cheese sauce or stew.
ingredients: 2 cups buckwheat flour|1 cup water|1/2 tsp salt|For sauce: 100g cheese, crumbled|3 green chilies, sliced|1 tomato, diced|1 tbsp butter|1/2 cup water
instructions: Mix buckwheat flour, water and salt into a smooth dough.|Press through a noodle press or potato ricer to form noodles directly into boiling water.|Cook 3-4 minutes until noodles float and are tender, drain.|For sauce: melt butter, add chilies, tomato and water, simmer 5 minutes.|Add cheese, stir until melted.|Serve noodles topped with the cheese-chili sauce.

title: Red Rice Bhutan
summary: Bhutanese nutty red rice, the staple grain of Bhutan
description: Bhutanese red rice is a medium-grain rice with a distinctive nutty flavor and pink color, grown in the high-altitude Paro Valley — the base of every Bhutanese meal.
ingredients: 2 cups Bhutanese red rice|3 cups water|1 tsp salt|1 tbsp butter
instructions: Rinse rice until water runs mostly clear.|Bring water to a boil with salt.|Add rice, stir once, reduce to low heat.|Cover tightly and cook 20 minutes.|Remove from heat, let steam covered 10 minutes.|Fluff with a fork, stir in butter if desired.|Serve alongside any datshi dish.

title: Goep Tripe
summary: Bhutanese tripe stew with ginger and chilies
description: Goep is a Bhutanese tripe dish — beef stomach cleaned and simmered with ginger, garlic, chilies and Sichuan pepper until tender.
ingredients: 500g beef tripe, cleaned and sliced|3 green chilies, sliced|1-inch ginger, sliced|4 cloves garlic, sliced|1 onion, sliced|1/2 tsp Sichuan peppercorns|1 tbsp vegetable oil|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Boil tripe in water for 1 hour until tender, drain and slice into strips.|Heat oil, sauté onion, ginger and garlic.|Add chilies and Sichuan pepper, stir 1 minute.|Add tripe strips and water.|Simmer 20 minutes until flavors meld.|Season with salt, serve with red rice.

title: Momo Bhutan
summary: Bhutanese steamed dumplings with spicy dipping sauce
description: Bhutanese momos are steamed dumplings filled with seasoned minced beef or pork, served with a fiery ezay (chili sauce) for dipping.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|3/4 cup water|300g minced beef or pork|1 onion, finely diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1-inch ginger, grated|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|salt and pepper|For ezay: 5 dried red chilies, soaked|2 tomatoes|1 clove garlic|salt|1 tbsp oil
instructions: Make dough with flour, water and a pinch of salt, knead smooth, rest 20 minutes.|Mix meat with onion, garlic, ginger, cilantro, salt and pepper.|Roll dough thin, cut circles, fill and pleat closed.|Steam for 15 minutes.|Make ezay: blend soaked chilies, tomatoes, garlic, salt and oil into a rough sauce.|Serve steamed momos with ezay.

title: Tshoem
summary: Bhutanese mixed vegetable stew with cheese
description: Tshoem is a Bhutanese vegetable medley — mixed seasonal vegetables stewed with cheese sauce, a everyday side dish in Bhutanese homes.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (beans, potatoes, squash, radish), chopped|100g cheese, crumbled|2 green chilies, sliced|1 onion, sliced|1 tomato, diced|1 tbsp butter|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Melt butter, sauté onion 3 minutes.|Add vegetables, chilies, tomato and water.|Simmer 15-20 minutes until tender.|Add cheese, stir until melted.|Season with salt, serve with rice.

title: Thukpa Noodle Soup
summary: Bhutanese hearty noodle soup with meat and vegetables
description: Thukpa is a warming Bhutanese noodle soup with hand-pulled or store-bought noodles in a spiced meat and vegetable broth — a Himalayan cold-weather staple.
ingredients: 200g egg noodles|250g chicken or beef, sliced thin|1 onion, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1-inch ginger, minced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 green chilies, sliced|6 cups chicken broth|1 cup spinach or bok choy|2 tbsp vegetable oil|soy sauce|salt|cilantro and green onions for garnish
instructions: Heat oil, sauté onion, garlic and ginger until fragrant.|Add meat, brown lightly.|Add tomatoes and chilies, cook 3 minutes.|Pour in broth, bring to a boil.|Simmer 15 minutes until meat is cooked.|Add noodles and greens, cook 5 minutes.|Season with soy sauce and salt.|Serve in deep bowls with cilantro and green onions.

title: Sikam Paa
summary: Bhutanese dried pork belly stir-fried with chilies
description: Sikam Paa is Bhutanese dried pork belly stir-fried with dried red chilies until crispy — intensely savory and fiery, a beloved winter dish.
ingredients: 400g dried pork belly (or regular pork belly strips)|8 dried red chilies|1 onion, sliced|3 cloves garlic, sliced|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1/2 cup water|salt
instructions: If using dried pork: soak in warm water 20 minutes, drain and slice.|Heat oil, fry pork strips until crispy on edges.|Add onion, garlic and dried chilies, stir-fry 5 minutes.|Add water, simmer 10 minutes until sauce reduces.|Season with salt, serve with red rice.

title: Norsha Noodles
summary: Bhutanese stir-fried flat noodles with mushrooms
description: Norsha Noodles are Bhutanese wide flat noodles stir-fried with local mushrooms, chilies and onions — a simple but satisfying everyday dish.
ingredients: 300g wide flat rice noodles or hand-cut wheat noodles|200g fresh mushrooms (shiitake or button), sliced|2 green chilies, sliced|1 onion, sliced|2 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp vegetable oil|2 tbsp soy sauce|salt|fresh cilantro
instructions: Cook noodles according to package, drain.|Heat oil, stir-fry mushrooms until golden.|Add onion, garlic and chilies, cook 3 minutes.|Add cooked noodles and soy sauce, toss together.|Stir-fry 2-3 minutes until heated through.|Season with salt, garnish with cilantro.

title: Ngopa
summary: Bhutanese roasted and ground turnip dish
description: Ngopa is a traditional Bhutanese turnip dish — turnips are sliced, dried and roasted, then cooked into a stew with butter and cheese.
ingredients: 4 large turnips, peeled and sliced|2 tbsp butter|100g cheese, crumbled|2 green chilies, sliced|1 onion, sliced|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Roast or dry turnip slices until slightly dehydrated and browned.|Melt butter, sauté onion and chilies.|Add roasted turnips and water.|Simmer 15 minutes until turnips are very tender.|Add cheese, stir until melted.|Season with salt, serve with rice.

title: Datshi Soup
summary: Bhutanese simple cheese and chili broth
description: Datshi Soup is the simplest expression of Bhutanese cuisine — a thin, warming broth of cheese, chilies, butter and water, sipped alongside rice.
ingredients: 150g farmer cheese or feta, crumbled|4 green chilies, sliced|2 tbsp butter|3 cups water|1 small onion, sliced|salt
instructions: Bring water to a boil with butter and onion.|Add sliced chilies, simmer 5 minutes.|Add cheese, stir until melted into a thin, milky broth.|Season with salt.|Serve as a side soup with red rice — pour over the rice or sip alongside.

title: Ara Barley Spirit
summary: Bhutanese traditional distilled barley and rice spirit
description: Ara is Bhutan's traditional alcoholic drink — fermented from rice, barley, millet or wheat, then distilled into a clear spirit. It is central to Bhutanese celebrations.
ingredients: 2 kg barley or rice|yeast cake (phab)|water|traditional distillation setup
instructions: Cook barley or rice until very soft.|Cool to warm temperature, mix with crumbled yeast cake.|Place in a fermentation container, cover loosely.|Ferment in a warm place for 5-7 days until bubbly and sour-sweet.|Transfer to a traditional still or pot still.|Heat gently, collect the condensed spirit.|The resulting ara is typically 15-25% alcohol.|Serve at room temperature in small cups during celebrations.

title: Gondo Datshi
description: Gondo Datshi is a quick Bhutanese dish of eggs scrambled into the classic cheese and chili sauce — a protein-rich comfort food made in minutes.
ingredients: 4 eggs|150g farmer cheese, crumbled|3 green chilies, sliced|1 small onion, sliced|1 tomato, diced|1 tbsp butter|1/2 cup water|salt
instructions: Melt butter, sauté onion 2 minutes.|Add chilies and tomato, cook 3 minutes.|Add water, bring to simmer.|Add cheese, stir until melted.|Crack eggs directly into the simmering sauce.|Stir gently to scramble the eggs into the cheese sauce.|Season with salt, serve immediately with red rice.

title: Salteña
summary: Bolivian baked empanada with a juicy spiced meat filling
description: Salteñas are Bolivia's morning street food — golden baked pastries with a sweet-spiced dough containing a rich, juicy filling of meat, potatoes, peas and olives.
ingredients: For dough: 3 cups flour, 1/4 cup sugar, 1/4 cup lard, 1 egg, 1/2 cup warm water, 1/2 tsp salt, 1 tsp annatto powder|For filling: 400g beef, finely diced|2 potatoes, diced small|1/2 cup peas|1/4 cup olives, sliced|1 hard-boiled egg, chopped|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp aji amarillo paste|1 tbsp gelatin dissolved in 1 cup broth|1 tsp cumin|salt and pepper|1 egg for glaze
instructions: Cook beef with onion, aji, cumin, salt and pepper until done. Add potatoes and peas.|Stir in dissolved gelatin broth, add olives and egg. Refrigerate filling until it sets into a thick gel.|Make dough: mix flour, sugar, salt and annatto. Cut in lard, add egg and water, knead. Rest 30 min.|Roll dough into circles, place a generous spoonful of gelled filling on each.|Fold over, crimp edges into a rope pattern (repulgue), brush with egg.|Bake at 220°C (425°F) for 18-20 minutes until golden.|Eat immediately — hold upright to sip the hot broth inside.

title: Sopa de Maní
summary: Bolivian peanut soup with pasta and beef
description: Sopa de Maní is Bolivia's famous peanut soup — a creamy broth flavored with ground peanuts, beef, vegetables and small pasta, topped with fried potatoes.
ingredients: 1 cup roasted peanuts, ground|500g beef ribs or stew meat|1 onion, diced|1 carrot, diced|1 stalk celery, diced|2 potatoes, cubed|1 cup small pasta (fideo)|1 tsp cumin|1/2 tsp oregano|6 cups beef broth|2 tbsp oil|salt and pepper|french fries for topping|fresh parsley
instructions: Brown beef in oil, add onion, carrot and celery, cook 5 minutes.|Add cumin, oregano, broth. Simmer 45 minutes until beef is tender.|Remove beef, shred it, return to pot.|Add ground peanuts, stir until dissolved and soup is creamy.|Add potatoes and pasta, cook 15 minutes.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve topped with french fries and parsley.

title: Api Morado
summary: Bolivian warm purple corn drink with cinnamon and cloves
description: Api Morado is a thick, sweet Bolivian breakfast drink made from ground purple corn simmered with cinnamon, cloves and sugar.
ingredients: 2 cups dried purple corn kernels, ground|6 cups water|1 cup sugar|2 cinnamon sticks|4 whole cloves|1 strip orange zest|1 tbsp cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp water
instructions: Soak ground purple corn in water overnight.|Transfer to a pot with cinnamon, cloves and orange zest.|Bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer 45 minutes.|Strain through a fine sieve, return liquid to the pot.|Add sugar, stir to dissolve.|Add cornstarch mixture, stir until thickened slightly.|Serve hot in mugs, traditionally with pasteles or buñuelos.

title: Fricasé
summary: Bolivian spicy pork stew with hominy and aji
description: Fricasé is a Bolivian weekend-morning stew of pork ribs simmered with yellow aji, cumin and hominy corn — a hearty hangover cure.
ingredients: 1 kg pork ribs, cut into pieces|1 cup dried hominy corn, soaked overnight|3 tbsp aji amarillo paste|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp cumin|1/2 tsp oregano|6 cups water|2 tbsp oil|salt|fresh parsley and chuño (freeze-dried potato, optional)
instructions: Boil soaked hominy in water until tender, about 1.5 hours.|In a separate pot, brown pork in oil.|Add onion, garlic, cumin and oregano, cook 5 minutes.|Add aji amarillo paste, stir well.|Add water, bring to a boil, simmer 1 hour until pork is very tender.|Add cooked hominy to the pork stew.|Season with salt, serve hot garnished with parsley.

title: Llajwa Sauce
summary: Bolivian fresh tomato and locoto pepper salsa
description: Llajwa is Bolivia's essential table condiment — fresh tomatoes ground with fiery locoto peppers in a stone mortar, present at every Bolivian meal.
ingredients: 3 ripe tomatoes|2 locoto peppers (or habaneros)|1 tbsp fresh quilquiña (or cilantro)|1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Roughly chop tomatoes and locoto peppers.|Grind together in a stone mortar (batán) or pulse briefly in a food processor.|The texture should be chunky, not smooth.|Stir in chopped herbs and salt.|Serve immediately as a condiment — it's best fresh.

title: Pique Macho
summary: Bolivian platter of beef, sausage, fries, eggs and chili
description: Pique Macho is Bolivia's ultimate sharing platter — a mountain of cubed beef, sliced sausage, french fries, boiled eggs, tomatoes and locoto peppers.
ingredients: 400g beef tenderloin, cubed|2 beef sausages, sliced|3 cups french fries, fried|2 hard-boiled eggs, quartered|2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, sliced into rings|2 locoto or jalapeño peppers, sliced|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp cumin|salt and pepper|fresh parsley
instructions: Season beef cubes with cumin, salt and pepper. Sear in very hot oil until browned.|Add sausage slices, cook until browned.|Add onion rings and peppers, toss briefly.|Arrange french fries on a large platter.|Pile the beef, sausage and onion mixture on top.|Scatter tomatoes, egg quarters and more pepper slices.|Garnish with parsley, serve family-style with llajwa.

title: Silpancho
summary: Bolivian pounded beef cutlet over rice with fried egg
description: Silpancho is a Cochabamba specialty — a thin, breaded beef cutlet served on a bed of rice with a fried egg on top and a fresh tomato-onion salad.
ingredients: 4 thin beef steaks, pounded very thin|1 cup breadcrumbs|2 eggs, beaten|salt, pepper and cumin|oil for frying|4 cups cooked rice|4 fried eggs|For salad: 2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|1 locoto pepper, minced|1 tbsp chopped parsley|salt and lime juice
instructions: Season pounded steaks with salt, pepper and cumin.|Dip in beaten egg, then coat in breadcrumbs.|Fry in oil until golden and crispy on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.|Make salsa criolla: mix tomatoes, onion, locoto, parsley, salt and lime juice.|On each plate: mound rice, place cutlet on top, top with a fried egg.|Spoon salsa alongside, serve immediately.

title: Chairo Soup
summary: Bolivian Andean soup with chuño, lamb and vegetables
description: Chairo is a thick Bolivian highland soup combining freeze-dried potatoes (chuño), fresh vegetables, wheat, lamb and herbs — deeply nourishing at altitude.
ingredients: 300g lamb, cubed|1 cup chuño (freeze-dried potato), soaked overnight|1 cup wheat berries, soaked|2 potatoes, cubed|1 carrot, diced|1/2 cup peas|1/2 cup fava beans|1 onion, diced|1 tsp cumin|1/2 tsp oregano|6 cups water|salt|fresh parsley and mint
instructions: Simmer lamb in water for 30 minutes, skim foam.|Add soaked wheat and chuño, cook 30 more minutes.|Add potatoes, carrot, peas, fava beans and onion.|Season with cumin, oregano and salt.|Simmer 20 more minutes until everything is very tender.|Serve garnished with fresh parsley and mint.

title: Falso Conejo
summary: Bolivian breaded beef in spicy peanut aji sauce
description: Falso Conejo (false rabbit) is thin breaded beef served in a spicy yellow aji and peanut sauce — so named because it resembles traditional rabbit preparations.
ingredients: 4 thin beef steaks|1 cup breadcrumbs|2 eggs, beaten|oil for frying|For sauce: 2 tbsp aji amarillo paste|1/4 cup ground peanuts|1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, blended|1 tsp cumin|1/2 cup peas|1 cup broth|2 tbsp oil|salt|boiled potatoes for serving
instructions: Bread and fry steaks as for silpancho.|For sauce: sauté onion, add aji paste, cumin, blended tomatoes and ground peanuts.|Add broth and peas, simmer 15 minutes until thick.|Place fried steaks on a plate with boiled potatoes.|Ladle the peanut aji sauce over the steaks.|Serve with rice.

title: Majao
summary: Bolivian eastern-lowland rice with dried beef and plantain
description: Majao is a dish from eastern Bolivia — seasoned rice cooked with charque (dried beef), ripe plantains and fried eggs, full of smoky, savory flavor.
ingredients: 2 cups rice|200g charque (dried beef) or beef jerky|2 ripe plantains, sliced|4 eggs|1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 bell pepper, diced|1 tsp cumin|1/2 tsp annatto/achiote|3 cups water|3 tbsp oil|salt
instructions: Shred dried beef, fry in oil until crispy. Remove.|In the same oil, sauté onion, tomatoes and bell pepper with cumin and annatto.|Add rice, stir to coat, add water and salt.|Cook rice covered on low heat 18 minutes.|Fry plantain slices until golden. Fry eggs sunny-side up.|Fluff rice, mix in the crispy dried beef.|Serve topped with fried plantains and a fried egg.

title: Chicharrón Bolivia
summary: Bolivian deep-fried pork ribs with hominy and llajwa
description: Bolivian Chicharrón is pork ribs boiled in their own fat with water, beer and spices until the water evaporates and the pork fries in its own rendered fat — incredibly crispy.
ingredients: 1.5 kg pork ribs, cut into pieces|1 cup beer|1 cup water|1 tbsp cumin|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 onion, quartered|salt|cooked hominy (mote) for serving|llajwa sauce
instructions: Place pork ribs in a wide heavy pot with beer, water, cumin, garlic, onion and salt.|Bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered.|As water and beer evaporate, the pork begins to fry in its own rendered fat.|Continue cooking, turning occasionally, until all liquid is gone and pork is deep golden and crispy (about 1.5 hours total).|Drain on paper towels.|Serve with boiled hominy and llajwa sauce.

title: Sajta de Pollo
summary: Bolivian chicken stew in yellow aji sauce with chuño
description: Sajta de Pollo is Bolivian chicken pieces simmered in a thick yellow aji pepper sauce, served over boiled chuño and potatoes.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces|3 tbsp aji amarillo paste|1 onion, diced|3 tomatoes, blended|1 tsp cumin|4 potatoes, boiled|2 cups chuño, soaked and boiled|2 tbsp oil|2 cups chicken broth|salt|fresh parsley
instructions: Season chicken with cumin and salt, brown in oil.|Add onion, cook until soft, add aji paste and blended tomatoes.|Add broth, bring to simmer, cook 30 minutes until chicken is done.|Sauce should be thick and bright yellow.|Arrange boiled potatoes and chuño on plates, place chicken on top.|Ladle sauce over everything, garnish with parsley.

title: Ají de Fideo
summary: Bolivian spicy noodle soup with ground beef and potatoes
