instructions: Brown chicken, season and set aside.|Fry onion, add blended tomatoes and paste, cook 20 minutes until reduced.|Add broth, thyme, bay leaves and scotch bonnet, bring to boil.|Add rice, stir once, place chicken on top.|Cover tightly, cook on low heat 20-25 minutes.|Fluff rice, discard bay leaves, serve.

title: Fufu Cameroon
description: Cameroonian fufu is made by boiling and pounding cocoyam (or cassava and plantain) into a smooth, stretchy dough eaten by pinching off pieces to scoop up soup.
ingredients: 1 kg cocoyam (taro) or mix of cassava and plantain|water for boiling
instructions: Peel and boil cocoyam until very tender, about 25 minutes.|Drain, place in a mortar.|Pound with a pestle until completely smooth and stretchy with no lumps.|Alternatively, use a heavy wooden spoon in a pot, mashing vigorously.|Shape into smooth balls, serve with any Cameroonian soup.

title: Pepper Soup Cameroon
summary: Cameroonian spicy broth with goat meat and aromatic spices
description: Cameroon Pepper Soup is a fiery, aromatic broth loaded with goat meat (or fish), flavored with alligator pepper, njangsa and ehuru seeds — deeply warming.
ingredients: 500g goat meat, cubed|1 onion, diced|2 scotch bonnet peppers|1 tbsp pepper soup spice mix (ehuru, njangsa, alligator pepper)|1-inch ginger, sliced|3 cloves garlic|6 cups water|salt|fresh scent leaves or basil
instructions: Boil goat meat with onion, ginger and garlic until tender, about 45 minutes.|Add scotch bonnet peppers and pepper soup spice mix.|Simmer 15 minutes for flavors to meld.|Season with salt.|Serve hot in bowls garnished with scent leaves or basil.

title: Plantain Porridge Cameroon
summary: Cameroonian ripe plantain porridge with palm oil
description: Plantain Porridge is a thick Cameroonian stew of ripe plantains cooked until they break down into a sweet, thick porridge with palm oil and crayfish.
ingredients: 4 ripe plantains, peeled and sliced|2 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|1 scotch bonnet pepper|1 tbsp ground crayfish|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion until soft.|Add plantain slices, water and scotch bonnet.|Cook 20-25 minutes until plantains are very soft and breaking down.|Mash some pieces to thicken the porridge.|Add ground crayfish, season with salt.|Serve hot as a main dish.

title: Sangah
summary: Cameroonian corn chaff with beans and palm oil
description: Sangah (corn chaff) is broken corn kernels cooked with beans, palm oil and greens — a traditional staple of western Cameroon highland communities.
ingredients: 2 cups broken corn kernels|1 cup beans, soaked|3 tbsp palm oil|1 cup pumpkin leaves or spinach|salt|6 cups water
instructions: Boil corn and beans together 1.5-2 hours until tender.|Add palm oil and greens.|Cook 10 more minutes.|Season with salt, serve hot.

title: Nkui
description: Nkui is a traditional Bamiléké soup — thick, slimy and made from a special bark that gives it a mucilaginous texture, cooked with meat and spices.
ingredients: 200g nkui bark or powder (or okra as substitute)|500g beef or goat, cubed|2 cups greens (huckleberry)|1 onion, diced|1 scotch bonnet pepper|1 tbsp ground crayfish|4 cups water|salt|palm oil
instructions: Boil meat until tender, about 40 minutes.|Add nkui bark (or sliced okra), stir — it will thicken into a slimy soup.|Add greens, onion, scotch bonnet and crayfish.|Simmer 15 minutes, add palm oil.|Season with salt.|Serve with fufu — the soup should be thick and slimy.

title: Achu Soup
summary: Cameroonian yellow palm oil soup served with taro
description: Achu Soup is a bright yellow soup from the Northwest region — made by emulsifying palm oil with a local limestone paste (kanwa), served with pounded taro.
ingredients: 1 cup red palm oil|1 tbsp kanwa (limestone) or baking soda|500g cow skin or tripe, boiled|2 tbsp ground pepper soup spices|salt|pounded taro for serving
instructions: Warm palm oil without heating too much.|Dissolve kanwa in a little water, add to the oil.|Stir vigorously — the oil will emulsify into a bright yellow sauce.|Add pepper soup spices and salt.|Add pieces of cow skin or tripe.|Simmer gently 10 minutes.|Serve in a bowl alongside pounded taro — dip the taro in the yellow soup.

title: Njangsa Stew
description: Njangsa is a wild seed used to thicken and flavor stews in Cameroon — ground into a paste, it gives a rich, slightly bitter and nutty character.
ingredients: 1/4 cup njangsa seeds (or pumpkin seeds as substitute), ground|500g beef or goat, cubed|2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp palm oil|1 scotch bonnet pepper|4 cups water|salt
instructions: Brown meat in palm oil, add onion, cook 5 minutes.|Add tomatoes, cook 10 minutes.|Add water, simmer 40 minutes until meat is tender.|Add ground njangsa seeds, stir until sauce thickens.|Add scotch bonnet, cook 10 more minutes.|Season with salt, serve with rice or fufu.

title: Okok
summary: Cameroonian gnetum leaf stew with ground peanuts
description: Okok (Eru leaves cooked differently) is a central Cameroonian stew of shredded gnetum leaves cooked with ground peanuts, palm oil and smoked fish.
ingredients: 300g gnetum (okok) leaves, finely shredded|1/2 cup ground peanuts|200g smoked fish|3 tbsp palm oil|1 scotch bonnet pepper|1/4 cup ground crayfish|salt|1 cup water
instructions: Wash shredded leaves well.|Heat palm oil, add ground peanuts, stir until mixed.|Add smoked fish, crayfish and scotch bonnet.|Add water, bring to simmer.|Add shredded leaves, cook 20-25 minutes on low heat.|Season with salt, serve with bobolo or plantains.

title: Baton de Manioc
description: Baton de Manioc (also called miondo) are rolls of fermented cassava dough steamed in banana leaves — the standard Cameroonian accompaniment to ndolé and soups.
ingredients: 1 kg cassava, peeled|water for soaking|banana leaves
instructions: Soak cassava in water 3-4 days until fermented and soft.|Remove fibers, mash into smooth paste.|Shape into sticks, wrap in banana leaves, tie securely.|Steam 45 minutes until firm.|Peel off banana leaf and eat with any sauce or soup.

title: Beignets Cameroun
description: Beignets (puff-puff) are Cameroon's favorite street snack — sweet fried dough balls, golden and fluffy, sold at every corner.
ingredients: 3 cups flour|1/3 cup sugar|1 tsp dry yeast|1 cup warm water|pinch of salt|1/2 tsp nutmeg|oil for deep frying
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, yeast, salt and nutmeg.|Add warm water, mix into a thick batter (not dough).|Cover and let rise 1-2 hours until doubled and bubbly.|Heat oil to 170°C (340°F).|Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot oil.|Fry 3-4 minutes, turning, until golden brown all over.|Drain on paper towels, serve warm.

title: Mboko
summary: Cameroonian palm wine fresh from the tree
description: Mboko is fresh palm wine tapped directly from raffia or oil palm trees in Cameroon — sweet, milky and mildly alcoholic, a daily social drink.
ingredients: palm wine tapped from raffia or oil palm tree
instructions: Make an incision in the crown of a mature raffia palm or oil palm.|Attach a calabash or container to collect the sap that flows.|Collect the palm wine after 6-12 hours.|Strain through a clean cloth to remove debris.|Drink fresh and sweet within a few hours.|If left longer, it ferments and becomes more alcoholic and sour.|Best enjoyed communally from a shared calabash.

title: Fufu Congo
description: Congolese Fufu is made from cassava flour cooked into a smooth, stretchy dough — the staple starch that accompanies every Congolese meal.
ingredients: 2 cups cassava flour|3 cups water|pinch of salt
instructions: Bring water to a boil.|Gradually add cassava flour, stirring vigorously with a wooden spoon.|Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly, 10-15 minutes until thick, smooth and pulling from pot.|Wet your hands and shape into a smooth ball.|Serve with pondu, moamba or any Congolese sauce.

title: Poulet Moamba
summary: Congolese chicken stewed in palm butter sauce
description: Poulet Moamba is the national dish of Congo — chicken simmered in a rich, earthy palm butter (moamba) sauce with garlic, tomatoes and okra.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces|1 cup palm butter (moamba)|3 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 scotch bonnet pepper|6 okra pods, sliced|juice of 1 lemon|2 tbsp oil|salt|4 cups water
instructions: Season chicken with lemon juice and salt, brown in oil.|Add onion and garlic, cook 3 minutes.|Add tomatoes, cook 5 minutes.|Add palm butter and water, stir well.|Add scotch bonnet pepper, bring to a simmer.|Cook 40 minutes until chicken is tender.|Add okra, cook 10 more minutes.|Season with salt, serve with fufu.

title: Saka Saka
summary: Congolese pounded cassava leaves with palm oil
description: Saka Saka (Pondu) is the everyday green dish of Congo — cassava leaves pounded and slow-cooked with palm oil, onion and dried fish until thick and rich.
ingredients: 500g cassava leaves, pounded (or frozen)|3 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|200g dried or smoked fish|1 eggplant, cubed (optional)|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Boil pounded cassava leaves in water for 30 minutes, drain.|Heat palm oil, sauté onion until golden.|Add dried fish and eggplant, cook 5 minutes.|Add cassava leaves and water.|Simmer on low heat for 30-45 minutes until very thick.|Season with salt, serve with fufu or rice.

title: Liboké
summary: Congolese fish steamed in banana leaf packets
description: Liboké is a Congolese method of cooking fish — whole fish is seasoned, wrapped in banana leaves with onion and tomato, then steamed over coals.
ingredients: 4 whole tilapia or other fish, cleaned|2 onions, sliced|2 tomatoes, sliced|1 scotch bonnet pepper, sliced|juice of 2 limes|2 tbsp palm oil|salt and pepper|banana leaves for wrapping
instructions: Season fish with lime juice, salt and pepper.|Soften banana leaves over a flame.|Place each fish on a banana leaf with onion slices, tomato slices, pepper and a drizzle of palm oil.|Wrap securely, tie with string.|Steam over a pot of boiling water for 30-40 minutes, or grill over low charcoal.|Unwrap at the table, the fish will be tender and infused with flavors.

title: Pondu
description: Pondu is the same as saka saka — Congo's most eaten dish of pounded cassava leaves, slowly stewed until dark and deeply flavored.
ingredients: 500g cassava leaves, pounded|3 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|100g smoked fish|1 tbsp ground peanuts|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Boil cassava leaves 30 minutes, drain.|Heat palm oil, cook onion until soft.|Add smoked fish and ground peanuts.|Add cassava leaves and water.|Simmer 45 minutes on low heat, stirring occasionally.|Season with salt, serve with fufu.

title: Makemba
description: Makemba are Congolese fried plantains — ripe plantains sliced and fried in palm oil until sweet and caramelized, served as a side with everything.
ingredients: 4 ripe plantains, peeled and sliced diagonally|palm oil or vegetable oil for frying|salt
instructions: Heat oil in a skillet.|Fry plantain slices 2-3 minutes per side until golden and caramelized.|Drain on paper towels, season with a pinch of salt.|Serve warm as a side dish.

title: Mfumbwa Stew
summary: Congolese wild spinach stew with peanut butter
description: Mfumbwa is a Congolese stew of wild spinach-like leaves cooked with peanut butter, palm oil and dried shrimp — thick, nutty and nutritious.
ingredients: 500g mfumbwa leaves or spinach, chopped|3 tbsp peanut butter|2 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|100g dried shrimp|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion until soft.|Add dried shrimp, cook 3 minutes.|Add chopped leaves, stir.|Dissolve peanut butter in water, add to the pot.|Simmer 20-25 minutes until leaves are tender and sauce is thick.|Season with salt, serve with fufu or rice.

title: Maboke Fish
summary: Congolese fish baked in leaves over fire
description: Maboke is the Lingala name for liboké — fish wrapped in leaves and cooked directly over hot coals, infusing it with smoky, herbal flavors.
ingredients: 4 fish fillets or small whole fish|1 onion, sliced|1 tomato, sliced|2 cloves garlic, sliced|1 scotch bonnet pepper|juice of 1 lime|2 tbsp palm oil|salt|banana or taro leaves
instructions: Season fish with lime, salt, garlic and palm oil.|Layer onion, tomato and pepper on fish.|Wrap in leaves, tie with string.|Place on a grill over medium coals, cook 20 minutes per side.|The leaves will char but protect the fish inside.|Unwrap and serve.

title: Kwanga
description: Kwanga is a Congolese bread made from fermented cassava — wrapped in leaves and steamed, it has a sour tang and dense, chewy texture.
ingredients: 1 kg cassava, peeled|water for soaking|banana leaves for wrapping
instructions: Soak cassava pieces in water for 3-5 days until very soft and fermented.|Remove fibrous core, mash into a smooth paste.|Shape into elongated loaves.|Wrap tightly in banana leaves.|Steam for 45-60 minutes until firm.|Slice and serve with stews — it keeps for several days.

title: Bush Mango Soup
description: Bush mango (dika nut) soup uses the seeds of wild African mangoes, ground into a thickener that gives a rich, slimy, nutty soup — uniquely Congolese.
ingredients: 1/4 cup ground dika nut (ogbono/bush mango seeds)|500g goat or beef, cubed|200g smoked fish|1 onion, diced|2 scotch bonnet peppers|3 tbsp palm oil|4 cups water|salt
instructions: Boil meat until tender, about 40 minutes.|Add palm oil, onion and peppers, cook 10 minutes.|Add ground dika nut, stir well — it will thicken into a slimy, rich soup.|Add smoked fish, simmer 15 minutes.|Season with salt, serve with fufu.

title: Fumbwa
summary: Congolese fumbwa leaf stew with palm oil and fish
description: Fumbwa is a Congolese stew of wild fumbwa leaves (similar to wild spinach) cooked with palm oil, peanuts and dried fish.
ingredients: 500g fumbwa leaves or spinach|3 tbsp palm oil|1/4 cup ground peanuts|200g dried fish|1 onion, diced|1 chili pepper|2 cups water|salt
instructions: Chop fumbwa leaves finely.|Heat palm oil, sauté onion until soft.|Add dried fish and chili, cook 5 minutes.|Add ground peanuts and water, stir.|Add fumbwa leaves, simmer 25 minutes until tender.|Season with salt, serve with fufu or kwanga.

title: Brazaville Brochette
summary: Congolese grilled meat skewers with pepper sauce
description: Brochettes are Congo-Brazzaville's street food — seasoned goat or beef grilled on skewers over charcoal, served with a fiery pepper dipping sauce.
ingredients: 500g goat or beef, cubed|2 tbsp oil|1 tsp salt|1 tsp pepper|1 bouillon cube, crumbled|skewers|For pepper sauce: 5 scotch bonnet peppers, 2 tomatoes, 1 onion, salt, oil
instructions: Season meat with oil, salt, pepper and crumbled bouillon.|Thread onto skewers.|Grill over hot charcoal 8-10 minutes, turning.|Make pepper sauce: blend peppers, tomatoes and onion, fry in oil 10 min.|Serve skewers with pepper sauce on the side.

title: Cassava Leaf Stew Congo
description: The everyday Congolese cassava leaf stew — leaves pounded until fine and slow-cooked in palm oil, the most commonly eaten dish in the country.
ingredients: 500g cassava leaves, pounded|3 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|1 clove garlic|salt|3 cups water
instructions: Boil pounded leaves 30 minutes, drain.|Heat palm oil, sauté onion and garlic.|Add leaves and water.|Simmer 40 minutes on low until thick and dark.|Season with salt, serve with fufu.

title: Ngai Ngai Soup
description: Ngai Ngai is a Congolese soup made from tart sorrel leaves, cooked with fish and palm oil — the sourness makes it a refreshing contrast to heavy starches.
ingredients: 300g sorrel leaves (ngai ngai), chopped|200g smoked fish|2 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|1 chili pepper|3 cups water|salt
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion until soft.|Add water, bring to boil.|Add sorrel leaves and smoked fish.|Simmer 15-20 minutes.|Add chili pepper, season with salt.|The soup should be tangy and sour — serve with fufu.

title: Red Palm Soup Congo
summary: Congolese simple palm oil soup with fish
description: A basic Congolese soup where palm oil is the star — emulsified into water with fish, onion and pepper to make a rich, orange-red broth.
ingredients: 1/2 cup red palm oil|300g fish, cut into pieces|1 onion, diced|1 scotch bonnet pepper|4 cups water|salt|1 tbsp ground crayfish
instructions: Bring water to a boil.|Add palm oil, fish, onion, pepper and crayfish.|Simmer 20 minutes until fish is cooked and oil is distributed.|Season with salt, serve with fufu or rice.

title: Grilled Tilapia Congo
summary: Congolese whole grilled tilapia with lemon and pepper
description: Grilled tilapia is a Congolese riverside specialty — whole fish scored, seasoned with lemon, salt and pepper, grilled over wood coals until smoky.
ingredients: 4 whole tilapia, cleaned and scored|juice of 3 limes|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp chili powder|2 tbsp oil
instructions: Rub fish with lime juice, salt, pepper, chili and oil.|Marinate 30 minutes.|Grill over medium coals 6-8 minutes per side until skin is charred and fish flakes.|Serve with fried plantains and pepper sauce.

title: Mushroom Stew Congo
description: Congo's vast forests provide many edible wild mushrooms — this stew cooks them simply with tomatoes, onion and palm oil.
ingredients: 500g mixed mushrooms, sliced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp palm oil|1 clove garlic, minced|salt and pepper|fresh basil or parsley
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion and garlic until soft.|Add mushrooms, cook 10 minutes until they release their liquid.|Add tomatoes, cook 5 more minutes.|Season with salt and pepper.|Garnish with basil, serve with fufu or rice.

title: Diri ak Pois
description: Rice and beans cooked together — a simple, filling Congolese everyday meal combining rice with red or kidney beans in a lightly seasoned broth.
ingredients: 2 cups rice|1 cup red beans, soaked and pre-cooked|1 onion, diced|2 tbsp oil|3 cups water|salt|1 bouillon cube
instructions: Sauté onion in oil until soft.|Add pre-cooked beans and water.|Add rice, bouillon cube and salt.|Cover, cook on low heat 20 minutes until rice is done.|Fluff with fork, serve.

title: Maize Porridge Congo
description: Maize porridge is a Congolese breakfast and evening staple — fine cornmeal cooked into a smooth, thick porridge, eaten with stew or sweetened with sugar.
ingredients: 2 cups fine cornmeal|4 cups water|salt|sugar or stew for serving
instructions: Bring 3 cups water to a boil.|Mix cornmeal with remaining cold water.|Pour into boiling water, stir vigorously.|Cook 15 minutes, stirring, until thick.|Serve with sugar and milk for breakfast, or with stew for dinner.

title: Nsaka Mal
summary: Congolese vegetable stew with peanut butter
description: Nsaka Mal is a traditional Congolese vegetable stew thickened with peanut butter, combining whatever seasonal vegetables are available.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (eggplant, squash, greens), chopped|3 tbsp peanut butter|2 tbsp palm oil|1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 cup water|salt
instructions: Heat palm oil, sauté onion until soft.|Add tomatoes, cook 5 minutes.|Add vegetables and water, simmer 15 minutes.|Dissolve peanut butter in a little water, add to the stew.|Cook 10 more minutes until thick.|Season with salt, serve with fufu or rice.

title: Gallo Pinto
summary: Costa Rican breakfast rice and beans with Salsa Lizano
description: Gallo Pinto is Costa Rica's iconic breakfast — leftover rice stir-fried with black beans, onion and the quintessential Salsa Lizano for that distinctive sweet-tangy flavor.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white rice|1 can black beans, drained (reserve liquid)|1 small onion, diced|1 bell pepper, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp Salsa Lizano (or Worcestershire sauce)|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|salt and pepper
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet, sauté onion, pepper and garlic until soft.|Add black beans with a splash of their liquid, cook 3 minutes.|Add rice, stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes until rice starts to get slightly crispy.|Add Salsa Lizano, toss well.|Season with salt and pepper, mix in cilantro.|Serve with scrambled eggs, sour cream, fried plantains and a tortilla.

title: Casado
summary: Costa Rican lunch plate with rice, beans, salad and protein
description: Casado (meaning "married man's lunch") is a complete Costa Rican meal — rice, black beans, salad, fried plantains and your choice of meat all on one plate.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked rice|1 cup black beans, cooked|4 pieces chicken, fish or beef|2 ripe plantains, sliced and fried|lettuce and tomato salad|Salsa Lizano|salt
instructions: Prepare rice and beans separately (beans can be soupy or refried).|Cook protein of choice: grill chicken, pan-fry fish, or sear beef.|Fry plantain slices until golden.|Make a simple salad of lettuce, tomato and lime dressing.|Arrange everything on a large plate: rice on one side, beans next to it, protein, plantains and salad.|Serve with Salsa Lizano on the table.

title: Sopa Negra Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican black bean soup with egg
description: Sopa Negra is a simple Costa Rican black bean soup — puréed beans in a savory broth with cilantro, served with a poached or hard-boiled egg in each bowl.
ingredients: 2 cups dried black beans, soaked overnight|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 bell pepper, diced|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|1 tsp cumin|1 tbsp Salsa Lizano|6 cups water|salt|4 eggs (hard-boiled or poached)|sour cream
instructions: Boil soaked beans until very tender, about 1 hour.|Blend half the beans, return to pot for a thick/chunky texture.|Sauté onion, garlic and bell pepper, add to soup.|Season with cumin, Salsa Lizano and salt.|Simmer 10 minutes.|Stir in cilantro.|Serve each bowl with a boiled or poached egg and a swirl of sour cream.

title: Olla de Carne
summary: Costa Rican beef and root vegetable soup
description: Olla de Carne is Costa Rica's hearty beef soup loaded with yuca, chayote, corn, plantains, potatoes and other root vegetables — a Sunday family meal tradition.
ingredients: 500g beef short ribs|2 ears corn, cut into rounds|2 potatoes, quartered|1 cup yuca, peeled and cubed|1 chayote, cubed|1 green plantain, peeled and sliced|2 carrots, sliced|1 onion, quartered|2 cloves garlic|1/4 cup cilantro, chopped|8 cups water|salt
instructions: Place beef in a large pot with water, onion and garlic, bring to boil, skim foam.|Simmer 45 minutes until beef is starting to get tender.|Add corn, yuca and potatoes, cook 15 minutes.|Add chayote, plantain and carrots, cook 15 more minutes.|Season with salt and cilantro.|Serve in big bowls with all the vegetables and broth.

title: Tamales Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican Christmas tamales wrapped in banana leaves
description: Costa Rican tamales are a Christmas tradition — masa dough filled with seasoned pork, rice, vegetables and olives, wrapped in banana leaves and steamed.
ingredients: 4 cups masa harina|1 cup chicken broth|1/4 cup lard|500g pork shoulder, braised and shredded|1 cup cooked rice|1 carrot, diced and cooked|1/4 cup green peas|8 olives|1/4 cup raisins|banana leaves for wrapping|string
instructions: Mix masa with broth, lard and salt into a spreadable dough.|Season shredded pork with achiote, salt and pepper.|Soften banana leaves over a flame.|Spread masa on a banana leaf, add pork, rice, carrot, peas, an olive and a few raisins.|Fold banana leaf into a neat packet, tie with string.|Steam for 1.5-2 hours.|Unwrap and serve — traditionally eaten Christmas Eve.

title: Chifrijo
summary: Costa Rican bar snack of rice, beans, pork rinds and pico de gallo
description: Chifrijo is Costa Rica's beloved bar food — a bowl of rice and beans topped with crispy chicharrones (pork rinds), pico de gallo and avocado, served with tortilla chips.
ingredients: 1 cup cooked rice|1 cup cooked black beans with broth|200g chicharrones (pork rinds)|For pico de gallo: 2 tomatoes diced, 1/2 onion diced, 1 jalapeño minced, juice of 1 lime, cilantro, salt|1 avocado, sliced|tortilla chips
instructions: Layer rice in the bottom of a bowl.|Ladle beans with broth over the rice.|Top with chicharrones broken into chunks.|Spoon pico de gallo over everything.|Add avocado slices.|Serve with tortilla chips for scooping.

title: Patacones Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican twice-fried smashed green plantains
description: Patacones are green plantains sliced, fried once, smashed flat, then fried again until crispy — Costa Rica's most popular side dish.
ingredients: 3 green plantains|oil for frying|salt|lime wedge
instructions: Peel plantains, cut into 2-inch rounds.|Fry in 170°C (340°F) oil for 3 minutes until softened but not browned.|Remove, smash each piece flat with a tostonera or plate.|Return to hot oil (now 180°C/360°F), fry 2-3 minutes until golden and crispy.|Drain on paper towels, season with salt.|Serve hot with beans, guacamole or ceviche.

title: Arroz con Leche Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican creamy cinnamon rice pudding
description: Arroz con Leche is Costa Rica's classic dessert — rice slow-cooked in milk with cinnamon, vanilla and condensed milk until thick and creamy.
ingredients: 1 cup short-grain rice|4 cups whole milk|1 can sweetened condensed milk|2 cinnamon sticks|1 strip lemon zest|1 tsp vanilla extract|pinch of salt|ground cinnamon for garnish
instructions: Rinse rice, par-boil in water for 5 minutes, drain.|Heat milk with cinnamon sticks and lemon zest in a heavy pot.|Add par-boiled rice, reduce to low heat.|Cook 30-35 minutes, stirring frequently, until rice is very soft.|Stir in condensed milk and vanilla.|Cook 5 more minutes until desired thickness.|Serve warm or cold, dusted with ground cinnamon.

title: Tres Leches Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican three-milk soaked sponge cake
description: Tres Leches is a beloved Costa Rican celebration cake — light sponge soaked in a mixture of evaporated milk, condensed milk and heavy cream, topped with whipped cream.
ingredients: 5 eggs|1 cup sugar|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 tsp baking powder|1/3 cup milk|1 tsp vanilla|1 can evaporated milk|1 can sweetened condensed milk|1 cup heavy cream|For topping: 2 cups heavy cream whipped with 3 tbsp sugar|cinnamon
instructions: Beat eggs and sugar until thick and pale.|Fold in flour and baking powder, then milk and vanilla.|Pour into a greased 9x13 pan, bake 180°C (350°F) 25 minutes.|Mix the three milks together.|Poke warm cake all over with a fork, pour milk mixture over slowly.|Refrigerate at least 4 hours to absorb.|Top with whipped cream, sprinkle cinnamon, serve cold.

title: Tamal Asado
summary: Costa Rican sweet baked corn cake
description: Tamal Asado is a sweet Costa Rican corn cake baked in the oven — made from fresh corn, cheese and sour cream, with a moist, dense texture.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh corn kernels|1/2 cup sugar|1/4 cup melted butter|2 eggs|1/2 cup sour cream|100g queso fresco, crumbled|1 tsp baking powder|1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Blend corn kernels until mostly smooth (some texture is fine).|Mix in sugar, butter, eggs, sour cream, cheese, baking powder and salt.|Pour into a greased baking dish.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 45-50 minutes until set and golden on top.|Let cool slightly, cut into squares.|Serve warm as a sweet side or dessert.

title: Empanadas de Chiverre
summary: Costa Rican turnovers filled with squash jam
description: Empanadas de Chiverre are sweet Costa Rican turnovers filled with chiverre (fig-leaf gourd) jam — golden, crispy and a Holy Week tradition.
ingredients: For dough: 2 cups flour, 1/4 cup sugar, 1/4 cup butter, 1 egg, 3 tbsp water, 1/2 tsp salt|For filling: 2 cups chiverre jam (or any squash jam/dulce de leche)|oil for frying|sugar for sprinkling
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, sugar, salt, cut in butter, add egg and water, knead. Rest 30 min.|Roll dough thin, cut circles.|Place a spoonful of chiverre filling on each circle.|Fold over, seal edges with a fork.|Fry in 170°C (340°F) oil until golden, about 3 min per side.|Drain, sprinkle with sugar while hot.

title: Ceviche Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican sea bass ceviche with Salsa Lizano
description: Costa Rican ceviche uses sea bass or tilapia "cooked" in lime juice, mixed with onion, cilantro and pepper, and uniquely finished with Salsa Lizano.
ingredients: 500g firm white fish, diced small|juice of 8-10 limes|1 red onion, finely diced|1 bell pepper, finely diced|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|1 tbsp Salsa Lizano|salt and pepper|tortilla chips or crackers
instructions: Place diced fish in a bowl, cover completely with lime juice.|Refrigerate 2-3 hours until fish is opaque throughout (cured).|Drain most of the lime juice.|Mix in onion, bell pepper, cilantro and Salsa Lizano.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve cold with tortilla chips or crackers.

title: Chorreadas
summary: Costa Rican sweet fresh corn pancakes
description: Chorreadas are thick Costa Rican corn pancakes made from fresh sweet corn — naturally sweet, golden and served with sour cream.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh corn kernels|2 eggs|2 tbsp sugar|2 tbsp flour|1/4 tsp salt|butter for cooking|sour cream for serving
instructions: Blend corn kernels with eggs until mostly smooth with some texture.|Mix in sugar, flour and salt.|Heat butter in a skillet over medium heat.|Pour 1/4 cup of batter for each pancake.|Cook 3-4 minutes per side until golden.|Serve warm with sour cream.

title: Picadillo de Arracache
summary: Costa Rican diced arracacha root vegetable hash
description: Picadillo de Arracache is a Costa Rican hash of diced arracacha (a South American root vegetable) cooked with onion, garlic, cilantro and tomato.
ingredients: 500g arracacha (or parsnip), peeled and diced small|1 onion, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|2 tomatoes, diced|2 tbsp oil|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|salt and pepper
instructions: Heat oil, sauté onion and garlic until soft.|Add diced arracacha, cook 5 minutes.|Add tomatoes and enough water to barely cover.|Cook 15-20 minutes until arracacha is tender and liquid absorbed.|Season with salt, pepper and cilantro.|Serve as a side dish with rice, beans and tortillas.

title: Tortillas Asadas
summary: Costa Rican griddled fresh corn tortillas
description: Costa Rican Tortillas Asadas are thick, hand-shaped corn tortillas cooked on a hot griddle — a daily staple made from fresh or dried corn.
ingredients: 2 cups masa harina|1 cup warm water|1/2 tsp salt|1 tbsp butter (optional)
instructions: Mix masa harina, water and salt into a smooth dough.|Let rest 10 minutes.|Divide into 8 balls, flatten each into a thick round tortilla.|Cook on a dry hot griddle 3-4 minutes per side until speckled brown.|Serve warm with sour cream, beans or cheese.

title: Pozol Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican sweet fermented corn drink
description: Pozol is a refreshing Costa Rican drink of fermented corn dough dissolved in water — slightly sour, mildly sweet and cold, perfect for hot days.
ingredients: 1 cup fresh corn masa|4 cups cold water|sugar to taste|cinnamon (optional)|ice
instructions: Dissolve corn masa in cold water, stirring until smooth.|Strain through a sieve if desired for smoothness.|Add sugar to taste and a pinch of cinnamon.|Serve over ice, stir before drinking.

title: Horchata Costa Rica
summary: Costa Rican herbal rice drink with spices
description: Costa Rican horchata is unique — not just rice milk, but a complex drink made with toasted rice, cinnamon, cocoa and various dried flowers and herbs.
ingredients: 1/2 cup rice, toasted until golden|1 tbsp cocoa powder|1 cinnamon stick|1 tbsp dried chamomile or mint|4 cups water|sugar to taste|ice
instructions: Toast rice in a dry pan until golden.|Blend toasted rice with water and cinnamon until smooth.|Strain through a fine mesh or cheesecloth.|Add cocoa powder, sugar and dried herbs, stir.|Chill thoroughly, serve over ice.

title: Chilasquiles
summary: Costa Rican corn tortilla stuffed with cheese and beans
description: Costa Rican Chilasquiles are different from Mexican chilaquiles — they are corn tortillas stuffed with a cheese and bean mixture, rolled and fried until crispy.
ingredients: 8 corn tortillas|1 cup refried black beans|200g mozzarella or queso fresco, cubed|oil for frying|sour cream for serving|Salsa Lizano
instructions: Spread a tablespoon of beans on each tortilla.|Place a few cubes of cheese in the center.|Roll up tightly, secure with a toothpick.|Fry in oil until golden and crispy on all sides.|Drain on paper towels, remove toothpicks.|Serve with sour cream and Salsa Lizano.

title: Guanábana Smoothie
description: Guanábana (soursop) smoothie is one of the most beloved drinks in Costa Rica — creamy, tangy soursop blended with milk and sugar into a refreshing fresco.
ingredients: 2 cups fresh soursop (guanábana) pulp|2 cups cold milk or water|1/4 cup sugar|ice cubes|1 tsp vanilla (optional)
instructions: Remove seeds from soursop pulp.|Blend pulp with milk, sugar and vanilla until smooth.|Strain if desired for a smoother texture.|Serve over ice immediately.

title: Resbaladera
summary: Costa Rican barley and rice refreshment drink
description: Resbaladera is a refreshing Costa Rican drink from Guanacaste — made from barley, rice and cinnamon, served cold with ice.
ingredients: 1/2 cup barley|1/2 cup rice|6 cups water|1 cinnamon stick|1/2 cup sugar|1 tsp vanilla|ice
instructions: Soak barley and rice separately for 4 hours.|Cook barley in 3 cups water with cinnamon until very soft, about 30 minutes.|Cook rice in 3 cups water until soft.|Blend both together until smooth.|Strain through cheesecloth or fine mesh.|Add sugar and vanilla, chill.|Serve cold over ice.

title: Halloumi
summary: Cypriot grilled or fried semi-hard cheese
description: Halloumi is Cyprus's most famous contribution to world cuisine — a semi-hard, brined cheese that doesn't melt, perfect for grilling or frying until golden outside and squeaky inside.
ingredients: 250g halloumi cheese, sliced 1cm thick|1 tbsp olive oil|juice of 1/2 lemon|fresh mint leaves|1/2 tsp dried oregano
instructions: Slice halloumi into 1cm thick pieces.|Heat a grill pan or skillet to very hot with a little olive oil.|Grill halloumi slices 2 minutes per side until golden grill marks appear.|Immediately squeeze lemon juice over the hot cheese.|Garnish with fresh mint and oregano.|Serve immediately — halloumi is best hot.

title: Kleftiko
summary: Cypriot slow-baked lamb sealed in parchment or clay
description: Kleftiko is lamb slow-baked for hours in a sealed clay oven or parchment until it falls apart — the name means "stolen" as bandits would slow-cook lamb in sealed pits.
ingredients: 1.5 kg lamb shoulder, bone-in|4 potatoes, quartered|1 onion, quartered|4 cloves garlic, whole|juice of 2 lemons|3 tbsp olive oil|1 tbsp dried oregano|2 bay leaves|1 cinnamon stick|salt and pepper|parchment paper and foil
instructions: Season lamb with lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper.|Place on a large sheet of parchment, surround with potatoes, onion, garlic, bay leaves and cinnamon.|Wrap parchment tightly, then wrap in foil to seal completely.|Bake at 160°C (325°F) for 4-5 hours until lamb is falling off the bone.|Open the packet at the table — the aroma is incredible.|Serve the lamb with the potatoes and cooking juices.

title: Sheftalia
summary: Cypriot crépinette sausages wrapped in caul fat
description: Sheftalia are Cypriot sausages made from ground pork and lamb wrapped in caul fat (not casing), grilled until the fat renders and the outside crisps up.
ingredients: 300g ground pork|200g ground lamb|1 large onion, very finely diced|1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped|1 tsp ground cinnamon|1/2 tsp allspice|salt and pepper|caul fat (panna), cut into 6-inch squares
instructions: Mix pork, lamb, onion, parsley, cinnamon, allspice, salt and pepper.|Place a sausage-shaped portion on each piece of caul fat.|Wrap the fat around the meat, tucking the ends under.|Grill over medium charcoal, turning carefully, for 12-15 minutes until cooked through and fat is crispy.|Serve in warm pita with salad, tomato and tzatziki.

title: Moussaka Cyprus
summary: Cypriot layered eggplant, potato and meat casserole with béchamel
description: Cypriot Moussaka layers fried eggplant, potatoes and spiced ground meat topped with a thick béchamel sauce, baked until golden.
ingredients: 2 eggplants, sliced|3 potatoes, sliced|500g ground beef or lamb|1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|1 tsp cinnamon|salt and pepper|For béchamel: 3 tbsp butter, 3 tbsp flour, 2 cups milk, 1 egg, pinch nutmeg|oil for frying|1/4 cup halloumi, grated
instructions: Fry eggplant and potato slices until golden, drain.|Brown ground meat with onion, add tomatoes, cinnamon, salt and pepper, cook 15 minutes.|Make béchamel: melt butter, add flour, cook 1 min, gradually add milk stirring until thick, remove from heat, stir in egg and nutmeg.|Layer in a baking dish: potatoes, meat, eggplant, more meat.|Pour béchamel over the top, sprinkle with halloumi.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 40 minutes until golden and bubbly.

title: Tavas
summary: Cypriot baked lamb and orzo casserole from Paphos
description: Tavas is a rustic Cypriot casserole from Paphos — lamb baked with orzo pasta, tomatoes, onions and cumin in a clay pot until everything is tender.
ingredients: 600g lamb shoulder, cubed|1 cup orzo pasta|2 onions, sliced|3 tomatoes, diced|2 tsp ground cumin|1 tsp paprika|salt and pepper|2 tbsp olive oil|2 cups water|fresh parsley
instructions: Layer onions in the bottom of a clay pot or baking dish.|Add lamb, season with cumin, paprika, salt and pepper.|Add tomatoes and orzo pasta.|Pour water and olive oil over everything.|Cover tightly, bake at 170°C (340°F) for 2 hours.|Check once — add water if needed, the orzo absorbs a lot.|Uncover last 15 minutes to brown top.|Garnish with parsley and serve.

title: Koupes
summary: Cypriot bulgur wheat croquettes filled with spiced meat
description: Koupes are Cypriot torpedo-shaped bulgur croquettes with a spiced ground meat filling, deep-fried until the shell is crispy — a popular meze item.
ingredients: For shell: 2 cups fine bulgur, 1 cup flour, salt, water|For filling: 300g ground beef or pork, 1 onion diced, 1 tsp cinnamon, 1/2 tsp allspice, 2 tbsp parsley, salt, pepper|oil for deep frying
instructions: Soak bulgur in warm water 30 minutes, drain well. Mix with flour and salt to form a pliable dough.|Cook filling: brown meat with onion, add spices and parsley, cool.|Take a portion of bulgur dough, shape into a hollow torpedo in your palm.|Fill with meat mixture, seal closed.|Deep fry at 175°C (350°F) for 4-5 minutes until dark golden.|Drain on paper towels, serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Souvla
summary: Cypriot large meat pieces slow-grilled on a rotisserie
description: Souvla is Cyprus's signature BBQ — large chunks of lamb, pork or chicken threaded on a long skewer and slowly rotated over charcoal for hours.
ingredients: 2 kg pork neck or lamb shoulder, cut into very large chunks|3 tbsp olive oil|juice of 2 lemons|2 tbsp dried oregano|1 tbsp coarse salt|1 tsp black pepper|pita bread and salad for serving
instructions: Season meat chunks with olive oil, lemon, oregano, salt and pepper.|Thread onto a long rotisserie skewer.|Set up over a low charcoal fire.|Rotate slowly for 2-3 hours, basting occasionally with lemon-oil mixture.|The meat should be deeply browned outside and tender inside.|Slice and serve in warm pita with village salad and tzatziki.

title: Loukoumades
description: Loukoumades are Cypriot fried dough balls — golden, puffy and light, drenched in warm honey syrup and sprinkled with cinnamon and crushed walnuts.
ingredients: 2 cups flour|1 tsp dry yeast|1 cup warm water|1/4 tsp salt|1 tbsp sugar|oil for frying|For syrup: 1 cup honey, 1/2 cup water, 1 tbsp lemon juice, cinnamon stick|crushed walnuts and cinnamon for topping
instructions: Mix flour, yeast, sugar and salt. Add warm water, mix into a sticky batter.|Cover, let rise 1.5 hours until very bubbly.|Make honey syrup: warm honey with water, lemon and cinnamon.|Heat oil to 175°C (350°F).|Drop spoonfuls of batter into oil, fry until golden all over.|Drain briefly, then drown in warm honey syrup.|Pile on a plate, top with crushed walnuts and cinnamon.

title: Flaounes
description: Flaounes are Cypriot Easter pastries — a special yeast dough filled with a mixture of halloumi, eggs and mint, baked until golden with a sesame crust.
ingredients: For dough: 3 cups flour, 1 tsp yeast, 1/2 cup warm milk, 1/4 cup oil, 1 tsp mastic, 1 tsp mahlab, salt|For filling: 300g halloumi grated, 100g cheddar grated, 3 eggs, 1 tbsp fresh mint, 1 tbsp raisins|1 egg yolk for glaze, sesame seeds
instructions: Make dough with flour, yeast, milk, oil, mastic, mahlab and salt. Knead, rest 1 hour.|Mix cheeses, eggs, mint and raisins for filling.|Divide dough into 12 pieces, roll into circles.|Place filling in center, fold up sides into a square or triangle, leaving top open.|Brush with egg yolk, sprinkle sesame seeds.|Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 20-25 minutes until golden.

title: Mahalepi
description: Mahalepi is a delicate Cypriot milk pudding set with cornstarch and flavored with mastic and rosewater — white, wobbly and refreshing.
ingredients: 4 cups milk|4 tbsp cornstarch|1/3 cup sugar|2 tbsp rosewater|1/4 tsp mastic, ground|1 tbsp crushed pistachios|rose syrup for drizzling
instructions: Dissolve cornstarch in 1/2 cup of the cold milk.|Heat remaining milk with sugar and mastic, stirring until dissolved.|Add cornstarch mixture, stir constantly until thickened, about 8 minutes.|Remove from heat, stir in rosewater.|Pour into molds or a dish, chill until set (3-4 hours).|Unmold, drizzle with rose syrup and pistachios.

title: Trahanas Soup
summary: Cypriot fermented wheat and yogurt soup
description: Trahanas is a traditional Cypriot soup made from dried fermented wheat and yogurt pellets — simmered with halloumi into a tangy, comforting bowl.
ingredients: 1 cup trahanas (sour or sweet)|4 cups chicken broth|100g halloumi, cubed|2 tbsp butter|salt and pepper|fresh mint
instructions: Soak trahanas in broth for 30 minutes.|Bring to a simmer, cook 20-25 minutes, stirring, until thick and creamy.|Add halloumi cubes, cook 5 minutes until they soften slightly.|Stir in butter, season with salt and pepper.|Serve hot, garnished with fresh mint.

title: Kolokasi Stew
summary: Cypriot taro root stew with pork and celery
description: Kolokasi is a Cypriot stew of taro root cooked with pork, celery and tomatoes in lemon juice — a traditional comfort dish unique to Cyprus.
ingredients: 500g kolokasi (taro root), peeled and roughly broken|400g pork, cubed|1 bunch celery, chopped|2 tomatoes, diced|juice of 2 lemons|3 tbsp olive oil|1 onion, diced|salt and pepper|4 cups water
instructions: Break taro into rough chunks by scoring and snapping (don't cut — it stays firmer).|Brown pork in olive oil, add onion and cook until soft.|Add tomatoes, cook 5 minutes.|Add water and lemon juice, bring to a simmer.|Add taro and celery, season with salt and pepper.|Cover and cook 45-60 minutes until taro is tender.|Serve hot with bread.

title: Louvi Salad
summary: Cypriot black-eyed pea salad with lemon and olive oil
description: Louvi is a simple Cypriot salad of boiled black-eyed peas dressed with olive oil, lemon juice and chard — a Lenten staple.
ingredients: 2 cups dried black-eyed peas, soaked|1 bunch Swiss chard, chopped|1/4 cup olive oil|juice of 2 lemons|1 small onion, diced|salt and pepper
instructions: Boil black-eyed peas until tender, about 30 minutes.|In the last 5 minutes, add chopped chard to the pot.|Drain, toss with olive oil, lemon juice and onion.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Afelia
summary: Cypriot pork braised in red wine and coriander seeds
description: Afelia is a classic Cypriot dish of pork cubes marinated and braised in red wine with crushed coriander seeds — simple, fragrant and deeply satisfying.
ingredients: 600g pork shoulder, cubed|1 cup dry red wine|2 tbsp crushed coriander seeds|3 tbsp olive oil|salt and pepper|bulgur or rice for serving
instructions: Marinate pork in wine and coriander for at least 2 hours (overnight best).|Heat olive oil, brown pork on all sides (reserve marinade).|Pour in the wine-coriander marinade.|Cover and simmer on low heat for 45-60 minutes until pork is very tender and sauce is reduced.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve with bulgur pilaf or rice.

title: Stifado Cyprus
summary: Cypriot beef or rabbit stew with pearl onions and red wine
description: Cypriot Stifado is a rich stew of beef or rabbit braised with lots of pearl onions in a wine and vinegar sauce with cinnamon and cloves.
ingredients: 600g beef chuck, cubed|500g pearl onions, peeled|1 cup red wine|2 tbsp red wine vinegar|3 tbsp tomato paste|1 cinnamon stick|3 cloves|2 bay leaves|3 tbsp olive oil|salt and pepper
instructions: Brown beef in olive oil, remove.|Add pearl onions, brown lightly.|Return beef, add wine, vinegar, tomato paste, spices and enough water to cover.|Bring to a boil, then reduce to a low simmer.|Cover and cook 2-2.5 hours until beef is meltingly tender and sauce is thick.|Season with salt and pepper, serve with crusty bread.

title: Zalatina
description: Zalatina is a Cypriot pork terrine made by boiling pork head and trotters with vinegar and lemon until the gelatin sets — served sliced cold as a meze.
ingredients: 1 kg pork head or trotters|1/4 cup white vinegar|juice of 2 lemons|2 bay leaves|1 tsp black peppercorns|salt|8 cups water
instructions: Boil pork in water with vinegar, bay leaves and peppercorns for 3-4 hours until falling apart.|Remove meat from bones, chop finely.|Strain broth, add lemon juice and salt.|Place chopped meat in a mold, pour broth over.|Refrigerate until set into a firm jelly (overnight).|Slice and serve cold as a meze with bread.

title: Loukanika
summary: Cypriot smoked pork sausage with coriander and red wine
description: Loukanika is a traditional Cypriot pork sausage flavored with red wine, coriander seeds and cinnamon, smoked until firm and aromatic.
ingredients: 1 kg pork shoulder, coarsely ground|200g pork fat, diced|1/2 cup red wine|2 tbsp crushed coriander seeds|1 tsp cinnamon|1 tsp black pepper|1 tbsp salt|natural hog casings
instructions: Mix ground pork, fat, wine, coriander, cinnamon, pepper and salt.|Let mixture marinate refrigerated overnight.|Stuff into natural hog casings, tie into 6-inch links.|Hang in a cool place for 24 hours.|Cold-smoke for 2-3 days, or dry in a well-ventilated area.|To serve: grill, fry or slice cold.

title: Tzatziki Cyprus
summary: Cypriot yogurt and cucumber dip with garlic and mint
description: Cypriot Tzatziki is a thick, creamy dip of strained yogurt with grated cucumber, garlic, olive oil and dried mint — served with every meze spread.
ingredients: 2 cups thick Greek yogurt|1 cucumber, grated and squeezed dry|2 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp olive oil|1 tbsp dried mint|salt|drizzle of olive oil for serving
instructions: Grate cucumber, squeeze out all excess water.|Mix yogurt, cucumber, garlic, olive oil and mint.|Season with salt.|Chill for at least 30 minutes.|Drizzle with olive oil before serving.|Serve with warm pita bread or as a meze accompaniment.

title: Melomakarona
summary: Cypriot honey-soaked walnut cookies for Christmas
description: Melomakarona are Cypriot Christmas cookies — dense, spiced and soaked in a honey-orange syrup, then topped with crushed walnuts and cinnamon.
ingredients: 3 cups flour|1/2 cup olive oil|1/4 cup sugar|1/4 cup orange juice|zest of 1 orange|1 tsp cinnamon|1/4 tsp cloves|1 tsp baking powder|For syrup: 1 cup honey, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 1 strip orange zest|crushed walnuts and cinnamon for topping
instructions: Mix oil and sugar, add OJ, zest, spices.|Add flour and baking powder, knead into a firm dough.|Shape into ovals, place on a lined baking sheet.|Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 20-25 minutes until firm.|Make syrup: boil sugar, water and orange zest 5 min, add honey.|Dip warm cookies in hot syrup for 15 seconds.|Place on a rack, top with walnuts and cinnamon.

title: Pourgouri
description: Pourgouri is a Cypriot bulgur pilaf cooked with vermicelli noodles and tomato — a common side dish that's healthier than rice and full of flavor.
ingredients: 2 cups coarse bulgur wheat|1/4 cup vermicelli noodles, broken|1 onion, diced|2 tomatoes, diced|3 tbsp olive oil|3 cups chicken broth|salt and pepper
instructions: Toast vermicelli in olive oil until golden.|Add onion, cook until soft.|Add tomatoes, cook 3 minutes.|Add bulgur and broth, bring to a boil.|Reduce heat, cover and cook 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed.|Rest 10 minutes covered, fluff with fork.|Serve as a side dish with any Cypriot meat.

title: Skoudehkaris
summary: Spiced rice and meat dish from Djibouti
description: A traditional Djiboutian rice dish cooked with tender meat, aromatic spices, and vegetables. The rice absorbs all the flavors creating a fragrant one-pot meal.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|500g lamb shoulder, cubed|3 tablespoons ghee|2 large onions, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon cinnamon|1 teaspoon turmeric|4 cups beef broth|1 cup diced carrots|1 cup diced potatoes|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee in a large pot and brown the lamb on all sides|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant|Stir in cumin, cinnamon, and turmeric, cooking for 1 minute|Add rice and toast for 2 minutes, stirring constantly|Pour in beef broth and add carrots and potatoes|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for 20 minutes until rice is tender|Let rest for 5 minutes before serving

title: Lahoh
description: A soft, spongy pancake-like bread that is a staple in Djiboutian cuisine. Served with honey, ghee, or savory accompaniments.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 tablespoon sugar|1 teaspoon salt|1 tablespoon instant yeast|1.5 cups warm water|3 tablespoons vegetable oil|Honey for serving
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a large bowl|Add warm water and oil, stirring until a thick batter forms|Let batter rest for 30 minutes until bubbly|Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat|Pour batter into skillet and cook until top is set and bottom is golden|Flip and cook other side briefly|Serve warm with honey or ghee

title: Maraq Soup Djibouti
description: A hearty Djiboutian soup made with tender meat, vegetables, and aromatic spices. Perfect comfort food served with bread.
ingredients: 600g beef chuck, cubed|2 tablespoons ghee|2 large onions, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|3 carrots, sliced|3 potatoes, cubed|1 teaspoon ginger powder|1 teaspoon turmeric|6 cups beef broth|1 cup diced tomatoes|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown beef cubes on all sides|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in ginger and turmeric, cooking for 1 minute|Add carrots, potatoes, and tomatoes|Pour in beef broth and bring to boil|Reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes until meat is tender|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Fah Fah
description: A fragrant Djiboutian dish combining tender meat with rice and warm spices. Often served at special occasions.
ingredients: 400g lamb, cubed|2 cups basmati rice|3 tablespoons ghee|2 onions, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 teaspoons coriander|1 teaspoon cardamom|1 teaspoon cloves|4 cups lamb broth|1 bay leaf|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown lamb pieces|Add onions and garlic, cook until golden|Toast coriander, cardamom, and cloves in the pot for 1 minute|Add rice and stir to coat with ghee|Pour in lamb broth and add bay leaf|Bring to boil, then cover and simmer for 20 minutes|Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes before serving

title: Canjeero Djibouti
description: A fermented spongy bread similar to injera, served as a base for stews and curries in Djiboutian cuisine.
ingredients: 2 cups teff flour|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 tablespoon salt|2.5 cups water|1 tablespoon active sourdough starter
instructions: Mix flours and salt in a bowl|Add water and sourdough starter, stirring well|Cover and let ferment for 3 days at room temperature|Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat|Pour batter onto skillet and cook until top is set|Serve warm with stews and curries

title: Mukbaza
description: A warming Djiboutian stew featuring tender meat, root vegetables, and aromatic spices cooked slowly until flavors meld.
ingredients: 700g goat meat, cubed|3 tablespoons ghee|3 onions, chopped|5 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon cinnamon|4 carrots, chunked|3 potatoes, chunked|5 cups water|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown goat meat in batches|Add onions, garlic, and ginger, sauté until fragrant|Stir in cumin and cinnamon|Return meat to pot and add carrots and potatoes|Pour in water and bring to boil|Reduce heat and simmer covered for 1.5 hours until meat is very tender|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Hilib Ari Djibouti
description: Tender grilled meat seasoned with traditional Djiboutian spices, often served with rice or bread.
ingredients: 800g lamb shoulder, sliced|4 tablespoons ghee|6 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|1 teaspoon black pepper|2 tablespoons lemon juice|Salt to taste
instructions: Mix ghee, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, pepper, and lemon juice|Coat lamb slices with the spice mixture and marinate for 30 minutes|Heat a grill or grill pan to high heat|Grill lamb slices for 3-4 minutes per side until cooked through|Serve hot with rice or flatbread

title: Sambuusa Djibouti
description: Crispy triangular pastries filled with spiced meat, a popular appetizer in Djiboutian cuisine.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 teaspoon salt|0.5 cup water|3 tablespoons vegetable oil|300g ground lamb|1 onion, finely chopped|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 teaspoon cumin|0.5 teaspoon cayenne pepper|Oil for frying
instructions: Mix flour and salt, add water and 3 tablespoons oil to form dough|Knead until smooth and let rest for 20 minutes|Brown ground lamb with onion and garlic|Add cumin and cayenne pepper to meat mixture|Roll dough thin and cut into squares|Place meat filling on each square and fold into triangle|Deep fry until golden brown on both sides|Drain on paper towels and serve hot

title: Spiced Rice Djibouti
description: Fragrant rice cooked with ghee and traditional Djiboutian spices, a staple side dish.
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice|4 tablespoons ghee|1 onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 teaspoon cumin seeds|1 teaspoon coriander seeds|4 green cardamom pods|2 bay leaves|4 cups vegetable broth|Salt to taste
instructions: Heat ghee in a pot and add cumin and coriander seeds|When seeds pop, add onion and garlic, sauté until golden|Add rice and stir to coat with ghee|Pour in vegetable broth and add cardamom and bay leaves|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and cover|Simmer for 18 minutes until rice is tender|Let rest for 5 minutes before fluffing with fork

title: Malawah Djibouti
description: A rich, flaky flatbread made with layers of dough and ghee, served with honey or savory dishes.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 teaspoon salt|1 cup warm water|1 cup ghee, melted|Honey for serving
instructions: Mix flour and salt, add water gradually to form soft dough|Knead for 10 minutes until smooth|Divide into 8 balls and let rest for 20 minutes|Roll each ball thin, brush with ghee, and fold into layers|Heat a skillet over medium heat|Cook each malawah for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crispy|Serve warm with honey or alongside stews

title: Basbousa Djibouti
description: A sweet semolina and coconut cake soaked in simple syrup, a popular dessert in Djibouti.
ingredients: 2 cups semolina|1 cup shredded coconut|1 cup sugar|1 cup yogurt|1 tablespoon baking powder|1 cup water|1 tablespoon lemon juice|1 tablespoon rose water|30 whole cloves
instructions: Mix semolina, coconut, sugar, yogurt, and baking powder|Press mixture into a greased pan and cut into diamond shapes|Place one clove in center of each piece|Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes until golden|Meanwhile, boil water with sugar, lemon juice, and rose water for 10 minutes|Pour hot syrup over hot basbousa immediately after baking|Cool completely before serving

title: Cambuulo
description: A protein-rich Djiboutian stew combining mung beans with tender meat and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1.5 cups dried mung beans|400g lamb, cubed|3 tablespoons ghee|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon turmeric|6 cups water|2 carrots, sliced|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Soak mung beans for 2 hours, then drain|Heat ghee and brown lamb pieces|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in cumin and turmeric|Add mung beans, water, and carrots|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour until beans are tender|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Muufo Djibouti
description: A traditional Djiboutian stew with tender meat, vegetables, and warm spices served with rice or bread.
ingredients: 600g beef chuck, cubed|3 tablespoons ghee|2 large onions, chopped|5 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|1 teaspoon cinnamon|1 teaspoon cloves|4 carrots, chunked|3 potatoes, chunked|5 cups beef broth|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown beef on all sides|Add onions, garlic, and ginger, cook until fragrant|Stir in cinnamon and cloves|Add carrots, potatoes, and beef broth|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for 1.5 hours|Meat should be very tender and vegetables soft|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Dabaab
description: A hearty Djiboutian chickpea preparation with aromatic spices and vegetables.
ingredients: 2 cups dried chickpeas|3 tablespoons ghee|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|1 teaspoon turmeric|3 carrots, sliced|2 cups vegetable broth|1 cup diced tomatoes|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain and boil for 1 hour until tender|Heat ghee and sauté onions and garlic|Add cumin, coriander, and turmeric|Add cooked chickpeas, carrots, broth, and tomatoes|Simmer for 30 minutes until flavors blend|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Anjeero with Honey
description: Soft, spongy fermented bread served with warm honey, a traditional Djiboutian breakfast.
ingredients: 2 cups teff flour|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 tablespoon salt|2.5 cups water|1 tablespoon active sourdough starter|1 cup honey|2 tablespoons ghee
instructions: Mix flours, salt, water, and sourdough starter|Cover and ferment for 3 days at room temperature|Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat|Pour batter onto skillet and cook until top is set|Warm honey with ghee in a small pot|Serve anjeero warm with honey drizzled on top

title: Goat Stew Djibouti
summary: Tender goat meat in aromatic stew
description: A rich Djiboutian stew featuring tender goat meat with vegetables and warming spices.
ingredients: 800g goat meat, cubed|4 tablespoons ghee|3 onions, chopped|6 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon cinnamon|4 carrots, chunked|3 potatoes, chunked|6 cups water|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown goat meat in batches|Add onions, garlic, and ginger, sauté until fragrant|Stir in cumin and cinnamon|Return meat to pot with carrots and potatoes|Add water and bring to boil|Reduce heat and simmer covered for 2 hours until meat is very tender|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Ful Medames Djibouti
description: A protein-rich Djiboutian dish of slow-cooked fava beans with garlic and spices.
ingredients: 2 cups dried fava beans|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tablespoons ghee|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|2 tablespoons lemon juice|6 cups water|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Soak fava beans overnight, then drain|Place beans in pot with water and bring to boil|Reduce heat and simmer for 2 hours until very soft|Heat ghee and sauté garlic until fragrant|Add cumin and coriander to garlic|Stir garlic mixture into cooked beans|Add lemon juice and season with salt and pepper|Serve warm with bread

title: Fish Curry Djibouti
description: A fragrant Djiboutian curry featuring fresh fish in a coconut and spice-based sauce.
ingredients: 800g white fish fillets|3 tablespoons ghee|2 onions, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon ginger, minced|2 teaspoons turmeric|1 teaspoon cumin|1 can coconut milk|2 tablespoons lemon juice|2 tomatoes, chopped|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and sauté onions, garlic, and ginger|Add turmeric and cumin, cooking for 1 minute|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes|Pour in coconut milk and bring to simmer|Add fish fillets and lemon juice|Simmer gently for 10 minutes until fish is cooked through|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Sambaaz
description: A Djiboutian dish combining ground meat with rice and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 400g ground lamb|2 cups basmati rice|3 tablespoons ghee|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 teaspoons cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|4 cups lamb broth|1 cup diced tomatoes|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown ground lamb|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in cumin and coriander|Add rice and toast for 2 minutes|Pour in lamb broth and tomatoes|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and cover|Simmer for 20 minutes until rice is tender|Let rest for 5 minutes before serving

title: Anjero Soup
description: A traditional Djiboutian soup made with pieces of spongy bread in a flavorful broth with meat and vegetables.
ingredients: 4 cups beef broth|400g beef chuck, cubed|2 tablespoons ghee|2 onions, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|1 teaspoon cumin|2 cups torn anjero bread|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee and brown beef cubes|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in cumin|Add carrots, potatoes, and beef broth|Bring to boil and simmer for 30 minutes|Add torn anjero bread pieces|Simmer for 10 more minutes until bread softens|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Sancocho Dominicano
summary: Traditional Dominican stew with meat and vegetables
description: A hearty Dominican stew featuring tender meat, root vegetables, and plantains in a flavorful broth. A national dish of Dominican Republic.
ingredients: 800g beef chuck, cubed|400g chicken thighs, cubed|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 large onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons tomato paste|3 carrots, chunked|3 potatoes, chunked|2 plantains, chunked|8 cups beef broth|1 teaspoon oregano|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown beef and chicken pieces|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes|Add carrots, potatoes, and plantains|Pour in beef broth and add oregano|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours|Meat should be very tender and vegetables soft|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: La Bandera
summary: Dominican flag plate with rice, beans, and meat
description: The national dish of Dominican Republic featuring white rice, red beans, and stewed meat arranged on one plate.
ingredients: 2 cups white rice|1.5 cups red beans, cooked|600g beef chuck, cubed|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons tomato paste|1 teaspoon oregano|2 cups beef broth|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Cook rice in salted water until tender|Heat olive oil and brown beef cubes|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in tomato paste and oregano|Add beef broth and simmer for 45 minutes until meat is tender|Warm cooked beans in a separate pot|Arrange rice, beans, and meat on plate|Serve hot with the meat sauce

title: Mangú
description: A Dominican breakfast dish of mashed plantains topped with sautéed onions, cheese, and sometimes salami.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, peeled and cubed|4 tablespoons butter|0.5 cup milk|3 onions, sliced|2 tablespoons olive oil|200g Dominican salami, sliced|1 cup queso de freír cheese, cubed|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Boil plantain cubes in salted water for 15 minutes until tender|Drain and mash with butter and milk until creamy|Heat olive oil and sauté onions until golden and caramelized|Fry salami slices until crispy|Fry cheese cubes until golden on outside|Spoon mangú onto plate and top with onions, salami, and cheese|Serve immediately while hot

title: Tostones Dominican
description: Crispy fried plantain slices that are flattened and fried again, a popular Dominican side dish and snack.
ingredients: 6 green plantains, peeled|Oil for frying|2 teaspoons salt|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons olive oil
instructions: Cut plantains into 1-inch thick slices|Heat oil in deep pan to 350°F|Fry plantain slices for 3 minutes until light golden|Remove and flatten each slice with a tostonera or glass|Return flattened slices to hot oil and fry for 2 more minutes until crispy|Drain on paper towels|Make garlic oil by heating olive oil with minced garlic|Sprinkle tostones with salt and drizzle with garlic oil|Serve hot

title: Chicharrón Dominican
description: Tender pork belly fried until the skin is crispy and golden, a beloved Dominican appetizer and main dish.
ingredients: 1.5 kg pork belly with skin|2 tablespoons salt|1 tablespoon black pepper|2 tablespoons garlic powder|2 tablespoons oregano|1 tablespoon cumin|2 tablespoons olive oil
instructions: Cut pork belly into 2-inch cubes|Mix salt, pepper, garlic powder, oregano, and cumin|Rub spice mixture all over pork pieces|Let marinate for at least 2 hours|Heat olive oil in large pot over medium-high heat|Fry pork pieces for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until skin is crispy and meat is tender|Drain on paper towels|Serve hot with lime wedges

title: Arroz con Leche Dominican
description: A sweet Dominican rice pudding made with milk, cinnamon, and raisins, served warm or cold.
ingredients: 1.5 cups short-grain rice|4 cups whole milk|1 can sweetened condensed milk|1 can evaporated milk|0.5 cup raisins|1 tablespoon cinnamon|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|2 tablespoons butter|Pinch of salt
instructions: Cook rice in 3 cups water until tender|Heat whole milk, condensed milk, and evaporated milk together|Add cooked rice to milk mixture|Stir in raisins, cinnamon, vanilla, butter, and salt|Simmer over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring frequently|Rice should be creamy and milk mostly absorbed|Pour into serving dish and sprinkle with cinnamon|Serve warm or chilled

title: Habichuelas con Dulce
description: A traditional Dominican dessert made with red beans, coconut milk, and sweet spices, served warm.
ingredients: 2 cups red beans, cooked|1 can coconut milk|1 can evaporated milk|0.5 cup sugar|1 tablespoon cinnamon|0.5 teaspoon nutmeg|0.5 teaspoon cloves|2 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|Pinch of salt
instructions: Blend cooked red beans with coconut milk until smooth|Heat evaporated milk and sugar together|Add bean mixture to milk and stir well|Add cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, butter, vanilla, and salt|Simmer over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally|Mixture should be thick and creamy|Serve warm in bowls|Top with cinnamon if desired

title: Locrio de Pollo
description: A Dominican one-pot dish combining chicken, rice, and vegetables in a flavorful broth.
ingredients: 800g chicken thighs, cubed|2 cups long-grain rice|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons tomato paste|1 red bell pepper, diced|1 cup diced carrots|4 cups chicken broth|1 teaspoon oregano|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown chicken pieces|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes|Add rice and toast for 2 minutes|Add bell pepper, carrots, chicken broth, and oregano|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and cover|Simmer for 25 minutes until rice is tender and chicken cooked through|Let rest for 5 minutes before serving

title: Asopao Dominican
summary: Creamy Dominican rice and chicken stew
description: A thick, creamy Dominican stew combining chicken, rice, and vegetables in a rich broth.
ingredients: 800g chicken thighs, cubed|2 cups short-grain rice|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons tomato paste|1 red bell pepper, diced|1 cup diced carrots|6 cups chicken broth|0.5 cup green peas|1 teaspoon oregano|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown chicken pieces|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes|Add rice and toast for 2 minutes|Add bell pepper, carrots, chicken broth, and oregano|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes|Rice should be very soft and mixture creamy|Add peas and cook for 5 more minutes|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Mofongo Dominican
summary: Mashed plantain with garlic and pork
description: A Dominican classic of mashed plantains mixed with garlic, olive oil, and crispy pork bits, served as a side or main dish.
ingredients: 6 green plantains, peeled and cubed|0.5 cup olive oil|8 cloves garlic, minced|200g pork belly, cubed and fried|1 teaspoon salt|0.5 teaspoon black pepper|0.5 cup chicken broth
instructions: Boil plantain cubes in salted water for 12 minutes until tender|Heat olive oil and sauté garlic until fragrant|Fry pork belly cubes until crispy|Drain plantains and place in mortar or mofongo bowl|Add garlic oil and crispy pork to plantains|Mash together until combined but still chunky|Add chicken broth to reach desired consistency|Season with salt and pepper|Serve immediately in a mound

title: Pernil Dominican
summary: Roasted pork leg with crispy skin
description: A Dominican holiday favorite of slow-roasted pork leg with crispy, golden skin and tender, juicy meat.
ingredients: 4 kg pork leg with skin|2 tablespoons salt|1 tablespoon black pepper|2 tablespoons garlic powder|2 tablespoons oregano|1 tablespoon cumin|4 tablespoons olive oil|2 cups water
instructions: Score the pork skin in a crosshatch pattern|Mix salt, pepper, garlic powder, oregano, and cumin|Rub spice mixture all over pork, getting under the skin|Let marinate for at least 4 hours or overnight|Preheat oven to 325°F|Place pork in roasting pan with water|Roast for 4-5 hours, basting every 30 minutes|Skin should be crispy and golden, meat very tender|Rest for 15 minutes before carving

title: Sopa de Salami
description: A hearty Dominican soup featuring Dominican salami, vegetables, and potatoes in a flavorful broth.
ingredients: 300g Dominican salami, sliced|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|1 red bell pepper, diced|6 cups beef broth|1 teaspoon oregano|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat olive oil and fry salami slices until crispy|Remove salami and set aside|In same pot, sauté onions and garlic|Add potatoes, carrots, and bell pepper|Pour in beef broth and add oregano|Bring to boil and simmer for 30 minutes until vegetables are tender|Return salami to pot|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Morir Soñando
summary: Dominican milk and orange juice drink
description: A refreshing Dominican beverage combining fresh orange juice with evaporated milk, served over ice.
ingredients: 2 cups fresh orange juice|1 can evaporated milk|0.5 cup sugar|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|Ice cubes|Cinnamon for garnish
instructions: Pour orange juice into a blender|Add evaporated milk, sugar, and vanilla extract|Blend until frothy and well combined|Fill glasses with ice cubes|Pour drink over ice|Stir well and sprinkle with cinnamon|Serve immediately

title: Yuca en Escabeche
description: Boiled cassava root served in a tangy vinegar-based sauce with onions and peppers.
ingredients: 1 kg cassava root, peeled and cubed|3 onions, sliced|2 red bell peppers, sliced|1 cup white vinegar|0.5 cup olive oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 teaspoon oregano|1 teaspoon salt|0.5 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Boil cassava cubes in salted water for 20 minutes until tender|Drain and place in serving dish|Heat olive oil and sauté onions, peppers, and garlic|Add vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper|Simmer for 5 minutes|Pour hot vinegar mixture over cassava|Let cool to room temperature|Serve at room temperature or chilled

title: Cocido Dominican
summary: Dominican boiled meat and vegetable stew
description: A simple Dominican stew of boiled meat and vegetables, often served with broth and bread.
ingredients: 800g beef chuck, cubed|400g chicken thighs, cubed|3 potatoes, chunked|3 carrots, chunked|2 onions, quartered|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 plantains, chunked|8 cups water|1 teaspoon salt|0.5 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Place beef and chicken in large pot with water|Bring to boil and skim off foam|Add onions and garlic|Simmer for 45 minutes|Add potatoes, carrots, and plantains|Continue simmering for 30 minutes until all vegetables are tender|Season with salt and pepper|Serve meat and vegetables with broth

title: Mondongo Dominican
description: A traditional Dominican stew made with tender tripe, vegetables, and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1 kg beef tripe, cleaned and cubed|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons tomato paste|3 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|1 red bell pepper, diced|6 cups beef broth|1 teaspoon oregano|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Boil tripe in salted water for 1 hour until tender, then drain|Heat olive oil and sauté onions and garlic|Add tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes|Add cooked tripe, potatoes, carrots, and bell pepper|Pour in beef broth and add oregano|Simmer for 45 minutes until vegetables are tender|Season with salt and pepper before serving

title: Batata Asada
description: Simple roasted sweet potatoes, a traditional Dominican side dish often served with main meals.
ingredients: 6 medium sweet potatoes|3 tablespoons olive oil|2 teaspoons salt|1 teaspoon black pepper|1 teaspoon garlic powder|1 teaspoon oregano
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F|Wash sweet potatoes and prick with fork|Rub with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and oregano|Place on baking sheet|Roast for 45 minutes until tender when pierced|Cut open and serve hot with butter if desired

title: Bolitas de Tamarindo
description: Sweet and tangy tamarind candies rolled in sugar, a popular Dominican treat.
ingredients: 1 cup tamarind pulp|1 cup sugar|0.5 cup water|2 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|Sugar for rolling
instructions: Heat tamarind pulp, sugar, and water in a pot|Stir constantly over medium heat for 15 minutes until mixture thickens|Add butter and vanilla extract|Cool slightly until handleable|Roll into small balls|Roll each ball in sugar|Place on parchment paper to cool completely|Store in airtight container

title: Majarete
description: A sweet Dominican pudding made with fresh corn, milk, and cinnamon, served warm or cold.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh corn kernels|2 cups whole milk|1 can evaporated milk|0.5 cup sugar|3 tablespoons cornstarch|1 tablespoon cinnamon|2 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|Pinch of salt
instructions: Blend corn kernels with whole milk until smooth|Strain through fine sieve to remove corn solids|Heat corn milk with evaporated milk and sugar|Mix cornstarch with 2 tablespoons water to make slurry|Add cornstarch slurry to milk mixture, stirring constantly|Cook over medium heat for 10 minutes until thickened|Add butter, cinnamon, vanilla, and salt|Pour into serving dish and sprinkle with cinnamon|Serve warm or chilled

title: Dulce de Coco
description: A sweet Dominican candy made with fresh coconut and sugar, often served as a dessert or snack.
ingredients: 2 cups fresh grated coconut|2 cups sugar|0.5 cup water|1 tablespoon butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|0.5 teaspoon cinnamon
instructions: Combine sugar and water in a pot|Heat over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves|Add grated coconut and stir well|Continue cooking for 20 minutes, stirring frequently, until mixture thickens|Add butter, vanilla, and cinnamon|Cool slightly and drop spoonfuls onto parchment paper|Let cool completely before serving|Store in airtight container

title: Ikan Pepe
description: A traditional East Timorese dish of grilled fish coated with a spicy chili and garlic paste.
ingredients: 800g whole fish or fish fillets|6 red chilies, chopped|6 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|3 tablespoons coconut oil|2 tablespoons lime juice|1 teaspoon turmeric|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Make paste with chilies, garlic, ginger, turmeric, and salt|Rub paste all over fish and let marinate for 30 minutes|Heat coconut oil in grill pan or on grill|Grill fish for 5-7 minutes per side until cooked through|Drizzle with lime juice before serving|Serve hot with rice

title: Batar Daan
description: A traditional East Timorese preparation of corn cooked with coconut milk and spices.
ingredients: 3 cups fresh corn kernels|1 can coconut milk|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|2 red chilies, sliced|2 tablespoons coconut oil|1 teaspoon turmeric|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat coconut oil and sauté onions, garlic, ginger, and chilies|Add turmeric and cook for 1 minute|Add corn kernels and stir well|Pour in coconut milk and add salt|Simmer for 20 minutes until corn is tender and flavors blend|Serve hot as a side dish

title: Tapai East Timor
description: A traditional East Timorese fermented rice cake with a slightly sweet and tangy flavor.
ingredients: 2 cups glutinous rice|2 cups water|1 tablespoon rice yeast starter|2 tablespoons sugar|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Cook glutinous rice in water until tender|Cool rice to room temperature|Mix rice yeast starter with sugar and salt|Combine with cooked rice and mix well|Place in clean jar and cover loosely|Ferment at room temperature for 3-5 days|Rice will become slightly sweet and tangy|Serve as a snack or dessert

title: Katupa
description: Traditional East Timorese parcels of rice and meat wrapped in banana leaves and steamed.
ingredients: 2 cups glutinous rice|300g ground pork|2 onions, finely chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons coconut oil|1 teaspoon turmeric|1 teaspoon salt|Banana leaves for wrapping
instructions: Cook glutinous rice until just tender|Heat coconut oil and sauté onions and garlic|Add ground pork and cook until browned|Add turmeric and salt|Mix cooked rice with pork mixture|Place portion on banana leaf and wrap into parcel|Steam parcels for 20 minutes|Serve hot

title: Kaldeirada
description: A traditional East Timorese fish stew with potatoes, tomatoes, and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 800g white fish, cubed|3 tablespoons coconut oil|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|3 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|1 can diced tomatoes|2 cups fish broth|1 teaspoon turmeric|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat coconut oil and sauté onions, garlic, and ginger|Add turmeric and cook for 1 minute|Add potatoes and carrots, cook for 5 minutes|Add fish, tomatoes, and fish broth|Bring to simmer and cook for 20 minutes until fish is cooked through|Season with salt and pepper|Serve hot with rice

title: Saboko
description: A traditional East Timorese stew made with cassava leaves, coconut milk, and spices.
ingredients: 500g cassava leaves, chopped|1 can coconut milk|2 onions, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ginger, minced|2 red chilies, sliced|2 tablespoons coconut oil|1 teaspoon turmeric|1 teaspoon salt
