ingredients: 500g radishes, thinly sliced|2 onions, thinly sliced|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|2 green chiles, minced|3 limes, juiced|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper
instructions: Combine radishes, onions, cilantro, and green chiles|Add lime juice, salt, and pepper|Toss gently and let sit for 10 minutes|Adjust seasoning to taste|Serve cold

title: Enchiladas Guatemaltecas
description: Guatemalan enchiladas made with corn tortillas filled with meat or cheese, topped with a rich sauce and cheese.
ingredients: 12 corn tortillas|400g chicken, cooked and shredded|200g queso fresco, crumbled|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 cup diced tomatoes|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|500ml chicken broth|3 tbsp oil
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onions and garlic|Add tomatoes, tomato paste, cumin, and salt|Simmer for 10 minutes to make sauce|Warm tortillas and fill with chicken and cheese|Roll and place in a baking dish|Pour sauce over enchiladas, top with cheese, and bake at 180°C for 20 minutes

title: Tostadas Guatemala
description: Guatemalan tostadas made with crispy fried tortillas topped with beans, meat, cheese, and fresh vegetables.
ingredients: 8 corn tortillas|200g refried beans|300g chicken, cooked and shredded|100g queso fresco, crumbled|1 cup shredded cabbage|1 avocado, sliced|1/2 cup sour cream|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|Oil for frying
instructions: Fry tortillas in hot oil until crispy|Spread refried beans on each tortilla|Top with shredded chicken|Add cabbage, avocado, and cheese|Drizzle with sour cream|Garnish with cilantro and serve

title: Pollo en Crema
description: A rich Guatemalan dish where chicken is cooked in a creamy sauce with onions, peppers, and spices.
ingredients: 600g chicken, cut into pieces|200ml heavy cream|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 red bell peppers, diced|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp paprika|1 tsp salt|500ml chicken broth|3 tbsp oil
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet and brown the chicken|Add onions and garlic, sauté until soft|Add bell peppers and cook for 5 minutes|Pour in broth and bring to a simmer|Add cumin, paprika, and salt|Simmer for 20 minutes, then stir in cream and cook for 5 more minutes

title: Sopa de Lima Guatemala
description: A traditional Guatemalan soup made with chicken, lime juice, and fresh herbs. The lime adds a bright, tangy flavor.
ingredients: 500g chicken, cubed|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|4 limes, juiced|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|750ml chicken broth|2 tbsp oil
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot and sauté onions and garlic|Add chicken and cook until no longer pink|Pour in broth and bring to a boil|Add lime juice and cumin|Simmer for 15 minutes|Add cilantro and salt, simmer for 5 more minutes

title: Guacamol Guatemalteco
description: A traditional Guatemalan guacamole made with ripe avocados, tomatoes, onions, cilantro, and lime juice.
ingredients: 3 ripe avocados|2 tomatoes, diced|1 onion, finely diced|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|2 green chiles, minced|2 limes, juiced|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper
instructions: Cut avocados in half, remove pit, and scoop into a bowl|Mash with a fork until desired consistency|Add tomatoes, onion, cilantro, and green chiles|Add lime juice, salt, and pepper|Mix gently and adjust seasoning|Serve immediately

title: Ponche
description: A traditional Guatemalan warm drink made with dried fruits, spices, and sugar. Often served during celebrations.
ingredients: 2 liters water|100g dried guava, chopped|100g dried apple, chopped|50g dried plum, chopped|100g brown sugar|1 cinnamon stick|4 whole cloves|1 star anise|1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: Bring water to a boil in a large pot|Add dried fruits, cinnamon, cloves, and star anise|Simmer for 20 minutes|Add brown sugar and vanilla|Simmer for 5 more minutes|Strain and serve hot

title: Champurradas
description: A traditional Guatemalan drink made with corn flour and chocolate, served hot. Rich and satisfying.
ingredients: 1 liter milk|500ml water|100g corn flour|100g chocolate, chopped|100g sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|1/2 tsp cinnamon|1/4 tsp nutmeg
instructions: Mix corn flour with water until smooth|Heat milk in a large pot|Add corn mixture slowly, stirring constantly|Add chocolate, sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg|Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring frequently|Serve hot

title: Atol Chuco
description: A traditional Guatemalan savory drink made with corn flour, vegetables, and spices. Often served for breakfast.
ingredients: 1 liter chicken broth|500ml water|150g corn flour|1 onion, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 cup corn kernels|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|1/2 tsp black pepper|2 tbsp oil
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot and sauté onion and garlic|Mix corn flour with water until smooth|Add broth to the pot and bring to a boil|Add corn flour mixture slowly, stirring constantly|Add corn kernels, cumin, salt, and pepper|Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring frequently

title: Riz Gras Guinée
summary: Rich rice dish with meat and vegetables
description: A hearty Guinean rice dish cooked with beef, tomatoes, and aromatic spices, creating a flavorful one-pot meal.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|500g beef, cubed|3 large tomatoes, chopped|2 onions, diced|3 carrots, sliced|2 tbsp tomato paste|4 cups beef broth|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot and brown the beef on all sides.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.|Add tomatoes, carrots, and broth; bring to a boil.|Add rice, stir well, reduce heat and cover.|Simmer for 20-25 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.

title: Sauce Arachide Guinea
description: A traditional Guinean peanut butter sauce served with meat and vegetables, rich and satisfying.
ingredients: 500g chicken, cubed|1 cup natural peanut butter|4 cups water|2 onions, chopped|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cayenne pepper
instructions: Heat oil and brown chicken pieces.|Add onions and garlic, cook until softened.|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Stir in peanut butter and water, mixing well.|Add carrots and potatoes, bring to a boil.|Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes until vegetables are tender.|Season with salt and cayenne pepper.

title: Fouti
description: A savory Guinean stew made with cassava leaves, meat, and palm oil for authentic flavor.
ingredients: 500g beef, cubed|500g fresh cassava leaves, chopped|3 tbsp palm oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 cups beef broth|2 hot peppers, chopped|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat palm oil in a large pot.|Brown beef and set aside.|Sauté onions and garlic until fragrant.|Add tomatoes and hot peppers, cook for 5 minutes.|Return beef to pot, add broth and cassava leaves.|Simmer for 45 minutes until cassava leaves are tender.|Season with salt and pepper.

title: Poulet Yassa Guinea
summary: Marinated chicken with lemon and onions
description: Tender chicken marinated in lemon juice and onions, then grilled or pan-fried for a tangy Guinean classic.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces|6 lemons, juiced|4 large onions, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 bay leaves|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|½ tsp cayenne pepper|1 cup water
instructions: Combine lemon juice, onions, garlic, salt, pepper, and cayenne in a bowl.|Marinate chicken pieces for at least 2 hours.|Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.|Remove chicken from marinade and brown on all sides.|Pour marinade into skillet, add bay leaves and water.|Reduce heat and simmer for 30-35 minutes until chicken is cooked through.

title: Thieré Guinea
description: A nutritious Guinean grain dish made with millet couscous and seasonal vegetables.
ingredients: 2 cups millet couscous|4 cups vegetable broth|2 carrots, diced|1 cup green peas|1 onion, diced|3 tbsp butter|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Bring broth to a boil in a large pot.|Add millet couscous and stir well.|Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.|In a separate pan, melt butter and sauté onion and garlic.|Add carrots and peas, cook for 5 minutes.|Fold vegetables into couscous and season with salt and pepper.

title: Fufu Guinéen
description: A traditional Guinean staple made by pounding plantains and cassava into a smooth, starchy paste.
ingredients: 4 large plantains, peeled and cubed|2 cups cassava root, peeled and cubed|4 cups water|2 tbsp butter|1 tsp salt|½ tsp white pepper
instructions: Boil plantains and cassava in salted water for 20-25 minutes until very soft.|Drain well, reserving 1 cup cooking liquid.|Pound in a mortar and pestle or food processor until smooth.|Add butter and reserved liquid as needed to reach desired consistency.|Season with salt and white pepper.|Serve warm with sauce.

title: Boiled Yam Guinea
description: A basic yet satisfying Guinean side dish of boiled yam seasoned with salt and spices.
ingredients: 2 lbs yam, peeled and cubed|6 cups water|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|2 bay leaves|2 tbsp butter
instructions: Bring salted water to a boil in a large pot.|Add yam cubes and bay leaves.|Boil for 15-20 minutes until yam is tender.|Drain well and transfer to a serving dish.|Toss with butter, salt, and black pepper.|Serve hot.

title: Mafé Guinea
description: A rich and creamy Guinean peanut stew with tender beef and vegetables.
ingredients: 600g beef, cubed|¾ cup peanut butter|3 cups beef broth|2 onions, chopped|3 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cayenne pepper
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef on all sides.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until softened.|Stir in peanut butter and broth, mixing well.|Add carrots and potatoes.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 40 minutes.|Season with salt and cayenne pepper.

title: Kansiyé
description: A traditional Guinean okra stew with meat, tomatoes, and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 500g chicken, cubed|500g fresh okra, sliced|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 tomatoes, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 cups chicken broth|1 hot pepper, chopped|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat oil and brown chicken pieces.|Add onions and garlic, cook until softened.|Stir in tomatoes and hot pepper, cook for 5 minutes.|Add chicken broth and bring to a boil.|Add okra and reduce heat.|Simmer for 25-30 minutes until okra is tender.|Season with salt and pepper.

title: Gbegiri Guinea
description: A hearty Guinean bean soup enriched with meat and traditional spices.
ingredients: 2 cups dried beans, soaked overnight|400g beef, cubed|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|6 cups beef broth|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 bay leaves|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add drained beans, broth, and bay leaves.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve hot.

title: Tô de Maïs Guinea
description: A creamy Guinean cornmeal porridge, often served for breakfast or as a side dish.
ingredients: 2 cups cornmeal|6 cups water or milk|2 tbsp butter|1 tsp salt|½ tsp vanilla extract|2 tbsp sugar
instructions: Bring water or milk to a boil in a large pot.|Slowly whisk in cornmeal to avoid lumps.|Reduce heat and stir constantly for 15-20 minutes.|Add butter, salt, sugar, and vanilla.|Continue stirring until porridge reaches desired thickness.|Serve hot.

title: Bouille de Mil
description: A nutritious Guinean millet porridge, creamy and comforting.
ingredients: 1.5 cups millet flour|5 cups water|2 tbsp honey|1 tbsp butter|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cinnamon
instructions: Bring water to a boil in a large pot.|Slowly whisk in millet flour to prevent lumps.|Reduce heat and stir frequently for 20 minutes.|Add butter, honey, salt, and cinnamon.|Continue cooking for 5 more minutes.|Serve warm.

title: Cassava Leaf Stew Guinea
summary: Tender cassava leaves in savory stew
description: A nutritious Guinean stew featuring tender cassava leaves with meat and aromatic seasonings.
ingredients: 600g pork, cubed|600g fresh cassava leaves, chopped|3 tbsp palm oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 cups pork broth|2 hot peppers, chopped|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat palm oil in a large pot.|Brown pork and set aside.|Sauté onions and garlic until fragrant.|Add tomatoes and hot peppers, cook for 5 minutes.|Return pork to pot, add broth and cassava leaves.|Simmer for 50 minutes until cassava leaves are very tender.|Season with salt and pepper.

title: Chicken Brochette Guinea
description: Tender chicken pieces marinated in spices and grilled on skewers, a popular Guinean street food.
ingredients: 800g chicken breast, cubed|4 tbsp vegetable oil|3 tbsp lemon juice|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 onions, minced|1 tsp paprika|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cayenne pepper|½ tsp black pepper|Wooden skewers, soaked
instructions: Combine oil, lemon juice, garlic, onions, paprika, salt, cayenne, and black pepper.|Marinate chicken for at least 1 hour.|Thread chicken onto skewers.|Grill over medium-high heat for 12-15 minutes, turning occasionally.|Serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Groundnut Balls
description: Crispy fried balls made from ground peanuts and spices, a popular Guinean snack.
ingredients: 2 cups roasted peanuts, ground|1 cup all-purpose flour|2 eggs|½ cup water|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cayenne pepper|Oil for frying
instructions: Mix ground peanuts, flour, garlic, salt, and cayenne in a bowl.|Beat eggs with water and add to mixture.|Stir until a thick batter forms.|Heat oil in a deep pan to 350°F.|Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot oil.|Fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.|Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

title: Banana Fritters Guinea
description: Golden fried banana fritters dusted with cinnamon sugar, a delightful Guinean dessert.
ingredients: 6 ripe bananas, sliced|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 egg|½ cup milk|2 tbsp sugar|1 tsp baking powder|½ tsp vanilla extract|¼ tsp salt|Oil for frying|2 tbsp cinnamon sugar
instructions: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.|Whisk egg, milk, sugar, and vanilla together.|Combine wet and dry ingredients until smooth.|Heat oil to 350°F in a deep pan.|Dip banana slices in batter and fry until golden.|Drain on paper towels.|Dust with cinnamon sugar and serve warm.

title: Mint Tea Guinea
description: A traditional Guinean mint tea, served hot and sweetened, perfect for hospitality.
ingredients: 4 cups water|3 green tea bags|1 cup fresh mint leaves|3 tbsp honey|2 tbsp sugar|Lemon slices
instructions: Bring water to a boil.|Add green tea bags and mint leaves.|Steep for 5 minutes.|Strain into cups.|Stir in honey and sugar.|Serve hot with lemon slices.

title: Djenné Fish
description: A simple yet flavorful Guinean grilled fish dish, seasoned with traditional spices.
ingredients: 2 whole fish (1.5 lbs each), cleaned|4 tbsp vegetable oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 onions, sliced|3 lemons, juiced|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp paprika|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Make diagonal slashes on both sides of each fish.|Mix oil, garlic, lemon juice, salt, pepper, paprika, and hot peppers.|Rub mixture inside and outside fish.|Place onion slices inside fish cavity.|Grill over medium heat for 8-10 minutes per side.|Serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Tamarind Drink Guinea
description: A refreshing Guinean drink made from tamarind pulp, sweetened and served cold.
ingredients: 1 cup tamarind pulp|6 cups water|½ cup sugar|2 tbsp lemon juice|Ice cubes|Mint leaves for garnish
instructions: Soak tamarind pulp in 2 cups warm water for 15 minutes.|Strain through a fine sieve, pressing to extract juice.|Combine tamarind juice with remaining water.|Stir in sugar and lemon juice.|Chill thoroughly.|Serve over ice with mint leaves.

title: Foul Doukhi
description: A traditional Guinean bean paste seasoned with spices, served as a side or spread.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked fava beans|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tomatoes, chopped|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cayenne pepper|¼ cup water
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan.|Sauté onions and garlic until softened.|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add cooked beans, cumin, salt, and cayenne.|Mash beans with a potato masher to desired consistency.|Add water if needed for creaminess.|Simmer for 10 minutes and serve warm.

title: Pepperpot Guyana
description: A traditional Guyanese stew made with meat, cassava juice, and warm spices, often served during holidays.
ingredients: 1 kg beef, cubed|½ cup cassava juice|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 cinnamon sticks|4 cloves|2 bay leaves|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|3 cups beef broth
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef on all sides.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add cassava juice, broth, cinnamon, cloves, and bay leaves.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2 hours.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve hot.

title: Cook-Up Rice
description: A hearty Guyanese one-pot meal combining rice, beans, and coconut milk.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|1 cup dried kidney beans, soaked|1 can coconut milk|3 cups water|2 onions, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp vegetable oil|2 carrots, diced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onions and garlic.|Add drained beans and cook for 5 minutes.|Add rice, water, and coconut milk.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover.|Simmer for 25-30 minutes until rice is tender.|Stir in carrots and cook for 5 more minutes.|Season with salt and pepper.

title: Metem
description: A sweet Guyanese cassava cake made with grated cassava and coconut.
ingredients: 2 lbs fresh cassava, grated|1 cup grated coconut|1 cup brown sugar|4 tbsp butter, melted|1 tsp vanilla extract|½ tsp cinnamon|¼ tsp nutmeg|½ tsp salt|2 tbsp raisins
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.|Mix grated cassava, coconut, brown sugar, butter, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt.|Fold in raisins.|Pour into a greased 9x13 baking pan.|Bake for 45-50 minutes until golden brown.|Cool before cutting into squares.

title: Dhal Puri
description: A popular Guyanese Indian-influenced flatbread filled with spiced lentils.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 cup split peas flour|1 cup cooked split peas|3 tbsp vegetable oil|1 onion, minced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp turmeric|1 tsp salt|½ cup water
instructions: Mix flour, salt, and 1 tbsp oil.|Add water gradually to form dough.|Knead for 5 minutes and let rest.|Sauté onion and garlic in oil.|Add cooked split peas, cumin, and turmeric.|Divide dough into 8 portions and roll thin.|Place filling on each, fold, and roll again.|Cook on a hot griddle for 2-3 minutes per side.

title: Bake and Saltfish Guyana
description: A traditional Guyanese breakfast of fried bread served with seasoned salted fish.
ingredients: 500g salted cod, soaked overnight|3 cups all-purpose flour|1 tbsp baking powder|1 tsp salt|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 cup water|2 onions, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tomatoes, chopped|1 hot pepper, chopped|Oil for frying
instructions: Boil salted cod for 20 minutes, then flake.|Sauté onions, garlic, tomatoes, and hot pepper.|Add flaked fish and cook for 5 minutes.|Mix flour, baking powder, and salt.|Add oil and water to form dough.|Divide into portions and fry until golden.|Serve bake with fish mixture.

title: Curry Goat Guyana
description: A flavorful Guyanese curry made with tender goat meat and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1 kg goat meat, cubed|3 tbsp curry powder|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|2 cups water|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown goat meat.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in curry powder and cook for 2 minutes.|Add water, potatoes, carrots, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours.|Serve hot with rice.

title: Black Pudding Guyana
description: A traditional Guyanese blood sausage seasoned with spices and herbs.
ingredients: 500g pork blood|250g pork meat, minced|1 cup breadcrumbs|1 onion, minced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|½ tsp cayenne pepper|½ tsp black pepper|Pork casings
instructions: Mix pork blood, minced meat, breadcrumbs, onion, garlic, cilantro, cumin, salt, cayenne, and black pepper.|Stuff mixture into pork casings.|Tie ends securely.|Boil in water for 30 minutes.|Cool and slice.|Fry slices until crispy before serving.

title: Roti Guyana
description: A simple Guyanese flatbread, often served with curries and stews.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 tsp salt|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 cup warm water|Extra oil for cooking
instructions: Mix flour and salt in a bowl.|Add oil and water gradually, kneading until smooth.|Let dough rest for 30 minutes.|Divide into 8 portions and roll thin.|Heat a griddle or skillet over medium-high heat.|Cook each roti for 1-2 minutes per side until light brown.|Brush with oil and serve warm.

title: Chow Mein Guyana
description: A Guyanese-style chow mein with noodles, vegetables, and protein.
ingredients: 400g egg noodles|300g chicken, sliced|2 cups mixed vegetables|3 tbsp vegetable oil|3 cloves garlic, minced|3 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp oyster sauce|1 tsp sesame oil|2 green onions, chopped|1 hot pepper, chopped
instructions: Cook noodles according to package directions and drain.|Heat oil in a wok or large skillet.|Stir-fry chicken until cooked through.|Add garlic and vegetables, cook for 3 minutes.|Add noodles, soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.|Toss well and cook for 2 minutes.|Garnish with green onions and hot pepper.

title: Pine Tart
description: A sweet Guyanese tart with pineapple and custard filling.
ingredients: 1 pre-made pie crust|2 cups fresh pineapple, diced|1 cup sugar|3 tbsp cornstarch|1 cup milk|3 egg yolks|2 tbsp butter|1 tsp vanilla extract|¼ tsp nutmeg
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.|Place pie crust in a tart pan.|Cook pineapple with ½ cup sugar for 10 minutes.|Mix cornstarch with milk and egg yolks.|Heat remaining sugar and butter, add cornstarch mixture.|Cook until thickened, then add vanilla and nutmeg.|Fold in pineapple.|Pour into crust and bake for 25-30 minutes.

title: Garlic Pork
description: A savory Guyanese pork dish cooked with plenty of garlic and spices.
ingredients: 800g pork shoulder, cubed|8 cloves garlic, minced|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|3 tomatoes, chopped|1 cup water|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp vinegar|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown pork on all sides.|Add garlic and onions, sauté until fragrant.|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add water, soy sauce, vinegar, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour.|Serve hot with rice or roti.

title: Fried Plantain Guyana
description: A simple yet delicious Guyanese side of fried plantain slices.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, peeled and sliced|Oil for frying|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|½ tsp garlic powder
instructions: Heat oil to 350°F in a deep pan.|Fry plantain slices in batches until golden.|Drain on paper towels.|Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.|Serve hot.

title: Pholourie
description: A popular Guyanese Indian-influenced snack of fried chickpea fritters.
ingredients: 2 cups dried chickpeas, soaked overnight|1 onion, minced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp turmeric|1 hot pepper, minced|1 tsp salt|½ cup flour|Oil for frying
instructions: Drain chickpeas and grind coarsely.|Mix with onion, garlic, cilantro, cumin, turmeric, hot pepper, and salt.|Add flour to bind.|Heat oil to 350°F.|Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry until golden.|Drain on paper towels.|Serve with tamarind sauce.

title: Chicken Curry Guyana
description: A fragrant Guyanese chicken curry with potatoes and spices.
ingredients: 1 kg chicken, cubed|3 tbsp curry powder|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 potatoes, cubed|1 cup water|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown chicken.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in curry powder and cook for 2 minutes.|Add water, potatoes, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes.|Serve hot with rice or roti.

title: Cassava Bread
description: A traditional Guyanese cassava bread made from grated cassava root.
ingredients: 3 lbs fresh cassava, peeled and grated|1 tsp salt|2 tbsp vegetable oil
instructions: Squeeze excess moisture from grated cassava.|Mix with salt.|Heat a large griddle or cast iron skillet over medium heat.|Spread cassava mixture thin on griddle.|Cook for 10-15 minutes until edges are crispy.|Flip and cook other side.|Cool and break into pieces.

title: Mauby Drink
description: A traditional Guyanese herbal drink made from mauby bark, served cold.
ingredients: 2 oz dried mauby bark|6 cups water|½ cup sugar|1 cinnamon stick|3 cloves|1 tbsp anise seeds|Ice cubes
instructions: Boil water with mauby bark, cinnamon, cloves, and anise seeds for 15 minutes.|Strain through a fine sieve.|Stir in sugar until dissolved.|Cool to room temperature.|Chill thoroughly.|Serve over ice.

title: Sorrel Drink Guyana
description: A refreshing Guyanese drink made from sorrel flowers, sweetened and spiced.
ingredients: 2 cups dried sorrel flowers|8 cups water|¾ cup sugar|1 cinnamon stick|4 cloves|1 tbsp ginger, grated|Ice cubes
instructions: Boil water with sorrel flowers, cinnamon, cloves, and ginger for 15 minutes.|Strain through a fine sieve.|Stir in sugar until dissolved.|Cool to room temperature.|Chill thoroughly.|Serve over ice.

title: Bara
description: A popular Guyanese Indian-influenced fried bread, often served with curry.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 tbsp baking powder|1 tsp salt|1 onion, minced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 hot pepper, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|¾ cup water|Oil for frying
instructions: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt.|Add onion, garlic, hot pepper, and cilantro.|Add water gradually to form dough.|Let rest for 15 minutes.|Divide into portions and flatten.|Heat oil to 350°F.|Fry until golden brown on both sides.|Drain on paper towels.

title: Parsad
description: A traditional Guyanese sweet rice pudding made with coconut milk and spices.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|1 can coconut milk|4 cups water|1 cup sugar|4 tbsp butter|1 tsp vanilla extract|½ tsp cinnamon|¼ tsp nutmeg|½ tsp salt|2 tbsp raisins
instructions: Boil water and add rice.|Cook until rice is tender.|Add coconut milk, sugar, butter, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt.|Stir well and simmer for 10 minutes.|Fold in raisins.|Serve warm or cold.

title: Mithai
description: A traditional Guyanese Indian-influenced sweet made from condensed milk and spices.
ingredients: 2 cans sweetened condensed milk|1 can evaporated milk|4 tbsp butter|1 tsp vanilla extract|½ tsp cardamom powder|¼ tsp nutmeg|2 tbsp chopped pistachios
instructions: Combine condensed milk, evaporated milk, and butter in a heavy-bottomed pot.|Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly for 30-40 minutes.|Mixture should thicken and turn golden.|Add vanilla, cardamom, and nutmeg.|Pour onto a greased baking sheet.|Cool completely and cut into squares.|Garnish with pistachios.

title: Diri Kole
description: A traditional Haitian rice and beans dish cooked together until sticky and flavorful.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|1 cup dried kidney beans, soaked|4 cups water|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onions and garlic.|Add tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.|Add beans and water, bring to a boil.|Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.|Add rice, tomatoes, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Stir well, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes until rice is tender.|Serve hot.

title: Griot
description: A popular Haitian dish of marinated pork fried until crispy and served with sauce.
ingredients: 800g pork shoulder, cubed|6 lemons, juiced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 onions, minced|2 hot peppers, chopped|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|½ cup water|Oil for frying
instructions: Combine lemon juice, garlic, onions, hot peppers, oil, salt, pepper, and water.|Marinate pork for at least 2 hours.|Heat oil in a deep pan to 350°F.|Remove pork from marinade and fry until golden and crispy.|Drain on paper towels.|Serve with reserved marinade as sauce.

title: Bouillon Haiti
description: A traditional Haitian soup with beef, vegetables, and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 600g beef, cubed|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|2 cups cabbage, chopped|6 cups beef broth|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.|Add broth, carrots, potatoes, cabbage, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour.|Serve hot.

title: Tassot
description: A Haitian dish of fried beef seasoned with lime and spices.
ingredients: 800g beef, thinly sliced|6 limes, juiced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 onions, minced|2 hot peppers, chopped|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|Oil for frying
instructions: Combine lime juice, garlic, onions, hot peppers, oil, salt, and pepper.|Marinate beef for 1 hour.|Heat oil in a large skillet to 350°F.|Fry beef slices until crispy and cooked through.|Drain on paper towels.|Serve with reserved marinade.

title: Lambi
description: A traditional Haitian conch stew with vegetables and spices.
ingredients: 800g conch meat, cleaned and cubed|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|2 cups water|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onions and garlic.|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add conch, carrots, potatoes, water, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours.|Conch should be very tender.|Serve hot with rice.

title: Pain Patate
description: A traditional Haitian sweet potato bread, dense and flavorful.
ingredients: 2 lbs sweet potatoes, peeled and grated|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 cup sugar|4 tbsp butter, melted|2 eggs|1 tsp vanilla extract|½ tsp cinnamon|¼ tsp nutmeg|½ tsp salt|2 tbsp raisins
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.|Mix grated sweet potatoes, flour, sugar, butter, eggs, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt.|Fold in raisins.|Pour into a greased loaf pan.|Bake for 50-60 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.|Cool before slicing.

title: Mais Moulin
description: A creamy Haitian cornmeal porridge, often served for breakfast.
ingredients: 2 cups cornmeal|6 cups water or milk|2 tbsp butter|1 tsp salt|½ tsp vanilla extract|2 tbsp sugar|¼ tsp cinnamon
instructions: Bring water or milk to a boil.|Slowly whisk in cornmeal to avoid lumps.|Reduce heat and stir constantly for 15-20 minutes.|Add butter, salt, sugar, vanilla, and cinnamon.|Continue stirring until porridge reaches desired thickness.|Serve hot.

title: Poulet en Sauce Haiti
description: A traditional Haitian chicken dish cooked in a rich tomato sauce.
ingredients: 1 kg chicken, cut into pieces|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|4 tomatoes, chopped|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 cup water|2 carrots, sliced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown chicken.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.|Add tomatoes, water, carrots, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes.|Serve hot with rice.

title: Legume Haiti
description: A traditional Haitian vegetable stew with beans and spices.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (carrots, cabbage, spinach)|1 cup cooked beans|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tomatoes, chopped|2 cups vegetable broth|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onions and garlic.|Add tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add vegetables, beans, broth, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.|Serve hot with rice or bread.

title: Soup Joumou
description: A traditional Haitian pumpkin soup served during Independence Day celebrations.
ingredients: 2 lbs pumpkin, peeled and cubed|500g beef, cubed|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|6 cups beef broth|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Add pumpkin, carrots, potatoes, broth, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour.|Pumpkin should be very soft.|Serve hot.

title: Akra
description: A popular Haitian snack of fried bean fritters seasoned with spices.
ingredients: 2 cups dried black-eyed peas, soaked|1 onion, minced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 hot peppers, minced|2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|½ cup flour|Oil for frying
instructions: Drain peas and grind coarsely.|Mix with onion, garlic, hot peppers, parsley, salt, and pepper.|Add flour to bind.|Heat oil to 350°F.|Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry until golden.|Drain on paper towels.|Serve hot.

title: Bannann Peze
description: A simple Haitian side of fried plantain slices, crispy and delicious.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, peeled and sliced|Oil for frying|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper
instructions: Heat oil to 350°F in a deep pan.|Fry plantain slices in batches until golden.|Drain on paper towels.|Season with salt and pepper.|Serve hot.

title: Pen Patat
description: A traditional Haitian sweet potato pudding, creamy and comforting.
ingredients: 2 lbs sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed|1 can coconut milk|½ cup sugar|4 tbsp butter|1 tsp vanilla extract|½ tsp cinnamon|¼ tsp nutmeg|½ tsp salt|2 tbsp raisins
instructions: Boil sweet potatoes until very soft.|Mash with coconut milk, sugar, butter, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt.|Fold in raisins.|Transfer to a serving dish.|Serve warm or cold.

title: Griyo
description: A traditional Haitian dish of fried pork chunks served with sauce.
ingredients: 800g pork shoulder, cubed|6 limes, juiced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 onions, minced|2 hot peppers, chopped|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|Oil for frying
instructions: Combine lime juice, garlic, onions, hot peppers, oil, salt, and pepper.|Marinate pork for 1 hour.|Heat oil in a deep pan to 350°F.|Fry pork until golden and crispy.|Drain on paper towels.|Serve with reserved marinade.

title: Poul Nan Sos
description: A traditional Haitian chicken dish cooked in a rich brown sauce.
ingredients: 1 kg chicken, cut into pieces|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tbsp tomato paste|1 cup water|2 carrots, sliced|2 potatoes, cubed|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|2 hot peppers, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and brown chicken.|Add onions and garlic, sauté until fragrant.|Stir in tomato paste and cook for 3 minutes.|Add water, carrots, potatoes, salt, pepper, and hot peppers.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes.|Serve hot with rice.

title: Rum Punch Haiti
description: A traditional Haitian rum punch with tropical fruits and spices.
ingredients: 1 cup dark rum|2 cups pineapple juice|1 cup orange juice|½ cup lime juice|¼ cup sugar syrup|1 cinnamon stick|3 cloves|Ice cubes|Pineapple slices for garnish
instructions: Combine rum, pineapple juice, orange juice, and lime juice.|Add sugar syrup, cinnamon stick, and cloves.|Stir well and chill for at least 2 hours.|Remove cinnamon and cloves.|Serve over ice with pineapple slices.

title: Akasan
description: A traditional Haitian cornmeal drink, creamy and slightly sweet.
ingredients: 1 cup cornmeal|4 cups water|1 can evaporated milk|½ cup sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|½ tsp cinnamon|¼ tsp nutmeg
instructions: Mix cornmeal with 1 cup cold water to form a paste.|Bring remaining water to a boil.|Slowly add cornmeal paste, stirring constantly.|Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring frequently.|Add evaporated milk, sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg.|Simmer for 5 more minutes.|Serve hot or cold.

title: Fritay Mix
description: A Haitian assortment of fried snacks including plantains, fish, and vegetables.
ingredients: 2 green plantains, sliced|300g fish fillets|2 cups mixed vegetables|3 tbsp vegetable oil|2 onions, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|Oil for frying
instructions: Season fish with salt, pepper, onions, and garlic.|Heat oil to 350°F.|Fry plantain slices until golden.|Fry fish until cooked through.|Fry vegetables until tender.|Drain all items on paper towels.|Serve hot together.

title: Avocado Salad Haiti
description: A simple Haitian salad with fresh avocados, tomatoes, and lime dressing.
ingredients: 3 ripe avocados, sliced|3 tomatoes, diced|1 red onion, thinly sliced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped|3 tbsp lime juice|2 tbsp vegetable oil|1 tsp salt|½ tsp black pepper|1 hot pepper, minced
instructions: Arrange avocado slices on a serving plate.|Top with tomatoes and red onion.|Whisk together lime juice, oil, salt, pepper, and hot pepper.|Pour dressing over salad.|Garnish with cilantro.|Serve immediately.

title: Chadèque Drink
description: A traditional Haitian drink made from tropical fruits and spices.
ingredients: 2 cups fresh papaya, cubed|1 cup fresh mango, cubed|1 cup pineapple juice|½ cup lime juice|¼ cup sugar|1 cinnamon stick|2 cloves|Ice cubes|Mint leaves for garnish
instructions: Combine papaya, mango, pineapple juice, and lime juice.|Add sugar, cinnamon stick, and cloves.|Blend until smooth.|Strain through a fine sieve.|Chill thoroughly.|Serve over ice with mint leaves.

title: Plate Lunch
summary: Classic Hawaiian plate with protein, rice, and macaroni salad
description: A quintessential Hawaiian meal featuring a protein (usually kalua pork or chicken), two scoops of rice, and creamy macaroni salad. This iconic plate lunch originated from plantation workers and remains a staple of Hawaiian cuisine.
ingredients: 1 lb kalua pork or chicken|2 cups cooked white rice|2 cups cooked elbow macaroni|1 cup mayonnaise|1/2 cup diced onion|1/4 cup rice vinegar|2 tbsp sugar|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Cook rice and macaroni separately according to package directions|Mix mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, and diced onion for mac salad|Combine cooked macaroni with dressing and refrigerate|Warm the protein and arrange on plate with rice and mac salad|Serve immediately

title: Loco Moco
summary: Rice bowl topped with burger patty, fried egg, and gravy
description: A hearty Hawaiian comfort food consisting of a bed of rice topped with a beef patty, fried egg, and rich brown gravy. This dish is popular for breakfast and lunch throughout Hawaii.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white rice|4 ground beef patties (4 oz each)|4 eggs|2 tbsp butter|2 tbsp flour|1.5 cups beef broth|1/2 cup diced onion|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Cook rice and set aside|Brown ground beef patties in a skillet and set aside|Make gravy by melting butter, adding flour, then beef broth and onions|Fry eggs sunny-side up in butter|Assemble: rice base, beef patty, fried egg, and gravy on top

title: Kalua Pig
summary: Slow-roasted pork cooked in underground oven
description: Traditional Hawaiian kalua pig is slow-cooked in an underground imu oven until tender and smoky. This version uses a home oven for convenience while maintaining authentic flavors.
ingredients: 8 lb pork shoulder|2 tbsp sea salt|1 tbsp liquid smoke|2 cups water|4 banana leaves|1 tbsp garlic powder|1 tbsp onion powder
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F|Rub pork with salt, garlic powder, and onion powder|Line roasting pan with banana leaves|Place pork on leaves, add water and liquid smoke|Cover tightly with foil and roast for 6-8 hours until shredded|Shred meat and serve with cooking liquid

title: Poke Bowl Classic
summary: Raw ahi tuna marinated in soy and sesame
description: A fresh and vibrant Hawaiian poke bowl featuring cubed ahi tuna marinated in a savory soy-sesame mixture, served over rice with fresh vegetables and seaweed.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs sushi-grade ahi tuna, cubed|1/4 cup soy sauce|2 tbsp sesame oil|1 tbsp rice vinegar|1 tsp sriracha|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp sesame seeds|2 cups cooked sushi rice|Nori seaweed, cucumber, avocado
instructions: Combine soy sauce, sesame oil, vinegar, sriracha, and garlic|Add cubed tuna and marinate for 30 minutes|Prepare rice bowls|Top rice with marinated tuna and marinade|Garnish with sesame seeds, nori, cucumber, and avocado

title: Shave Ice
summary: Finely shaved ice with colorful syrups
description: A refreshing Hawaiian treat made from finely shaved ice topped with colorful fruit syrups. Often served with a scoop of ice cream at the bottom for extra sweetness.
ingredients: 4 cups ice|1/4 cup cherry syrup|1/4 cup blue raspberry syrup|1/4 cup lime syrup|2 scoops vanilla ice cream|1 tbsp condensed milk
instructions: Shave ice finely using a shave ice machine|Place ice cream scoop in serving cup|Top with shaved ice|Drizzle with syrups in desired pattern|Add condensed milk on top|Serve immediately with spoon

title: Malasadas
summary: Portuguese fried dough pastries with sugar coating
description: Sweet Portuguese-Hawaiian fried pastries dusted with cinnamon sugar. These pillowy dough balls are a beloved Hawaiian treat, especially popular during Malasada Day in February.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup sugar|1 tbsp baking powder|1/2 tsp salt|2 eggs|1/2 cup milk|2 tbsp melted butter|Oil for frying|1/4 cup cinnamon sugar for coating
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt|Whisk eggs, milk, and melted butter together|Combine wet and dry ingredients until dough forms|Heat oil to 350°F|Drop spoonfuls of dough into hot oil and fry until golden|Coat with cinnamon sugar while warm

title: Spam Musubi
summary: Grilled spam and rice wrapped in nori seaweed
description: A popular Hawaiian snack combining grilled spam, rice, and nori seaweed in a convenient hand-held form. This fusion dish reflects Hawaii's unique culinary heritage.
ingredients: 1 can spam, sliced|2 cups cooked sushi rice|4 sheets nori seaweed|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp sugar|1 tbsp mirin|1 tsp sesame oil
instructions: Mix soy sauce, sugar, mirin, and sesame oil|Grill spam slices until caramelized|Brush spam with sauce mixture|Place nori on bamboo mat, shiny side down|Add rice layer, then spam, then more rice|Roll tightly and slice into pieces

title: Lomi Lomi Salmon
summary: Salted salmon with tomato and onion
description: A traditional Hawaiian appetizer featuring salted salmon mixed with diced tomatoes and onions. The name "lomi lomi" means to massage or knead, referring to the preparation method.
ingredients: 1 lb salted salmon, diced|3 large tomatoes, diced|1 large onion, finely diced|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|2 tbsp lime juice|1 tbsp sesame oil|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Rinse salted salmon and dice into small pieces|Dice tomatoes and onions|Combine salmon, tomatoes, and onions in a bowl|Massage mixture gently for 2-3 minutes|Add cilantro, lime juice, and sesame oil|Season with salt and pepper and serve chilled

title: Poi
summary: Taro root paste, staple Hawaiian food
description: A traditional Hawaiian staple made from taro root that has been pounded into a smooth paste. Poi is a nutritious food with cultural significance in Hawaiian cuisine.
ingredients: 2 lbs fresh taro root|2 cups water|1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Peel and cube taro root|Boil taro in salted water for 20-25 minutes until very tender|Drain and reserve 1 cup cooking water|Pound taro with a poi pounder or food processor|Add cooking water gradually until desired consistency|Serve at room temperature

title: Kulolo
description: A traditional Hawaiian dessert made from taro root and coconut milk, steamed until set. This rich and creamy pudding is a beloved treat at Hawaiian gatherings.
ingredients: 2 lbs taro root, grated|1 can (14 oz) coconut milk|1 cup brown sugar|1/2 cup water|1/4 tsp salt|2 tbsp butter
instructions: Grate taro root finely|Mix coconut milk, brown sugar, water, and salt|Combine taro with coconut mixture|Pour into buttered baking dish|Steam for 45-60 minutes until set|Cool and cut into squares

title: Chicken Long Rice
summary: Chicken and glass noodles in savory broth
description: A comforting Hawaiian dish featuring tender chicken and delicate glass noodles in a savory broth. This one-pot meal is popular for family dinners and gatherings.
ingredients: 2 lbs chicken thighs, cubed|8 oz glass noodles|6 cups chicken broth|1 onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp ginger, minced|2 green onions, chopped
instructions: Heat oil and sauté garlic and ginger|Add chicken and brown on all sides|Pour in broth and add onion|Simmer for 20 minutes until chicken is cooked|Add glass noodles and soy sauce|Cook for 5 minutes until noodles are tender|Garnish with green onions

title: Saimin
summary: Hawaiian noodle soup with broth and toppings
description: A beloved Hawaiian noodle soup featuring thin egg noodles in a light broth, topped with various ingredients like char siu, fish cake, and green onions.
ingredients: 8 oz saimin noodles|6 cups dashi or chicken broth|4 oz char siu pork, sliced|4 fish cakes, sliced|2 eggs, beaten|2 green onions, chopped|1 tbsp soy sauce|1 tsp sesame oil
instructions: Bring broth to boil with soy sauce and sesame oil|Cook saimin noodles according to package directions|Drain noodles and place in serving bowls|Pour hot broth over noodles|Top with char siu, fish cakes, and green onions|Drizzle with sesame oil and serve immediately

title: Haupia
description: A traditional Hawaiian dessert made from coconut milk and cornstarch, creating a silky custard that is often cut into squares. This simple yet delicious treat is a staple at Hawaiian celebrations.
ingredients: 2 cans (14 oz each) coconut milk|1 cup sugar|1/2 cup cornstarch|1/4 cup water|1/4 tsp salt
instructions: Mix cornstarch and water until smooth|Combine coconut milk and sugar in a saucepan|Heat until sugar dissolves|Slowly add cornstarch mixture while stirring|Cook over medium heat until thickened, about 10 minutes|Pour into buttered 9x13 pan and cool completely|Cut into squares and serve

title: Portuguese Bean Soup Hawaii
summary: Hearty soup with beans, sausage, and vegetables
description: A warming Portuguese-Hawaiian soup featuring kidney beans, Portuguese sausage, and vegetables in a rich tomato broth. This comfort food is a favorite in Hawaiian homes.
ingredients: 1 lb Portuguese sausage, sliced|2 cans (15 oz each) kidney beans|1 can (28 oz) diced tomatoes|4 cups beef broth|2 potatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 carrots, diced|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Brown sausage in a large pot|Add onion, garlic, and carrots, sauté until soft|Add potatoes, beans, tomatoes, and broth|Bring to boil, then simmer for 30 minutes|Season with salt and pepper|Serve hot

title: Manapua
summary: Steamed buns with char siu pork filling
description: Soft steamed buns filled with savory char siu pork. These Chinese-Hawaiian treats are popular as snacks and are often sold at local bakeries and food stands.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1 tbsp sugar|1 tsp salt|1 tbsp baking powder|1 tbsp vegetable oil|1 cup warm water|1 lb char siu pork, diced|1/4 cup hoisin sauce|2 tbsp soy sauce
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder|Add oil and warm water to form dough|Knead until smooth and let rest 30 minutes|Mix char siu with hoisin and soy sauce|Divide dough into 8 portions and flatten|Fill with pork mixture and seal|Steam for 15 minutes until cooked through

title: Shrimp Scampi Plate
summary: Garlic butter shrimp served with rice and vegetables
description: A popular Hawaiian plate lunch featuring succulent shrimp cooked in garlic butter sauce, served with rice and fresh vegetables. This dish showcases Hawaii's fresh seafood.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs large shrimp, peeled|6 tbsp butter|8 cloves garlic, minced|1/4 cup white wine|2 tbsp lemon juice|2 cups cooked white rice|2 cups steamed broccoli|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Cook rice and steam broccoli|Melt butter in a large skillet|Add garlic and sauté for 1 minute|Add shrimp and cook until pink, about 3-4 minutes per side|Deglaze with white wine and lemon juice|Arrange on plate with rice and broccoli|Drizzle with pan sauce

title: Ahi Tuna Poke
summary: Seared ahi tuna with soy marinade
description: A premium version of poke featuring seared ahi tuna with a savory soy-based marinade. The seared exterior contrasts beautifully with the tender interior.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs sushi-grade ahi tuna, cubed|1/4 cup soy sauce|2 tbsp sesame oil|1 tbsp rice vinegar|1 tsp sriracha|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp sesame seeds|2 cups cooked sushi rice
instructions: Sear tuna cubes quickly in a hot skillet, 30 seconds per side|Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, vinegar, sriracha, garlic, and ginger|Marinate seared tuna for 15 minutes|Serve over sushi rice|Garnish with sesame seeds

title: Garlic Shrimp
summary: Shrimp cooked with abundant garlic and butter
description: A simple yet flavorful dish featuring shrimp cooked with generous amounts of garlic and butter. This dish is often served as a plate lunch with rice and vegetables.
ingredients: 2 lbs large shrimp, peeled|8 tbsp butter|12 cloves garlic, minced|1/4 cup white wine|2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped|1 tbsp lemon juice|Salt and pepper to taste|2 cups cooked white rice
instructions: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat|Add minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute|Add shrimp and cook for 2-3 minutes per side|Deglaze with white wine and lemon juice|Add parsley and season with salt and pepper|Serve over rice

title: Mocha Loco Moco
description: A creative twist on the classic loco moco, featuring a rich mocha-flavored gravy that adds depth and sophistication to this beloved Hawaiian comfort food.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white rice|4 ground beef patties (4 oz each)|4 eggs|3 tbsp butter|3 tbsp flour|1.5 cups beef broth|2 tbsp instant coffee|1 tbsp cocoa powder|1/2 cup diced onion
instructions: Cook rice and set aside|Brown beef patties and set aside|Make mocha gravy: melt butter, add flour, then broth|Whisk in instant coffee and cocoa powder|Add diced onion and simmer for 5 minutes|Fry eggs sunny-side up|Assemble: rice, beef patty, fried egg, mocha gravy

title: Lilikoi Chiffon Pie
description: A delicate and airy Hawaiian dessert featuring passion fruit (lilikoi) in a light chiffon pie. The tropical flavor and fluffy texture make this a favorite at Hawaiian celebrations.
ingredients: 1 graham cracker crust|1 cup lilikoi juice (passion fruit)|5 eggs, separated|3/4 cup sugar|1/4 cup cornstarch|1/4 tsp salt|1 tbsp gelatin|1/4 cup water|1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: Prepare graham cracker crust in 9-inch pie pan|Heat lilikoi juice and mix with egg yolks, sugar, cornstarch, and salt|Cook over medium heat until thickened, about 5 minutes|Dissolve gelatin in water and add to mixture|Cool to room temperature|Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form|Fold egg whites into lilikoi mixture|Pour into crust and refrigerate for 4 hours

title: Baleadas
summary: Flour tortillas filled with beans and cheese
description: A beloved Honduran street food consisting of soft flour tortillas filled with refried beans, cheese, and often topped with crema and pickled onions. This simple yet satisfying dish is eaten for breakfast or as a snack.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 tsp salt|2 tbsp butter|3/4 cup warm water|2 cups refried beans|2 cups shredded cheese|1/2 cup crema or sour cream|1 cup pickled onions
instructions: Mix flour and salt, cut in butter|Add warm water and knead until smooth|Divide into 8 portions and roll into thin circles|Cook tortillas on griddle until soft|Fill with refried beans and cheese|Fold in half and serve with crema and pickled onions

title: Sopa de Caracol
summary: Conch soup with coconut milk and seafood
description: A rich and flavorful Honduran seafood soup featuring conch, shrimp, and fish in a creamy coconut broth. This coastal specialty is popular in Caribbean regions of Honduras.
ingredients: 1 lb conch, cleaned and diced|1 lb shrimp, peeled|1 lb fish fillets, cubed|1 can (14 oz) coconut milk|4 cups seafood broth|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 potatoes, diced|1 tbsp cilantro, chopped|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion and garlic|Add conch and cook for 10 minutes|Add seafood broth and potatoes|Simmer for 15 minutes|Add shrimp and fish, cook for 5 minutes|Stir in coconut milk and cilantro|Season with salt and pepper and serve

title: Plato Típico
summary: Traditional Honduran plate with beans, rice, and protein
description: The quintessential Honduran meal featuring refried beans, white rice, fried cheese, fried plantains, and a protein like chicken or beef. This hearty plate represents the essence of Honduran home cooking.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white rice|2 cups refried beans|4 slices queso de freír (fried cheese)|2 plantains, sliced and fried|1 lb chicken or beef, cooked|1 avocado, sliced|1 tomato, sliced|Oil for frying|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Cook rice and beans|Fry cheese slices until golden|Slice and fry plantains until crispy|Cook protein of choice|Arrange all components on a large plate|Serve with avocado and tomato slices

title: Tapado Honduras
summary: Seafood stew with coconut and plantains
description: A traditional Honduran seafood stew combining fish, shrimp, and other seafood with coconut milk, plantains, and vegetables. This dish reflects the Caribbean influence in Honduran coastal cuisine.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs fish fillets, cubed|1 lb shrimp, peeled|1 can (14 oz) coconut milk|4 cups fish broth|2 plantains, sliced|2 potatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion and garlic|Add fish broth and bring to boil|Add potatoes and plantains|Simmer for 15 minutes|Add fish and shrimp, cook for 5 minutes|Stir in coconut milk and cilantro|Season and serve hot

title: Nacatamales
summary: Corn dough tamales with meat filling
description: A traditional Honduran tamale made with corn dough and filled with seasoned pork or chicken, wrapped in banana leaves. These are often prepared for special occasions and celebrations.
ingredients: 3 cups masa harina|1 cup lard|2 cups chicken broth|1 lb pork, cooked and shredded|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp achiote paste|20 banana leaves|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Beat lard until fluffy, add masa harina and broth|Mix pork with onion, garlic, and achiote paste|Soak banana leaves in hot water|Spread masa on banana leaf, add pork filling|Fold and wrap securely|Steam for 45 minutes until cooked through

title: Catrachitas
description: Crispy fried tortilla rounds topped with refried beans, cheese, crema, and pickled onions. These popular appetizers are served at celebrations and gatherings throughout Honduras.
ingredients: 8 corn tortillas|1 cup refried beans|1 cup shredded cheese|1/2 cup crema or sour cream|1 cup pickled onions|1/4 cup cilantro, chopped|Oil for frying|Salt to taste
instructions: Heat oil to 350°F|Fry tortillas until crispy|Spread refried beans on each tortilla|Top with cheese and crema|Add pickled onions and cilantro|Serve immediately

title: Carne Asada Honduras
description: Tender grilled beef marinated in a flavorful blend of citrus, garlic, and spices. This popular Honduran dish is often served with tortillas, beans, and fresh vegetables.
ingredients: 2 lbs beef sirloin, sliced thin|1/2 cup orange juice|1/4 cup lime juice|6 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp cumin|1 tbsp oregano|1/4 cup olive oil|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Mix orange juice, lime juice, garlic, cumin, oregano, and olive oil|Marinate beef for at least 2 hours|Heat grill to high temperature|Grill beef for 3-4 minutes per side|Rest for 5 minutes before serving|Serve with tortillas and vegetables

title: Mondongo Honduras
description: A traditional Honduran stew made with beef tripe, vegetables, and spices. This hearty dish is popular for breakfast and is believed to have restorative properties.
ingredients: 2 lbs beef tripe, cleaned and diced|4 cups beef broth|2 potatoes, diced|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 carrots, diced|1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Boil tripe for 1 hour until tender|Heat oil and sauté onion and garlic|Add cooked tripe and beef broth|Add potatoes, carrots, and tomatoes|Simmer for 30 minutes|Add cilantro and season with salt and pepper|Serve hot

title: Tamales Honduras
summary: Corn dough wrapped in corn husks
description: Traditional Honduran tamales made with corn dough and filled with chicken or pork, wrapped in corn husks. These are a staple at celebrations and holidays.
ingredients: 3 cups masa harina|1 cup lard|2 cups chicken broth|1 lb chicken, cooked and shredded|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|20 corn husks, soaked|1 tbsp cumin|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Beat lard until fluffy|Mix in masa harina and broth until light|Season chicken with onion, garlic, and cumin|Spread masa on corn husk|Add chicken filling in center|Fold husk and steam for 45 minutes

title: Chicharrones Honduras
description: Crispy fried pork belly pieces that are a beloved snack and appetizer in Honduras. Often served with tortillas, beans, and fresh lime.
ingredients: 2 lbs pork belly, cut into strips|2 tbsp salt|1 tbsp cumin|1 tbsp garlic powder|Oil for frying|Lime wedges for serving
instructions: Season pork belly with salt, cumin, and garlic powder|Heat oil to 350°F|Fry pork belly until golden and crispy, about 15 minutes|Drain on paper towels|Serve with lime wedges and tortillas

title: Yuca con Chicharrón
description: A hearty Honduran dish combining boiled yuca (cassava) with crispy fried pork. This simple yet satisfying meal is popular throughout Central America.
ingredients: 2 lbs yuca root, peeled and cubed|1.5 lbs pork belly, cubed|1 onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp cilantro, chopped|Salt and pepper to taste|Oil for frying
instructions: Boil yuca in salted water for 20 minutes until tender|Fry pork belly until crispy|Sauté onion and garlic in pork fat|Combine yuca and pork|Add cilantro and season with salt and pepper|Serve hot

title: Atol de Maíz Honduras
description: A traditional Honduran hot drink made from corn flour, milk, and spices. This comforting beverage is often served for breakfast or as a warm treat.
ingredients: 1 cup corn flour|4 cups milk|2 cups water|1/4 cup sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|1/2 tsp cinnamon|Pinch of nutmeg
instructions: Mix corn flour with 1 cup cold water until smooth|Heat remaining water and milk together|Slowly add corn flour mixture to hot liquid, stirring constantly|Add sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg|Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally|Serve hot

title: Horchata Honduras
summary: Rice and milk beverage with spices
description: A refreshing Honduran beverage made from rice, milk, and spices. This sweet drink is popular as a breakfast beverage and is often served cold.
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked rice|4 cups milk|2 cups water|1/4 cup sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|1/2 tsp cinnamon|Pinch of nutmeg|Ice for serving
instructions: Soak rice in water for 30 minutes|Blend rice and water until smooth|Strain through fine mesh|Mix rice liquid with milk and sugar|Add vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg|Chill and serve over ice

title: Pinol
description: A traditional Honduran drink made from roasted corn flour and cacao. This nutritious beverage is often served as a breakfast drink or energy booster.
ingredients: 1 cup corn flour, roasted|2 tbsp cacao powder|4 cups milk|2 cups water|1/4 cup sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|Pinch of salt
instructions: Mix corn flour and cacao powder|Heat water and milk together|Whisk corn and cacao mixture into hot liquid|Add sugar, vanilla, and salt|Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring constantly|Serve hot

title: Rosquillas Honduras
description: Traditional Honduran fried pastries shaped like rings and flavored with anise. These crispy treats are often served with coffee or hot chocolate.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup sugar|1 tbsp baking powder|1/2 tsp salt|2 eggs|1/4 cup milk|2 tbsp melted butter|1 tbsp anise seeds|Oil for frying
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and anise seeds|Whisk eggs, milk, and melted butter together|Combine wet and dry ingredients|Roll dough and cut into ring shapes|Fry in 350°F oil until golden|Drain and serve

title: Riguas Honduras
description: Traditional Honduran pastries made with corn dough and filled with cheese. These are often served as a breakfast item or snack.
ingredients: 2 cups corn flour|1 cup all-purpose flour|1 tsp salt|1 tbsp sugar|1 tbsp baking powder|1/2 cup melted butter|1 cup warm water|2 cups shredded cheese|Oil for frying
instructions: Mix corn flour, all-purpose flour, salt, sugar, and baking powder|Add melted butter and warm water to form dough|Divide into portions and flatten|Fill with shredded cheese|Fold and seal|Fry in 350°F oil until golden

title: Enchiladas Honduras
summary: Rolled tortillas with sauce and toppings
description: Honduran-style enchiladas made with corn tortillas, filled with chicken or cheese, and topped with a savory sauce and fresh toppings.
ingredients: 12 corn tortillas|2 cups cooked chicken, shredded|2 cups enchilada sauce|2 cups shredded cheese|1/2 cup crema or sour cream|1 onion, sliced|1/4 cup cilantro, chopped|Oil for frying
instructions: Heat oil and soften tortillas|Dip tortillas in enchilada sauce|Fill with chicken and cheese|Roll and place seam-side down in baking dish|Pour remaining sauce over enchiladas|Top with cheese and bake at 350°F for 25 minutes|Serve with crema, onion, and cilantro

title: Tajadas
description: Crispy fried plantain slices that are a popular side dish and snack in Honduras. These are often served with beans, rice, or as an accompaniment to main dishes.
ingredients: 4 plantains, peeled and sliced|Oil for frying|Salt to taste
instructions: Heat oil to 350°F|Fry plantain slices until golden on both sides|Drain on paper towels|Season with salt|Serve hot

title: Licuado de Melón
description: A refreshing Honduran smoothie made with fresh cantaloupe, milk, and sugar. This cooling beverage is popular during hot weather.
ingredients: 1 ripe cantaloupe, peeled and cubed|2 cups milk|1/4 cup sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|Ice cubes
instructions: Blend cantaloupe with milk and sugar|Add vanilla extract|Blend until smooth|Serve over ice

title: Arroz con Leche Honduras
description: A creamy and comforting Honduran rice pudding flavored with cinnamon and vanilla. This traditional dessert is often served warm or cold.
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked rice|4 cups milk|1/2 cup sugar|1/4 cup raisins|1 tsp vanilla extract|1 tsp cinnamon|2 tbsp butter|Pinch of salt
instructions: Cook rice in 2 cups water until tender|Heat milk with sugar and butter|Add cooked rice to milk mixture|Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally|Add raisins, vanilla, cinnamon, and salt|Cook for 5 more minutes|Serve warm or cold

title: Char Siu
summary: Glazed roasted pork with sweet and savory sauce
description: A classic Cantonese dish featuring tender pork roasted until caramelized and glazed with a sweet and savory sauce. This iconic dish is used in many Hong Kong dishes and is a staple of dim sum restaurants.
ingredients: 2 lbs pork shoulder, cut into strips|1/4 cup hoisin sauce|3 tbsp soy sauce|2 tbsp honey|1 tbsp sesame oil|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp ginger, minced|1 tsp five-spice powder
instructions: Mix hoisin, soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and five-spice|Marinate pork for at least 2 hours|Roast at 400°F for 30-35 minutes, basting occasionally|Cool slightly and slice|Serve with rice or noodles

title: Har Gow Dim Sum
description: Delicate shrimp dumplings with a translucent wheat starch wrapper. These are a signature dim sum item in Hong Kong, known for their tender texture and succulent shrimp filling.
ingredients: 1 cup wheat starch|1/2 cup tapioca starch|3/4 cup boiling water|1 tbsp oil|1 lb shrimp, minced|2 oz pork fat, minced|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tsp sesame oil|1 tbsp cornstarch
instructions: Mix wheat starch and tapioca starch|Add boiling water and oil, knead until smooth|Mix shrimp, pork fat, soy sauce, sesame oil, and cornstarch|Divide dough into 12 portions and flatten|Fill with shrimp mixture and pleat edges|Steam for 8-10 minutes until translucent

title: Egg Tart HK
description: A beloved Hong Kong pastry featuring a crispy, flaky crust filled with a smooth egg custard. These iconic tarts are a staple of Hong Kong bakeries and dim sum restaurants.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup butter|1/4 cup sugar|1 egg yolk|1/4 cup water|3 eggs|1/2 cup sugar|1/2 cup evaporated milk|1/4 cup water
instructions: Make pastry: mix flour, butter, sugar, egg yolk, and water|Chill for 30 minutes|Press into tart molds|Make custard: whisk eggs, sugar, evaporated milk, and water|Pour custard into pastry shells|Bake at 400°F for 15-20 minutes until golden

title: Wonton Noodle Soup
summary: Thin noodles with wontons in broth
description: A classic Hong Kong comfort food featuring thin egg noodles and pork wontons in a light, flavorful broth. This simple yet satisfying dish is a staple of Hong Kong noodle shops.
ingredients: 8 oz wonton wrappers|1/2 lb shrimp, minced|1/4 lb pork, minced|2 green onions, chopped|1 tbsp soy sauce|1 tsp sesame oil|8 oz egg noodles|6 cups chicken broth|Bok choy for garnish
instructions: Mix shrimp, pork, green onions, soy sauce, and sesame oil|Fill wonton wrappers and fold into wontons|Boil wontons in water until they float|Cook noodles separately|Heat broth and pour into bowls|Add noodles and wontons|Garnish with bok choy

title: Roast Goose HK
summary: Crispy roasted goose with savory glaze
description: A premium Hong Kong dish featuring a whole goose roasted until the skin is crispy and golden. The meat is tender and flavorful, often served with a savory dipping sauce.
ingredients: 1 whole goose (5-6 lbs)|1/4 cup soy sauce|2 tbsp honey|1 tbsp sesame oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp ginger, minced|1 tsp five-spice powder|2 cups water
instructions: Clean and dry goose thoroughly|Mix soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and five-spice|Rub mixture inside and outside goose|Roast at 375°F for 2-2.5 hours, basting occasionally|Cool slightly and chop into pieces|Serve with dipping sauce

title: Cheung Fun
description: Delicate steamed rice noodle rolls filled with shrimp, pork, or vegetables. These silky rolls are a popular dim sum item, often served with a savory soy sauce.
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour|1/2 cup tapioca starch|1.5 cups water|1 tbsp oil|1/2 lb shrimp, minced|1/4 lb pork, minced|2 green onions, chopped|1 tbsp soy sauce
instructions: Mix rice flour, tapioca starch, water, and oil|Steam batter in a flat pan for 2-3 minutes until set|Mix shrimp, pork, green onions, and soy sauce|Spread filling on rice noodle sheet|Roll tightly and cut into pieces|Serve with soy sauce

title: Siu Mai HK
description: A signature dim sum dumpling featuring a mixture of pork and shrimp wrapped in a thin wonton wrapper with the top left open. These are steamed until tender and served with soy sauce.
ingredients: 24 wonton wrappers|1/2 lb pork, minced|1/4 lb shrimp, minced|2 oz pork fat, minced|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tsp sesame oil|1 tbsp cornstarch|1 green onion, chopped|1 tbsp mushroom, minced
instructions: Mix pork, shrimp, pork fat, soy sauce, sesame oil, cornstarch, green onion, and mushroom|Place wonton wrapper in palm|Add filling and gather edges, leaving top open|Steam for 8-10 minutes|Serve with soy sauce

title: Lo Mai Gai
summary: Sticky rice with chicken in lotus leaf
description: Glutinous rice mixed with chicken, Chinese sausage, and mushrooms, wrapped in a lotus leaf and steamed. This aromatic dim sum dish is a favorite among Hong Kong diners.
ingredients: 2 cups glutinous rice|1.5 cups chicken broth|1 lb chicken, diced|2 Chinese sausages, sliced|4 dried mushrooms, soaked and diced|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp sesame oil|4 lotus leaves, soaked
instructions: Cook glutinous rice with chicken broth|Mix cooked rice with chicken, sausage, mushrooms, soy sauce, and sesame oil|Divide mixture among lotus leaves|Wrap securely and steam for 20 minutes|Serve in lotus leaf

title: Pineapple Bun
description: A beloved Hong Kong bakery item featuring a soft, sweet bun topped with a crispy, pineapple-patterned crust. Despite the name, there is no pineapple in the bun.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup sugar|1 tbsp baking powder|1/2 tsp salt|2 eggs|1/2 cup milk|2 tbsp melted butter|1 cup all-purpose flour|1/2 cup butter|1/4 cup sugar
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, eggs, milk, and melted butter|Knead until smooth and let rise for 1 hour|Make topping: mix flour, butter, and sugar until crumbly|Divide dough into 8 portions|Top each with crumbly mixture|Bake at 375°F for 20-25 minutes until golden

title: Milk Tea Hong Kong
description: A signature Hong Kong beverage made with strong black tea and evaporated milk. This creamy, slightly sweet drink is a staple of Hong Kong cafes and is often served hot or cold.
ingredients: 4 tbsp black tea leaves|2 cups boiling water|1/2 cup evaporated milk|2 tbsp condensed milk|Ice (for iced version)
instructions: Steep black tea in boiling water for 3-5 minutes|Strain tea into a cup|Add evaporated milk and condensed milk|Stir well|Serve hot or over ice

title: Congee HK
description: A comforting Hong Kong breakfast dish made from rice cooked until it breaks down into a creamy porridge. It is typically served with various toppings like preserved vegetables, eggs, and meat.
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked rice|8 cups chicken broth|1/2 lb chicken, diced|2 eggs|1/4 cup preserved vegetables, chopped|2 green onions, chopped|1 tbsp soy sauce|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Bring broth to boil and add rice|Simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally|Add chicken and cook for 10 minutes|Beat eggs and slowly pour into congee while stirring|Add preserved vegetables and soy sauce|Season with salt and pepper|Garnish with green onions

title: Fish Ball Noodles
summary: Noodles with fish balls in broth
description: A popular Hong Kong street food featuring thin noodles and homemade fish balls in a light, flavorful broth. The fish balls are tender and have a delicate flavor.
ingredients: 1 lb white fish fillets, minced|2 tbsp cornstarch|1 egg white|1 tsp sesame oil|8 oz egg noodles|6 cups fish broth|2 green onions, chopped|Bok choy for garnish
instructions: Mix minced fish, cornstarch, egg white, and sesame oil|Form into balls and boil until they float|Cook noodles separately|Heat broth and pour into bowls|Add noodles and fish balls|Garnish with green onions and bok choy

title: Crispy Roast Pork HK
description: A Hong Kong specialty featuring pork roasted until the skin is crispy and golden while the meat remains tender and juicy. This dish is often served with rice and a savory dipping sauce.
ingredients: 3 lbs pork belly|2 tbsp salt|1 tbsp five-spice powder|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp honey|1 tbsp sesame oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp ginger, minced
instructions: Score pork skin in a crosshatch pattern|Rub with salt and five-spice powder|Mix soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger|Rub mixture on pork meat side|Roast at 425°F for 45-50 minutes until skin is crispy|Cool slightly and slice|Serve with rice

title: Turnip Cake
description: A savory dim sum dish made from grated turnip (daikon radish) mixed with rice flour and topped with crispy fried shallots and preserved vegetables. This is often pan-fried until golden.
ingredients: 1 lb daikon radish, grated|1 cup rice flour|1/2 cup water|1/4 lb Chinese sausage, diced|2 oz dried shrimp, soaked and chopped|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp sesame oil|2 green onions, chopped|Oil for frying
instructions: Mix grated radish, rice flour, and water|Add sausage, shrimp, soy sauce, sesame oil, and green onions|Steam in a rectangular pan for 30 minutes|Cool and cut into slices|Pan-fry until golden on both sides|Serve with soy sauce

title: Beef Brisket Noodle
summary: Tender beef brisket with noodles in spiced broth
description: A beloved Hong Kong noodle dish featuring tender beef brisket braised in a fragrant spiced broth, served with thin noodles. This comfort food is popular for lunch and dinner.
ingredients: 2 lbs beef brisket, cubed|8 oz egg noodles|6 cups beef broth|3 tbsp soy sauce|2 tbsp oyster sauce|1 tbsp sesame oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tbsp ginger, minced|2 star anise|1 cinnamon stick
instructions: Blanch beef brisket and rinse|Heat oil and sauté garlic and ginger|Add beef and brown slightly|Add broth, soy sauce, oyster sauce, star anise, and cinnamon|Simmer for 1.5 hours until beef is tender|Cook noodles separately|Serve noodles in bowls with beef and broth

title: Curry Fish Balls
description: A popular Hong Kong street food featuring homemade fish balls served in a creamy curry sauce. These are often eaten on skewers as a quick snack.
ingredients: 1 lb white fish fillets, minced|2 tbsp cornstarch|1 egg white|1 tsp sesame oil|2 tbsp curry powder|1 can (14 oz) coconut milk|1 cup fish broth|1 onion, sliced|2 tbsp soy sauce
instructions: Mix minced fish, cornstarch, egg white, and sesame oil|Form into balls and boil until they float|Heat oil and sauté onion|Add curry powder and cook for 1 minute|Add coconut milk and fish broth|Add fish balls and soy sauce|Simmer for 10 minutes and serve

title: Pandan Waffle
description: A Southeast Asian-influenced Hong Kong treat featuring a crispy waffle infused with pandan leaf flavor and topped with a sweet glaze. These are often sold at Hong Kong street stalls.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup sugar|1 tbsp baking powder|1/2 tsp salt|2 eggs|1/2 cup milk|2 tbsp melted butter|1/4 cup pandan extract|Oil for waffle iron
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt|Whisk eggs, milk, melted butter, and pandan extract|Combine wet and dry ingredients|Heat waffle iron and brush with oil|Pour batter and cook until golden|Serve warm with sweet glaze

title: Egg Waffles
summary: Crispy waffle with egg custard flavor
description: A beloved Hong Kong street snack featuring a crispy, golden waffle with a unique egg-shaped pattern. These are often served warm with a sweet glaze or condensed milk.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1/2 cup sugar|1 tbsp baking powder|1/2 tsp salt|3 eggs|1/2 cup milk|2 tbsp melted butter|1 tsp vanilla extract|Oil for waffle iron
instructions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt|Whisk eggs, milk, melted butter, and vanilla|Combine wet and dry ingredients|Heat waffle iron and brush with oil|Pour batter into egg-shaped molds|Cook until golden and crispy|Serve warm with condensed milk

title: Dan Dan Noodles HK
summary: Spicy sesame noodles with ground meat
description: A Hong Kong adaptation of the Sichuan classic, featuring thin noodles coated in a spicy sesame sauce and topped with ground pork. This dish offers a perfect balance of heat and flavor.
ingredients: 8 oz egg noodles|1/2 lb ground pork|3 tbsp sesame paste|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp rice vinegar|1 tbsp chili oil|1 tsp Sichuan peppercorn powder|2 green onions, chopped|2 cloves garlic, minced
instructions: Cook noodles and drain|Heat oil and sauté garlic|Add ground pork and cook until done|Mix sesame paste, soy sauce, vinegar, chili oil, and Sichuan pepper|Toss noodles with sauce|Top with cooked pork and green onions|Serve at room temperature

title: Stinky Tofu HK
description: A Hong Kong delicacy featuring fermented tofu with a distinctive pungent aroma and complex flavor. Despite its smell, stinky tofu is beloved by locals and is often served fried or in soup.
ingredients: 1 block stinky tofu (14 oz)|2 tbsp soy sauce|1 tbsp chili sauce|1 tbsp sesame oil|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 green onion, chopped|Oil for frying
instructions: Cut stinky tofu into cubes|Heat oil in a wok or skillet|Fry tofu until golden on all sides|Mix soy sauce, chili sauce, sesame oil, and garlic|Pour sauce over fried tofu|Garnish with green onion|Serve immediately

title: Skyr
description: A traditional Icelandic cultured dairy product similar to Greek yogurt but with a unique tangy flavor and creamy texture. Served plain or with berries and granola.
ingredients: 1 liter whole milk|1/4 cup skyr starter culture|2 tablespoons sugar|1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract|Fresh berries for serving|Granola for topping
instructions: Heat milk to 86°F (30°C) and add starter culture|Let sit undisturbed for 8-10 hours until set|Strain through cheesecloth for 4-6 hours|Mix in sugar and vanilla, serve chilled with berries and granola

title: Hákarl
description: An ancient Icelandic dish of fermented Greenland shark that has been buried and aged for months. Known for its pungent ammonia smell and acquired taste.
ingredients: 2 kg Greenland shark meat|Sea salt for curing|Cloth for wrapping|Wooden box for fermentation
instructions: Clean shark meat and remove skin|Bury in sand or soil for 6-12 weeks|Dig up and hang to dry for several months|Cut into cubes and serve as a traditional delicacy

title: Plokkfiskur
description: A comforting traditional Icelandic fish stew made with fresh fish, potatoes, and onions in a creamy sauce. Perfect for cold Icelandic winters.
ingredients: 800g fresh white fish fillets|4 medium potatoes, diced|2 large onions, chopped|400ml fish stock|200ml heavy cream|3 tablespoons butter|2 tablespoons flour|Salt and pepper to taste|Fresh parsley for garnish
instructions: Melt butter and sauté onions until soft|Sprinkle flour and cook for 1 minute|Add stock and potatoes, simmer for 15 minutes|Add fish pieces and cream, cook for 8-10 minutes until fish is tender|Season with salt and pepper, garnish with parsley

title: Skyr Cake
description: A light and fluffy cake made with Icelandic skyr, creating a moist crumb and tangy flavor. Often topped with berries and whipped cream.
ingredients: 2 cups skyr|200g flour|150g sugar|3 eggs|100ml vegetable oil|2 teaspoons baking powder|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|Fresh berries for topping|Whipped cream for serving
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C)|Mix skyr, eggs, oil, and vanilla until smooth|Fold in flour, sugar, and baking powder|Pour into greased pan and bake for 30-35 minutes|Cool and top with berries and whipped cream

title: Kjötsúpa
description: A hearty Icelandic soup made with lamb or beef, root vegetables, and barley. A warming comfort food that has been eaten for centuries.
