instructions: Blend chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice, and 50 ml olive oil until smooth.|Season hummus with salt and pepper, spread on a serving platter.|Heat remaining olive oil and brown ground meat with onion.|Season meat with cumin, salt, and pepper.|Spread yogurt over the hummus.|Top with cooked meat mixture.|Scatter fried bread pieces and pine nuts over the top.|Serve immediately with warm flatbread.

title: Lamb Ouzi
summary: Rolled pastry with spiced lamb and rice filling
description: A festive Palestinian dish featuring thin pastry rolled with a savory filling of spiced lamb, rice, and nuts. Baked until crispy and golden, it makes an impressive main course.
ingredients: 500 g lamb, ground|1 cup long-grain rice, cooked|1 large onion, finely diced|100 g pine nuts|100 ml olive oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp allspice|1 tsp cinnamon|8 sheets phyllo pastry|100 ml melted butter
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown ground lamb with onion and garlic.|Add cooked rice, pine nuts, and spices.|Cool the filling slightly.|Brush phyllo sheets with melted butter.|Place filling on one end of each sheet and roll tightly, tucking in sides.|Place rolls seam-side down on a greased baking sheet.|Brush with remaining butter.|Bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes until golden brown.|Serve hot.

title: Dagga Salad Palestine
summary: Herb and vegetable salad with olive oil dressing
description: A fresh and vibrant Palestinian salad made with an abundance of fresh herbs, tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions, dressed simply with olive oil and lemon juice. A perfect side dish for any meal.
ingredients: 2 bunches fresh parsley, chopped|1 bunch fresh mint, chopped|1 bunch fresh cilantro, chopped|4 large tomatoes, diced|2 cucumbers, diced|1 red onion, finely diced|150 ml olive oil|100 ml lemon juice|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp sumac
instructions: Combine all fresh herbs in a large bowl.|Add diced tomatoes, cucumbers, and red onion.|In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and sumac.|Pour dressing over the salad and toss gently.|Let sit for 10 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.|Serve at room temperature.

title: Muhalabi Palestine
summary: Creamy milk pudding with rose water and pistachios
description: A delicate Palestinian dessert made with a smooth, creamy milk pudding flavored with rose water and topped with pistachios and coconut. Light and elegant, perfect after a heavy meal.
ingredients: 1 liter whole milk|100 g cornstarch|100 g sugar|2 tbsp rose water|1 tbsp orange blossom water|100 g pistachios, chopped|50 g shredded coconut|1 pinch saffron|2 tbsp butter|1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: Mix cornstarch with 200 ml cold milk until smooth.|Heat remaining milk with sugar until steaming.|Slowly pour cornstarch mixture into hot milk, stirring constantly.|Continue cooking and stirring until mixture thickens, about 5-7 minutes.|Remove from heat and stir in rose water, orange blossom water, butter, and vanilla.|Pour into serving bowls and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.|Top with chopped pistachios and shredded coconut before serving.

title: Kaak
summary: Sesame-topped bread rings with anise flavor
description: A traditional Palestinian bread shaped into rings and topped with sesame seeds. Flavored with anise, these bread rings are perfect for breakfast or as a snack with cheese and olives.
ingredients: 500 g all-purpose flour|7 g instant yeast|250 ml warm water|2 tsp salt|2 tbsp sugar|2 tbsp olive oil|1 tbsp anise seeds|100 g sesame seeds|1 egg, beaten|2 tbsp milk
instructions: Mix flour, yeast, salt, sugar, and anise seeds in a bowl.|Add warm water and olive oil, knead until smooth dough forms.|Let dough rise for 1 hour until doubled in size.|Divide dough into 12 equal pieces.|Roll each piece into a rope and form into a ring, sealing the ends.|Mix beaten egg with milk for egg wash.|Brush rings with egg wash and coat with sesame seeds.|Place on greased baking sheets and let rise for 30 minutes.|Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden brown.

title: Taboon Bread
summary: Traditional Palestinian flatbread baked in a clay oven
description: An authentic Palestinian flatbread traditionally baked in a taboon (clay oven). With a soft interior and slightly crispy exterior, it is the foundation for many Palestinian meals.
ingredients: 500 g all-purpose flour|7 g instant yeast|300 ml warm water|2 tsp salt|1 tbsp sugar|2 tbsp olive oil|1 tbsp nigella seeds (optional)|1 tbsp sesame seeds (optional)
instructions: Mix flour, yeast, salt, and sugar in a bowl.|Add warm water and olive oil, knead until smooth dough forms.|Let dough rise for 1 hour until doubled in size.|Divide dough into 6 equal pieces and shape into balls.|Let rest for 15 minutes.|Flatten each ball into a round, about 1/4 inch thick.|Sprinkle with nigella and sesame seeds if desired.|Bake on a preheated baking stone or sheet at 220°C for 8-10 minutes until puffed and lightly browned.

title: Harira Palestine
summary: Hearty lentil and tomato soup with spices
description: A warming Palestinian soup made with lentils, tomatoes, and aromatic spices. Often served during Ramadan to break the fast, it is nutritious and deeply satisfying.
ingredients: 2 cups red lentils|1 kg tomatoes, diced|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|8 cups vegetable or chicken broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp coriander|1 tsp paprika|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add diced tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Add lentils and broth, bring to a boil.|Add cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper.|Reduce heat and simmer for 30-35 minutes until lentils are very soft.|Stir occasionally and mash some lentils to thicken the soup.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Makdous Palestine
summary: Pickled eggplant stuffed with walnuts and garlic
description: A traditional Palestinian preserve made with small eggplants stuffed with walnuts, garlic, and red pepper, then pickled in olive oil. Served as an appetizer or side dish.
ingredients: 1 kg small eggplants|300 g walnuts, finely chopped|8 cloves garlic, minced|2 red chili peppers, finely chopped|500 ml olive oil|100 ml white vinegar|2 tbsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp coriander seeds|2 bay leaves
instructions: Boil eggplants in salted water for 10 minutes until slightly softened.|Drain and cool, then make a slit lengthwise in each eggplant.|Mix walnuts, garlic, red chili, salt, and pepper for filling.|Stuff each eggplant with the walnut mixture.|Pack stuffed eggplants into sterilized jars.|Heat olive oil with vinegar, coriander seeds, and bay leaves.|Pour hot oil mixture over eggplants to cover completely.|Seal jars and let cool, then refrigerate for at least 2 weeks before serving.

title: Olive Oil Cake Palestine
summary: Moist cake made with quality olive oil and citrus
description: A simple yet elegant Palestinian cake made with high-quality olive oil, creating a moist crumb. Flavored with lemon and orange, it is perfect with coffee or tea.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|2 tsp baking powder|1 tsp salt|3 eggs|150 g sugar|200 ml olive oil|100 ml milk|2 tbsp lemon zest|1 tbsp orange zest|100 ml lemon juice|50 ml orange juice|1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C and grease a 9-inch cake pan.|Mix flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.|Whisk eggs with sugar until pale and fluffy.|Slowly add olive oil while whisking.|Alternate adding flour mixture and milk, beginning and ending with flour.|Stir in lemon zest, orange zest, lemon juice, orange juice, and vanilla.|Pour batter into prepared pan.|Bake for 35-40 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.|Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack.

title: Freekeh Salad
summary: Roasted green wheat salad with vegetables and herbs
description: A nutritious Palestinian salad made with freekeh (roasted green wheat), fresh vegetables, and aromatic herbs. Dressed with olive oil and lemon juice, it is perfect as a side dish or light meal.
ingredients: 2 cups freekeh (roasted green wheat), cooked|3 large tomatoes, diced|2 cucumbers, diced|1 red bell pepper, diced|1 red onion, finely diced|1 bunch fresh parsley, chopped|1 bunch fresh mint, chopped|150 ml olive oil|100 ml lemon juice|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp sumac
instructions: Cook freekeh according to package directions and cool.|Combine cooked freekeh with diced tomatoes, cucumbers, bell pepper, and red onion.|Add fresh parsley and mint.|In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and sumac.|Pour dressing over the salad and toss well.|Let sit for 15 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.|Serve at room temperature.

title: Sancocho Panameño
summary: Hearty stew with meat, plantains, and root vegetables
description: A beloved Panamanian comfort food featuring tender meat, plantains, yams, and other root vegetables in a rich, flavorful broth. This warming stew is perfect for family meals.
ingredients: 1 kg beef or chicken, cubed|4 large plantains, peeled and cubed|3 yams, peeled and cubed|2 large potatoes, cubed|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp achiote paste|100 ml olive oil|8 cups beef or chicken broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown meat on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Stir in achiote paste and cook for 1 minute.|Return meat to the pot and add broth.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.|Add plantains, yams, potatoes, salt, pepper, and cumin.|Continue simmering for 30-40 minutes until vegetables are tender.|Serve hot in bowls.

title: Arroz con Pollo Panama
summary: Chicken and rice cooked together with vegetables and spices
description: A classic Panamanian dish featuring chicken and rice cooked together with bell peppers, peas, and aromatic spices. The rice absorbs all the flavors, creating a delicious one-pot meal.
ingredients: 1 kg chicken, cut into pieces|3 cups long-grain rice|1 large onion, diced|1 red bell pepper, diced|1 green bell pepper, diced|1 cup frozen peas|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp achiote paste|100 ml olive oil|6 cups chicken broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown chicken pieces, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion, bell peppers, and garlic until softened.|Stir in achiote paste and cook for 1 minute.|Add rice and toast for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.|Pour in chicken broth and return chicken to the pot.|Add salt, pepper, and cumin.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes.|Stir in peas and cook for 5 more minutes until rice is tender.|Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

title: Ceviche Panama
summary: Fresh fish cured in citrus juice with vegetables
description: A refreshing Panamanian seafood dish made with fresh fish cured in lime juice and mixed with tomatoes, onions, and cilantro. Perfect as an appetizer or light meal.
ingredients: 1 kg fresh white fish, cubed|300 ml fresh lime juice|4 large tomatoes, diced|1 red onion, finely diced|1 cucumber, diced|1 red bell pepper, diced|1 bunch fresh cilantro, chopped|2 jalapeños, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 avocado, sliced
instructions: Place cubed fish in a glass bowl and cover with lime juice.|Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until fish is opaque.|Drain excess lime juice, reserving about 100 ml.|Add diced tomatoes, red onion, cucumber, bell pepper, cilantro, and jalapeños.|Drizzle with olive oil and reserved lime juice.|Season with salt and pepper.|Gently toss and let sit for 15 minutes.|Serve chilled, topped with avocado slices.

title: Hojaldre
summary: Flaky pastry with savory meat filling
description: A Panamanian pastry made with crispy, flaky dough filled with seasoned ground meat. Often served as a snack or appetizer, these pastries are irresistibly delicious.
ingredients: 500 g all-purpose flour|250 g butter, cold and cubed|1 tsp salt|150 ml ice water|500 g ground beef|1 large onion, finely diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp tomato paste|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 egg, beaten
instructions: Make pastry dough by mixing flour and salt, then cutting in cold butter until mixture resembles breadcrumbs.|Add ice water gradually until dough comes together.|Wrap in plastic and refrigerate for 30 minutes.|Brown ground beef with onion and garlic.|Add tomato paste, cumin, salt, and pepper, simmer for 10 minutes.|Cool filling completely.|Roll out pastry and cut into 3-inch squares.|Place filling on each square, fold into triangle, and seal edges.|Brush with beaten egg and bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden.

title: Empanadas Panama
summary: Fried pastry pockets with meat or cheese filling
description: Popular Panamanian fried pastries filled with seasoned meat or cheese. Crispy on the outside and tender inside, they are perfect as a snack or appetizer.
ingredients: 500 g all-purpose flour|2 tsp baking powder|1 tsp salt|2 eggs|100 ml milk|2 tbsp olive oil|500 g ground beef|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 liter vegetable oil for frying
instructions: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt.|Whisk eggs with milk and olive oil.|Combine wet and dry ingredients to form a dough.|Brown ground beef with onion and garlic.|Add cumin, salt, and pepper, simmer for 10 minutes.|Cool filling completely.|Roll out dough and cut into 4-inch circles.|Place filling on each circle, fold in half, and seal edges.|Heat oil to 180°C and fry empanadas until golden brown.|Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

title: Carimañola
summary: Fried yuca pastry with meat filling
description: A traditional Panamanian snack made with yuca dough filled with seasoned ground meat. These golden fried pastries are crispy outside and soft inside.
ingredients: 1 kg yuca, peeled and cubed|2 eggs|100 g all-purpose flour|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|500 g ground beef|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 liter vegetable oil for frying
instructions: Boil yuca until very tender, about 20 minutes.|Drain and mash yuca until smooth.|Mix in eggs, flour, salt, and pepper to form a dough.|Brown ground beef with onion and garlic.|Add cumin, salt, and pepper, simmer for 10 minutes.|Cool filling completely.|Form yuca dough into oval shapes, make a pocket, and fill with meat.|Seal the opening and shape into a smooth oval.|Heat oil to 180°C and fry carimañolas until golden brown.|Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

title: Guacho
summary: Panamanian soup with seafood and vegetables
description: A hearty Panamanian seafood soup made with fish, shrimp, and vegetables in a flavorful broth. Perfect for a satisfying meal.
ingredients: 500 g white fish, cubed|300 g shrimp, peeled|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 large potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, sliced|1 red bell pepper, diced|100 ml olive oil|8 cups fish or seafood broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add potatoes and carrots, cook for 5 minutes.|Pour in fish broth and bring to a boil.|Add fish pieces and simmer for 10 minutes.|Add shrimp and bell pepper, cook for 5 more minutes.|Season with salt, pepper, and cumin.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Patacones Panama
summary: Twice-fried plantain slices with garlic sauce
description: A popular Panamanian side dish made with green plantains that are fried, flattened, and fried again until crispy. Served with a tangy garlic sauce.
ingredients: 4 large green plantains|1 liter vegetable oil for frying|6 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|100 ml lime juice|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Peel plantains and cut into 1-inch thick slices.|Heat vegetable oil to 180°C.|Fry plantain slices for 3-4 minutes until softened but not browned.|Remove and flatten each slice between two pieces of plastic wrap.|Return flattened slices to hot oil and fry until golden and crispy.|Drain on paper towels.|Make sauce by mixing garlic, olive oil, lime juice, salt, pepper, and cumin.|Serve patacones hot with garlic sauce for dipping.

title: Chicheme
description: A traditional Panamanian drink made with fresh corn, milk, and spices. Served warm, it is a comforting beverage perfect for breakfast or as a snack.
ingredients: 6 ears of fresh corn, kernels removed|1 liter whole milk|500 ml water|100 g sugar|1 tsp vanilla extract|1 tsp cinnamon|1 pinch nutmeg|1 pinch salt|2 tbsp butter
instructions: Blend corn kernels with water until smooth.|Strain through a fine mesh to extract corn milk.|Heat milk in a pot until steaming.|Add corn milk to hot milk, stirring constantly.|Add sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt.|Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.|Stir in butter.|Serve hot in mugs.

title: Bienmesabe
description: A delightful Panamanian dessert made with coconut and egg yolks, creating a rich and creamy treat. Often served during special occasions.
ingredients: 400 g shredded coconut|300 g sugar|200 ml water|10 egg yolks|100 ml condensed milk|1 tbsp vanilla extract|1 tsp cinnamon|1 pinch nutmeg|50 g butter
instructions: Make syrup by boiling sugar and water for 10 minutes.|Add shredded coconut to syrup and simmer for 5 minutes.|Cool syrup slightly.|Beat egg yolks with condensed milk until pale.|Slowly add cooled coconut syrup to egg yolks, stirring constantly.|Return mixture to low heat and cook, stirring constantly, until thickened.|Remove from heat and stir in vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and butter.|Pour into serving dishes and refrigerate until set.

title: Sopa de Pipa
description: A creamy Panamanian soup made with fresh coconut milk and seafood. Rich and flavorful, it is a specialty of the coastal regions.
ingredients: 2 fresh coconuts, grated|500 g white fish, cubed|300 g shrimp, peeled|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|2 large potatoes, cubed|100 ml olive oil|6 cups fish or seafood broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Extract milk from grated coconut by squeezing through cheesecloth.|Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add potatoes and fish broth, bring to a boil.|Add fish pieces and simmer for 10 minutes.|Add shrimp and coconut milk, cook for 5 more minutes.|Season with salt, pepper, and cumin.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Arroz con Coco Panama
summary: Rice cooked in coconut milk with spices
description: A fragrant Panamanian rice dish cooked in coconut milk, creating a creamy and aromatic side dish. Perfect to accompany seafood or meat dishes.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|1 fresh coconut, grated|4 cups water|1 large onion, diced|3 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp coriander|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Extract milk from grated coconut by squeezing through cheesecloth.|Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add rice and toast for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.|Pour in water and coconut milk.|Add salt, pepper, cumin, and coriander.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes.|Let rest for 5 minutes.|Fluff with a fork and garnish with fresh cilantro.

title: Tamals Panama
summary: Corn dough wrapped in banana leaves with filling
description: Traditional Panamanian tamales made with corn dough and filled with meat, wrapped in banana leaves, and steamed. A festive dish often served during celebrations.
ingredients: 2 cups masa harina (corn flour)|200 g butter, softened|1 cup chicken or vegetable broth|2 tsp baking powder|1 tsp salt|500 g pork or chicken, cooked and shredded|1 large onion, diced|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|20 banana leaves, softened
instructions: Mix masa harina, butter, broth, baking powder, and salt until light and fluffy.|Brown onion and garlic, add shredded meat, cumin, salt, and pepper.|Spread 2 tbsp masa on each banana leaf.|Place 1 tbsp meat filling in the center.|Fold banana leaf to enclose filling.|Stack tamales in a steamer pot with banana leaves between layers.|Steam for 45-50 minutes until masa is cooked through.|Serve hot in banana leaves.

title: Chicharrón Panama
summary: Fried pork belly with crispy skin
description: A popular Panamanian dish featuring fried pork belly with crispy, golden skin. Often served with rice, beans, and plantains.
ingredients: 1.5 kg pork belly with skin|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp paprika|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 large onions, sliced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Cut pork belly into 2-inch cubes.|Mix salt, pepper, cumin, paprika, and garlic.|Rub spice mixture all over pork pieces.|Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.|Fry pork pieces until skin is crispy and meat is cooked through, about 30-40 minutes.|Remove pork and set aside.|In the same pan, sauté onions until caramelized.|Return pork to pan and toss with onions.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Tortilla de Maíz Panama
summary: Corn tortilla made from fresh corn dough
description: Traditional Panamanian corn tortillas made from fresh corn dough. Soft and warm, they are perfect for wrapping around fillings or serving alongside meals.
ingredients: 2 cups masa harina (corn flour)|1.5 cups warm water|1 tsp salt|2 tbsp butter, melted
instructions: Mix masa harina and salt in a bowl.|Gradually add warm water, mixing until a soft dough forms.|Knead dough for 2-3 minutes until smooth.|Divide dough into 12 equal balls.|Place a ball between two pieces of plastic wrap.|Flatten with a tortilla press or the bottom of a pan to about 1/4 inch thick.|Heat a griddle or non-stick pan over medium-high heat.|Cook tortillas for about 1-2 minutes per side until lightly browned.|Brush with melted butter and serve warm.

title: Plátano Asado
summary: Grilled ripe plantain with butter and cinnamon
description: A simple yet delicious Panamanian side dish made with ripe plantains grilled until caramelized and served with butter and cinnamon.
ingredients: 4 ripe plantains|100 g butter|50 g brown sugar|2 tsp cinnamon|1 pinch salt|1 tbsp lime juice
instructions: Peel ripe plantains and cut lengthwise into 1/2-inch thick slices.|Heat a grill or griddle over medium-high heat.|Grill plantain slices for 3-4 minutes per side until caramelized.|Mix butter, brown sugar, cinnamon, and salt.|Brush grilled plantains with butter mixture.|Drizzle with lime juice.|Serve warm.

title: Raspao
summary: Shaved ice dessert with fruit syrup
description: A refreshing Panamanian frozen dessert made with shaved ice topped with colorful fruit syrups. Perfect for hot days.
ingredients: 1 kg ice|200 ml strawberry syrup|200 ml mango syrup|200 ml passion fruit syrup|100 ml condensed milk|2 tbsp sugar|1 tbsp vanilla extract
instructions: Crush ice finely using a blender or ice crusher.|Pack crushed ice into serving cups.|Drizzle with desired fruit syrups.|Mix condensed milk with sugar and vanilla.|Drizzle condensed milk mixture over the ice.|Serve immediately with a spoon.

title: Natilla Panama
description: A traditional Panamanian custard dessert made with milk, eggs, and sugar, flavored with cinnamon and vanilla. Served chilled, it is a light and elegant dessert.
ingredients: 1 liter whole milk|100 g sugar|8 egg yolks|50 g cornstarch|2 tbsp butter|1 tbsp vanilla extract|2 tsp cinnamon|1 pinch salt|1 pinch nutmeg
instructions: Heat milk with sugar until steaming.|Mix cornstarch with 100 ml cold milk until smooth.|Slowly pour cornstarch mixture into hot milk, stirring constantly.|Continue cooking and stirring until mixture thickens, about 5-7 minutes.|Beat egg yolks in a separate bowl.|Slowly add hot milk mixture to egg yolks, stirring constantly.|Return mixture to low heat and cook, stirring constantly, for 2-3 minutes.|Remove from heat and stir in butter, vanilla, cinnamon, salt, and nutmeg.|Pour into serving dishes and refrigerate until set.

title: Chichas
summary: Fried corn dough balls with cheese
description: Popular Panamanian fried snacks made with corn dough and filled with cheese. Crispy outside and soft inside, they are perfect as an appetizer or snack.
ingredients: 2 cups masa harina (corn flour)|1 cup warm water|1 tsp salt|200 g white cheese, cubed|1 liter vegetable oil for frying|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Mix masa harina and salt in a bowl.|Gradually add warm water, mixing until a soft dough forms.|Knead dough for 2-3 minutes until smooth.|Divide dough into 12 equal balls.|Flatten each ball, place a cheese cube in the center, and seal.|Heat oil to 180°C.|Fry chichas until golden brown on all sides, about 3-4 minutes.|Drain on paper towels.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Seco Herrerano
summary: Beef stew with vegetables and spices
description: A hearty Panamanian beef stew from the Herrera province, made with tender beef, vegetables, and aromatic spices. A comforting and satisfying dish.
ingredients: 1 kg beef, cubed|4 large potatoes, cubed|2 large onions, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 large tomatoes, diced|1 red bell pepper, diced|100 ml olive oil|6 cups beef broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp paprika|2 bay leaves
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown beef pieces on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add diced tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.|Return beef to the pot and add beef broth.|Add salt, pepper, cumin, paprika, and bay leaves.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour.|Add potatoes and bell pepper, continue simmering for 30-40 minutes until vegetables are tender.|Remove bay leaves and serve hot.

title: Mumu
summary: Traditional earth oven feast with meat and vegetables
description: A ceremonial Papua New Guinean dish where meat, fish, and vegetables are wrapped in banana leaves and cooked in an underground earth oven. A festive and communal meal.
ingredients: 1 kg pork or chicken, cubed|500 g fish, cubed|4 large sweet potatoes, cubed|3 large taro roots, cubed|2 bunches banana leaves|1 large onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml coconut milk|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced
instructions: Prepare banana leaves by softening over heat.|Layer banana leaves in a large pot or earth oven.|Arrange meat, fish, sweet potatoes, and taro in layers.|Sprinkle with onion, garlic, salt, pepper, and ginger.|Pour coconut milk over the layers.|Cover with more banana leaves.|If using earth oven, cover with soil and cook for 2-3 hours.|If using pot, cover tightly and simmer for 1.5-2 hours.|Unwrap and serve hot.

title: Saksak
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean dish made with sago starch and coconut milk, creating a creamy and satisfying pudding. Often served as a main course or side dish.
ingredients: 2 cups sago pearls|1 liter coconut milk|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add sago pearls and stir well.|Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.|Add salt, pepper, and ginger.|Cook, stirring frequently, for 20-25 minutes until sago is translucent and tender.|The mixture should be thick and creamy.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Kaukau
summary: Sweet potato staple with simple preparation
description: A staple Papua New Guinean dish made with sweet potatoes, boiled or roasted and served simply with salt and pepper. Often the foundation of meals.
ingredients: 2 kg sweet potatoes|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Peel sweet potatoes and cut into large chunks.|Boil in salted water for 20-25 minutes until very tender.|Drain well.|Heat olive oil and add ginger and garlic.|Toss cooked sweet potatoes with oil mixture.|Season with salt and pepper.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Pitpit Greens PNG
summary: Sago palm shoots with coconut milk
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean vegetable dish made with tender sago palm shoots cooked in coconut milk. Nutritious and flavorful.
ingredients: 1 kg sago palm shoots, sliced|800 ml coconut milk|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add sago palm shoots and stir well.|Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.|Add salt, pepper, and ginger.|Simmer for 15-20 minutes until shoots are tender.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Coconut Crab PNG
description: A delicacy in Papua New Guinea, coconut crab is grilled and served with aromatic spices. Rich and flavorful, it is a special occasion dish.
ingredients: 2 large coconut crabs, cleaned|100 ml coconut milk|100 ml lime juice|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Mix coconut milk, lime juice, garlic, ginger, olive oil, salt, pepper, and cumin.|Marinate crab pieces in the mixture for at least 1 hour.|Preheat grill to medium-high heat.|Grill crab pieces for 8-10 minutes per side until cooked through.|Brush with remaining marinade while grilling.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Sago Porridge PNG
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean breakfast made with sago pearls cooked in milk and coconut milk, creating a creamy and comforting porridge.
ingredients: 1 cup sago pearls|500 ml whole milk|500 ml coconut milk|100 g sugar|2 tbsp butter|1 tbsp vanilla extract|1 tsp cinnamon|1 pinch nutmeg|1 pinch salt
instructions: Bring milk and coconut milk to a simmer.|Slowly add sago pearls, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.|Add sugar, butter, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt.|Continue cooking and stirring for 20-25 minutes until sago is translucent and porridge is creamy.|Serve hot in bowls.

title: Aibika Greens PNG
summary: Leafy greens cooked with coconut milk
description: A nutritious Papua New Guinean vegetable dish made with aibika (a leafy green) cooked in coconut milk with aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1 kg aibika leaves, chopped|800 ml coconut milk|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add chopped aibika leaves and stir well.|Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.|Add salt, pepper, and ginger.|Simmer for 10-15 minutes until leaves are tender.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Roro Fish
summary: Fish cooked in taro leaf wraps
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean dish where fish is wrapped in taro leaves and cooked until tender. The taro leaves impart a subtle flavor to the fish.
ingredients: 1 kg white fish fillets|20 large taro leaves|800 ml coconut milk|1 large onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Blanch taro leaves in boiling water for 2 minutes to soften.|Wrap each fish fillet in a taro leaf.|Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Arrange wrapped fish in a pot.|Pour coconut milk over the fish.|Add salt, pepper, and ginger.|Cover and simmer for 20-25 minutes until fish is cooked through.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Tulip Leaves Stew
summary: Stew made with tulip plant leaves
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean stew made with tender tulip plant leaves cooked with meat and vegetables in a flavorful broth.
ingredients: 1 kg tulip leaves, chopped|500 g pork or chicken, cubed|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 large potatoes, cubed|100 ml olive oil|6 cups meat broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown meat pieces on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Return meat to the pot and add meat broth.|Add salt, pepper, and ginger.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.|Add potatoes and tulip leaves, continue simmering for 20-25 minutes until vegetables are tender.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Breadfruit Chips PNG
description: A popular Papua New Guinean snack made with sliced breadfruit that is fried until crispy and golden. Served with salt, it is a delicious and satisfying treat.
ingredients: 2 large breadfruits|1 liter vegetable oil for frying|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp paprika
instructions: Peel breadfruits and remove the core.|Slice breadfruit into 1/4-inch thick chips.|Heat oil to 180°C.|Fry breadfruit chips in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes.|Drain on paper towels.|Season with salt, pepper, and paprika.|Serve hot.

title: Pandanus Soup
summary: Soup made with pandanus leaves and seafood
description: A fragrant Papua New Guinean soup made with pandanus leaves that impart a unique flavor, combined with seafood and vegetables.
ingredients: 500 g white fish, cubed|300 g shrimp, peeled|10 pandanus leaves, chopped|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 large potatoes, cubed|100 ml olive oil|6 cups fish or seafood broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add potatoes and fish broth, bring to a boil.|Add pandanus leaves and simmer for 10 minutes to infuse flavor.|Add fish pieces and simmer for 10 minutes.|Add shrimp and cook for 5 more minutes.|Season with salt, pepper, and ginger.|Serve hot.

title: Taro Soup PNG
summary: Creamy soup made with taro root
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean soup made with taro root cooked until very tender and blended into a creamy consistency. Nutritious and comforting.
ingredients: 1 kg taro root, peeled and cubed|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|100 ml olive oil|6 cups vegetable or chicken broth|500 ml coconut milk|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp ginger, minced|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until softened.|Add taro root and broth, bring to a boil.|Reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes until taro is very tender.|Blend soup until smooth using an immersion blender.|Stir in coconut milk.|Season with salt, pepper, and ginger.|Simmer for 5 more minutes.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Coconut Milk Chicken PNG
summary: Chicken cooked in rich coconut milk sauce
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean dish featuring tender chicken cooked in a rich and creamy coconut milk sauce with aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1.5 kg chicken, cut into pieces|800 ml coconut milk|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown chicken pieces on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.|Return chicken to the pot.|Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.|Add salt, pepper, and cumin.|Cover and simmer for 30-35 minutes until chicken is cooked through.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice.

title: Lime Leaf Soup
summary: Aromatic soup with lime leaves and seafood
description: A fragrant Papua New Guinean soup made with lime leaves that provide a citrusy flavor, combined with seafood and vegetables.
ingredients: 500 g white fish, cubed|300 g shrimp, peeled|15 lime leaves, chopped|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml olive oil|6 cups fish or seafood broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.|Add fish broth and bring to a boil.|Add lime leaves and simmer for 10 minutes to infuse flavor.|Add fish pieces and simmer for 10 minutes.|Add shrimp and cook for 5 more minutes.|Season with salt and pepper.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Sweet Potato Chips PNG
summary: Fried sweet potato slices with spices
description: A popular Papua New Guinean snack made with sliced sweet potatoes that are fried until crispy and golden. Served with spices, it is a delicious treat.
ingredients: 2 kg sweet potatoes|1 liter vegetable oil for frying|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp paprika|1 tsp cumin
instructions: Peel sweet potatoes and slice into 1/4-inch thick chips.|Soak chips in cold water for 30 minutes to remove excess starch.|Drain and pat dry thoroughly.|Heat oil to 180°C.|Fry sweet potato chips in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 4-5 minutes.|Drain on paper towels.|Season with salt, pepper, paprika, and cumin.|Serve hot.

title: Jungle Chicken
summary: Wild chicken cooked with forest herbs and spices
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean dish made with jungle fowl cooked with wild herbs and spices found in the forest. Rich and flavorful.
ingredients: 1.5 kg jungle chicken or game bird, cut into pieces|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml olive oil|6 cups meat broth|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp coriander|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown chicken pieces on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.|Return chicken to the pot and add meat broth.|Add salt, pepper, cumin, and coriander.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45-50 minutes until chicken is very tender.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Pawpaw Curry PNG
summary: Green papaya curry with coconut milk
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean curry made with green papaya cooked in a rich coconut milk sauce with aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1 kg green papaya, peeled and cubed|500 g pork or chicken, cubed|800 ml coconut milk|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|1 tsp coriander|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown meat pieces on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.|Return meat to the pot.|Add green papaya and stir well.|Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.|Add salt, pepper, cumin, and coriander.|Cover and simmer for 30-35 minutes until papaya is tender and meat is cooked through.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice.

title: Fish Head Soup PNG
summary: Flavorful soup made with fish heads and vegetables
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean soup made with fish heads that create a rich and flavorful broth, combined with vegetables and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1 kg fish heads, cleaned|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|2 large potatoes, cubed|100 ml olive oil|8 cups water|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.|Add fish heads and water, bring to a boil.|Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes to create a flavorful broth.|Strain broth and discard fish heads.|Return broth to the pot and add potatoes.|Add salt, pepper, and cumin.|Simmer for 20-25 minutes until potatoes are tender.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Bush Turkey
summary: Wild turkey cooked with traditional spices
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean dish made with bush turkey (wild game bird) cooked with aromatic spices and vegetables. Rich and flavorful.
ingredients: 1.5 kg bush turkey or game bird, cut into pieces|1 large onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml olive oil|6 cups meat broth|2 large potatoes, cubed|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|1 tsp cumin|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown turkey pieces on all sides, remove and set aside.|Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.|Return turkey to the pot and add meat broth.|Add salt, pepper, and cumin.|Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes.|Add potatoes and continue simmering for 20-25 minutes until potatoes are tender.|Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Banana Leaf Fish PNG
summary: Fish steamed in banana leaf packets
description: A traditional Papua New Guinean dish where fish is wrapped in banana leaves with aromatic spices and steamed until tender. The banana leaves impart a subtle flavor.
ingredients: 1 kg white fish fillets|20 large banana leaves|1 large onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tbsp ginger, minced|100 ml coconut milk|100 ml lime juice|100 ml olive oil|2 tsp salt|1 tsp black pepper|2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
instructions: Soften banana leaves over heat.|Mix coconut milk, lime juice, garlic, ginger, salt, and pepper.|Place a fish fillet on each banana leaf.|Top with onion slices and pour coconut milk mixture over the fish.|Fold banana leaf to enclose fish completely.|Place packets in a steamer basket.|Steam for 20-25 minutes until fish is cooked through.|Carefully unwrap and serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Chipa
summary: Cheesy cornmeal bread rings, a beloved Paraguayan staple
description: Chipa is a traditional Paraguayan cheese bread made with cornmeal and fresh cheese. These ring-shaped breads are crispy on the outside and soft inside, perfect for breakfast or as a snack. They are often sold by street vendors and are a symbol of Paraguayan culture.
ingredients: 2 cups cornmeal|1 cup fresh cheese (queso fresco), crumbled|4 eggs|1/2 cup butter, melted|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon anise seeds|1/4 cup milk
instructions: Mix cornmeal, cheese, salt, and anise seeds in a bowl|Beat eggs and combine with melted butter and milk|Mix wet and dry ingredients until a dough forms|Shape into rings and place on greased baking sheet|Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes until golden brown

title: Sopa Paraguaya
summary: Creamy corn and cheese soup, a comfort food classic
description: Despite its name meaning "Paraguayan soup," Sopa Paraguaya is actually a thick, savory cornmeal cake baked in a pan. It combines fresh corn, cheese, and onions for a hearty dish that is both filling and delicious.
ingredients: 3 cups fresh corn kernels|1 cup fresh cheese, diced|1 large onion, finely chopped|4 eggs|1 cup cornmeal|1/2 cup milk|4 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Sauté onion in butter until translucent|Mix corn, cheese, cornmeal, salt, and pepper|Beat eggs with milk and combine with corn mixture|Add sautéed onion and mix well|Pour into greased baking pan and bake at 350°F for 40 minutes until golden

title: Bori Bori
summary: Cornmeal dumplings in savory broth with meat
description: Bori Bori is a traditional Paraguayan soup featuring small cornmeal dumplings cooked in a rich beef broth with vegetables. The dumplings are light and fluffy, absorbing the flavors of the broth.
ingredients: 1 lb beef, cubed|1 cup cornmeal|2 eggs|1 onion, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|6 cups beef broth|2 carrots, diced|1 cup fresh cheese, crumbled|2 tablespoons oil|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef with onion and garlic|Add beef broth and bring to boil|Mix cornmeal, eggs, and cheese to form dumpling dough|Drop spoonfuls of dough into boiling broth|Add carrots and simmer 30 minutes until dumplings are cooked through

title: Chipá Guazú
summary: Fresh corn and cheese cake, a seasonal delicacy
description: Chipá Guazú is a larger, softer version of chipa made with fresh corn kernels and cheese. This dish is typically prepared during corn season and is a favorite at family gatherings and celebrations.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh corn kernels|1 cup fresh cheese, crumbled|3 eggs|1 onion, finely chopped|1/2 cup milk|4 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|2 tablespoons fresh parsley, chopped
instructions: Blend corn kernels with milk until slightly chunky|Mix corn mixture with cheese, eggs, and salt|Sauté onion in butter and add to mixture|Pour into greased baking pan|Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes until set and golden

title: Mbeju
description: Mbeju are thin, crispy pancakes made from cassava starch and fresh cheese. They are cooked on a griddle until golden and crispy on the edges, creating a delightful contrast of textures.
ingredients: 2 cups cassava starch|1 cup fresh cheese, grated|4 eggs|1/2 cup milk|1 onion, finely chopped|2 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Mix cassava starch, cheese, salt, and onion|Beat eggs with milk and combine with dry ingredients|Heat butter on griddle over medium-high heat|Pour batter and cook 3-4 minutes per side until crispy|Serve hot with sour cream or fresh cheese

title: Mazamorra
summary: Creamy corn porridge with milk and sugar
description: Mazamorra is a traditional Paraguayan corn porridge made with fresh corn kernels cooked in milk and sweetened with sugar. It is often served as a breakfast dish or dessert.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh corn kernels|4 cups milk|1/2 cup sugar|2 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/4 teaspoon cinnamon|1 pinch salt
instructions: Blend corn kernels with 1 cup milk until smooth|Heat remaining milk in pot with butter|Strain corn mixture and add to hot milk|Stir constantly to prevent lumps|Add sugar, vanilla, and cinnamon; simmer 20 minutes until creamy

title: Vori Vori
summary: Herb and cheese dumplings in light broth
description: Vori Vori are delicate dumplings made with fresh herbs, cheese, and cornmeal, served in a light broth. This dish showcases the Paraguayan love of fresh herbs and simple, wholesome ingredients.
ingredients: 1 cup cornmeal|1 cup fresh cheese, crumbled|2 eggs|1/2 cup fresh parsley, chopped|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|6 cups vegetable broth|1 onion, chopped|2 tablespoons oil|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until soft|Add vegetable broth and bring to boil|Mix cornmeal, cheese, eggs, parsley, cilantro, and salt|Drop spoonfuls of mixture into boiling broth|Simmer 20 minutes until dumplings float and are cooked through

title: Sopa So'o
summary: Hearty beef and cornmeal soup with vegetables
description: Sopa So'o is a traditional Paraguayan beef soup thickened with cornmeal and enriched with fresh cheese. It is a warming, nourishing dish perfect for family meals.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs beef, cubed|1 cup cornmeal|1 onion, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|8 cups beef broth|2 carrots, diced|1 cup fresh cheese, crumbled|2 tablespoons oil|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef with onion and garlic|Add beef broth and bring to boil|Mix cornmeal with 1 cup cold water to form paste|Slowly add cornmeal paste to broth, stirring constantly|Add carrots, cheese, salt, and pepper; simmer 30 minutes

title: Kivevé
summary: Pumpkin and corn stew with fresh cheese
description: Kivevé is a creamy vegetable stew made with pumpkin, corn, and fresh cheese. This dish is both nutritious and delicious, representing the agricultural bounty of Paraguay.
ingredients: 2 lbs pumpkin, cubed|2 cups fresh corn kernels|1 cup fresh cheese, diced|1 onion, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|4 tablespoons butter|2 cups vegetable broth|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Melt butter and sauté onion and garlic|Add pumpkin cubes and cook 10 minutes|Add corn and vegetable broth|Simmer 20 minutes until pumpkin is tender|Stir in cheese and cook 5 more minutes until melted

title: Dulce de Mamón
summary: Sweet papaya preserve, a traditional dessert
description: Dulce de Mamón is a traditional Paraguayan sweet preserve made from papaya cooked with sugar until it reaches a jam-like consistency. It is often served with fresh cheese or as a dessert.
ingredients: 3 lbs papaya, peeled and cubed|2 cups sugar|1/4 cup water|2 tablespoons lime juice|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/4 teaspoon cinnamon
instructions: Combine papaya, sugar, and water in large pot|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer|Stir occasionally for 45 minutes until thick|Add lime juice, vanilla, and cinnamon|Cook 10 more minutes and cool before serving

title: Asado Paraguay
summary: Grilled meat with traditional Paraguayan spices
description: Asado Paraguay is a traditional grilled meat dish featuring beef or pork seasoned with simple spices and grilled over charcoal. It is often served with fresh bread and chimichurri sauce.
ingredients: 2 lbs beef or pork, cut into steaks|4 tablespoons olive oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon oregano|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|2 tablespoons fresh parsley, chopped
instructions: Mix olive oil, garlic, cumin, oregano, salt, and pepper|Rub mixture over meat and let marinate 30 minutes|Grill over medium-high heat 6-8 minutes per side for medium|Rest meat 5 minutes before serving|Garnish with fresh parsley

title: Butifarra Paraguay
description: Butifarra is a traditional Paraguayan sausage made with pork, fresh herbs, and spices. It is typically grilled and served with bread and fresh vegetables.
ingredients: 2 lbs ground pork|1/2 cup fresh parsley, chopped|1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 onion, finely chopped|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|Sausage casings
instructions: Mix pork, parsley, cilantro, garlic, onion, cumin, salt, and pepper|Stuff mixture into sausage casings|Refrigerate for at least 2 hours|Grill over medium heat 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally|Serve hot with bread and fresh vegetables

title: Mandioca Frita Paraguay
summary: Crispy fried cassava root, a popular side dish
description: Mandioca Frita is cassava root cut into sticks and deep-fried until golden and crispy. It is a popular side dish served with grilled meats and is similar to French fries but with a unique flavor.
ingredients: 2 lbs cassava root|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Peel cassava and cut into stick-shaped pieces|Soak in cold water for 30 minutes|Drain and pat dry thoroughly|Heat oil to 350°F and fry cassava in batches 8-10 minutes until golden|Drain on paper towels and season with salt and pepper

title: Palometa Fish
summary: Grilled freshwater fish with simple seasonings
description: Palometa is a freshwater fish popular in Paraguay, typically grilled whole or filleted and served with lime and fresh herbs. It has a delicate flavor and tender flesh.
ingredients: 2 whole palometa fish (2 lbs each)|4 tablespoons olive oil|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 limes, sliced|1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Clean fish and pat dry|Mix olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper|Rub mixture inside and outside fish|Place lime slices inside fish cavity|Grill over medium heat 8-10 minutes per side until cooked through

title: Arroz con Leche Paraguay
summary: Creamy rice pudding with cinnamon and raisins
description: Arroz con Leche is a traditional Paraguayan rice pudding made with rice, milk, sugar, and cinnamon. It is a comforting dessert often served at family gatherings.
ingredients: 1 cup short-grain rice|4 cups milk|1/2 cup sugar|1/4 cup raisins|2 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/2 teaspoon cinnamon|1 pinch salt
instructions: Cook rice in 2 cups water until tender|Heat milk with butter and sugar|Add cooked rice to milk mixture|Stir in raisins, vanilla, cinnamon, and salt|Simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until creamy

title: Chipá Almidon
summary: Starch-based cheese bread, a lighter variation
description: Chipá Almidón is a variation of chipa made with cassava starch instead of cornmeal, resulting in a lighter, more delicate texture. It is still rich with cheese and perfect for breakfast.
ingredients: 2 cups cassava starch|1 cup fresh cheese, crumbled|4 eggs|1/2 cup butter, melted|1/4 cup milk|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon anise seeds
instructions: Mix cassava starch, cheese, salt, and anise seeds|Beat eggs and combine with melted butter and milk|Mix wet and dry ingredients until dough forms|Shape into rings and place on greased baking sheet|Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes until golden

title: Tereré
summary: Cold herbal drink, a refreshing Paraguayan beverage
description: Tereré is a traditional Paraguayan cold drink made with yerba mate and fresh herbs, served in a gourd with a metal straw. It is a social drink often shared among friends and family.
ingredients: 1 cup yerba mate|1/4 cup fresh mint leaves|1/4 cup fresh lemongrass|2 tablespoons fresh ginger, sliced|1 lime, sliced|4 cups cold water|1 tablespoon honey
instructions: Combine yerba mate, mint, lemongrass, and ginger in gourd|Add cold water and let steep 5 minutes|Add lime slices and honey|Stir well and serve with metal straw|Refill with water as needed

title: Cocido Paraguayo
description: Cocido Paraguayo is a traditional stew made with beef, vegetables, and fresh cheese. It is a warming, nourishing dish that represents the comfort food of Paraguay.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs beef, cubed|2 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, diced|1 onion, chopped|3 cloves garlic, minced|1 cup fresh cheese, diced|6 cups beef broth|2 tablespoons oil|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Heat oil and brown beef with onion and garlic|Add beef broth and bring to boil|Add potatoes and carrots|Simmer 30 minutes until vegetables are tender|Stir in cheese and cook 5 more minutes

title: Mbejú Cheese
description: Mbejú Cheese is a variation of traditional mbejú with extra cheese filling. These pancakes are crispy on the outside and creamy on the inside, making them irresistible.
ingredients: 2 cups cassava starch|1.5 cups fresh cheese, grated|4 eggs|1/2 cup milk|1 onion, finely chopped|3 tablespoons butter|1 teaspoon salt|1/4 cup additional cheese for filling
instructions: Mix cassava starch, 1 cup cheese, salt, and onion|Beat eggs with milk and combine with dry ingredients|Heat butter on griddle over medium-high heat|Pour batter, add cheese filling in center, fold|Cook 3-4 minutes per side until crispy and golden

title: Reviro
summary: Cassava flour and cheese mixture, a traditional side
description: Reviro is a simple but delicious dish made by mixing cassava flour with melted cheese and butter. It is often served alongside grilled meats and is a staple of Paraguayan cuisine.
ingredients: 2 cups cassava flour|1 cup fresh cheese, crumbled|4 tablespoons butter|1 onion, finely chopped|2 cloves garlic, minced|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|2 tablespoons fresh parsley, chopped
instructions: Melt butter in large skillet over medium heat|Sauté onion and garlic until soft|Add cassava flour and stir constantly for 5 minutes|Add cheese, salt, and pepper|Cook 5 more minutes until cheese melts and mixture is golden

title: Mofongo
summary: Mashed plantains with garlic and pork cracklings
description: Mofongo is Puerto Rico's most iconic dish, made by mashing fried green plantains with garlic, olive oil, and crispy pork cracklings. It is typically served as a side dish or with a savory broth.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, peeled and cubed|1/2 cup pork cracklings (chicharrones)|6 cloves garlic, minced|1/4 cup olive oil|1/4 cup reserved frying oil|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|1/4 cup chicken broth
instructions: Heat oil and fry plantain cubes until golden, about 8 minutes|Drain plantains on paper towels|In mortar, combine garlic, salt, and pepper|Add fried plantains and cracklings, mash together|Form into mound and serve with warm broth

title: Pernil Puerto Rico
summary: Roasted pork leg with crispy skin, a holiday favorite
description: Pernil is a traditional Puerto Rican roasted pork leg with a crispy, golden skin. It is often served during holidays and special occasions, and is a centerpiece of Puerto Rican celebrations.
ingredients: 12 lb pork leg (pernil)|12 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons adobo seasoning|2 tablespoons sofrito|1/4 cup olive oil|2 tablespoons salt|1 tablespoon black pepper|2 cups water
instructions: Score skin in crosshatch pattern|Mix garlic, adobo, sofrito, olive oil, salt, and pepper|Rub mixture all over pork leg|Place in roasting pan with water|Roast at 350°F for 4-5 hours until skin is crispy and meat is tender

title: Arroz con Gandules
summary: Rice with pigeon peas and sofrito
description: Arroz con Gandules is a traditional Puerto Rican rice dish made with pigeon peas, sofrito, and adobo seasoning. It is a staple side dish served at family meals and celebrations.
ingredients: 2 cups long-grain rice|2 cups pigeon peas (gandules)|3 tablespoons sofrito|4 cups chicken broth|1/4 cup olive oil|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic|Add sofrito and cook 2 minutes|Add rice and stir to coat with oil|Add pigeon peas, chicken broth, adobo, and salt|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered 20 minutes until rice is tender

title: Lechón Asado
summary: Roasted suckling pig, a festive centerpiece
description: Lechón Asado is a whole roasted suckling pig that is a traditional centerpiece for Puerto Rican celebrations. The meat is tender and flavorful, with crispy skin.
ingredients: 20 lb suckling pig|1 cup garlic, minced|1/2 cup adobo seasoning|1/2 cup sofrito|1 cup olive oil|1/4 cup salt|2 tablespoons black pepper|2 cups water
instructions: Clean and pat dry suckling pig|Mix garlic, adobo, sofrito, olive oil, salt, and pepper|Rub mixture inside and outside pig|Place on spit or in large roasting pan|Roast at 325°F for 5-6 hours, basting frequently, until skin is crispy

title: Asopao Puerto Rico
summary: Creamy rice stew with chicken and vegetables
description: Asopao is a Puerto Rican rice stew that is creamy and flavorful, made with chicken, vegetables, and sofrito. It is a comfort food that is both hearty and delicious.
ingredients: 2 lbs chicken, cubed|2 cups long-grain rice|1/4 cup sofrito|6 cups chicken broth|1 onion, diced|1 red bell pepper, diced|1 cup peas|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|2 tablespoons olive oil
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown chicken with onion and garlic|Add sofrito and cook 2 minutes|Add rice and stir to coat|Add chicken broth, bell pepper, peas, and adobo|Simmer 25 minutes until rice is tender and creamy

title: Pasteles PR
summary: Plantain and meat-filled dumplings wrapped in banana leaves
description: Pasteles are traditional Puerto Rican dumplings made with a plantain and yuca dough filled with seasoned pork, wrapped in banana leaves, and boiled. They are a labor of love often made during holidays.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, grated|2 cups yuca, grated|1 lb pork, seasoned and cooked|1/2 cup sofrito|1/4 cup olive oil|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|1 teaspoon salt|Banana leaves
instructions: Mix grated plantains and yuca with sofrito, olive oil, adobo, and salt|Spread mixture on banana leaf squares|Add spoonful of seasoned pork in center|Fold banana leaf to enclose filling|Boil in salted water for 45 minutes until dough is cooked through

title: Tostones PR
summary: Twice-fried plantain slices, crispy and savory
description: Tostones are green plantain slices that are fried, flattened, and fried again until crispy. They are a popular side dish and snack in Puerto Rico.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, peeled|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons olive oil
instructions: Cut plantains into 1-inch thick slices|Heat oil to 350°F and fry plantain slices 3-4 minutes until soft|Remove and flatten each slice with tostonera or glass|Fry again 2-3 minutes until golden and crispy|Drain and season with salt, pepper, and garlic oil

title: Tembleque
summary: Creamy coconut milk pudding, a traditional dessert
description: Tembleque is a traditional Puerto Rican dessert made with coconut milk, cornstarch, and sugar. It has a delicate, jiggly texture and is often served with cinnamon.
ingredients: 2 cups coconut milk|1 cup whole milk|1/2 cup sugar|1/4 cup cornstarch|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/4 teaspoon cinnamon|1 pinch salt
instructions: Mix cornstarch with 1/4 cup cold milk|Heat coconut milk and whole milk with sugar|Slowly add cornstarch mixture, stirring constantly|Cook 10 minutes until thickened, stirring frequently|Add vanilla and pour into serving dish; chill and serve with cinnamon

title: Coquito
summary: Puerto Rican eggnog with rum and spices
description: Coquito is a traditional Puerto Rican holiday drink made with coconut milk, evaporated milk, rum, and warm spices. It is similar to eggnog and is served during Christmas celebrations.
ingredients: 1 can (14 oz) coconut milk|1 can (12 oz) evaporated milk|1 can (12 oz) sweetened condensed milk|1 cup dark rum|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/2 teaspoon cinnamon|1/4 teaspoon nutmeg|1/4 teaspoon cloves
instructions: Combine coconut milk, evaporated milk, and condensed milk|Add rum, vanilla, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves|Stir well and refrigerate for at least 2 hours|Stir before serving|Serve chilled in small glasses

title: Pionono
summary: Plantain roll filled with meat, a savory pastry
description: Pionono is a Puerto Rican dish made with mashed plantains formed into a roll and filled with seasoned ground meat. It is then baked until golden and served as a main course.
ingredients: 6 ripe plantains, peeled|1 lb ground beef|1/2 cup sofrito|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|2 tablespoons olive oil|1 egg, beaten|1/2 cup breadcrumbs
instructions: Boil plantains until tender, then mash|Heat olive oil and brown ground beef with onion and garlic|Add sofrito and adobo, simmer 10 minutes|Spread mashed plantains on parchment, add meat filling|Roll tightly, brush with egg, coat with breadcrumbs, and bake at 375°F for 25 minutes

title: Sancocho PR
description: Sancocho is a traditional Puerto Rican stew made with beef, root vegetables, and plantains. It is a warming, nourishing dish that is perfect for family meals.
ingredients: 2 lbs beef, cubed|2 yams, cubed|2 plantains, cubed|2 potatoes, cubed|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1/4 cup sofrito|8 cups beef broth|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|2 tablespoons olive oil
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown beef with onion and garlic|Add sofrito and cook 2 minutes|Add beef broth and adobo, bring to boil|Add yams, plantains, and potatoes|Simmer 45 minutes until vegetables are tender

title: Carne Guisada
summary: Beef stew with tomato sauce and vegetables
description: Carne Guisada is a Puerto Rican beef stew made with tomato sauce, olives, and vegetables. It is a flavorful, comforting dish often served over rice.
ingredients: 2 lbs beef, cubed|1 can (8 oz) tomato sauce|1 onion, diced|1 red bell pepper, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1/2 cup green olives, sliced|1/4 cup sofrito|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|2 tablespoons olive oil|1 cup beef broth
instructions: Heat olive oil and brown beef with onion and garlic|Add sofrito and cook 2 minutes|Add tomato sauce, adobo, and beef broth|Add bell pepper and olives|Simmer 45 minutes until beef is tender

title: Bacalaítos
summary: Salted cod fritters, a popular appetizer
description: Bacalaítos are Puerto Rican fritters made with salted cod and a light batter. They are crispy on the outside and tender inside, perfect as an appetizer or snack.
ingredients: 1 lb salted cod, soaked and shredded|1 cup all-purpose flour|1/2 cup cornstarch|1 teaspoon baking powder|1 egg|3/4 cup water|1/4 cup sofrito|2 cloves garlic, minced|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying
instructions: Mix flour, cornstarch, and baking powder|Whisk egg with water and add to dry ingredients|Fold in shredded cod, sofrito, and garlic|Heat oil to 350°F|Drop spoonfuls of batter and fry 3-4 minutes per side until golden

title: Alcapurrias
summary: Plantain and yuca fritters with meat filling
description: Alcapurrias are Puerto Rican fritters made with a plantain and yuca dough filled with seasoned meat. They are deep-fried until golden and crispy.
ingredients: 4 green plantains, grated|2 cups yuca, grated|1 lb ground beef, seasoned|1/2 cup sofrito|1 onion, diced|4 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon adobo seasoning|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Mix grated plantains and yuca with sofrito and salt|Brown ground beef with onion, garlic, and adobo|Form plantain mixture into oval shapes|Fill with meat mixture and seal|Fry in 350°F oil 4-5 minutes per side until golden

title: Pinchos PR
summary: Grilled meat skewers with Caribbean spices
description: Pinchos are Puerto Rican grilled meat skewers marinated in adobo and sofrito. They are a popular street food and party favorite.
ingredients: 2 lbs pork or beef, cubed|1/2 cup sofrito|1/4 cup olive oil|6 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons adobo seasoning|1 tablespoon vinegar|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|Wooden skewers, soaked
instructions: Mix sofrito, olive oil, garlic, adobo, vinegar, salt, and pepper|Marinate meat in mixture for at least 2 hours|Thread meat onto skewers|Grill over medium-high heat 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally|Serve hot with lime wedges

title: Maduros
description: Maduros are ripe plantains sliced and fried until caramelized and sweet. They are a popular side dish and dessert in Puerto Rico.
ingredients: 4 ripe plantains, peeled|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying|1 teaspoon salt|1 tablespoon cinnamon sugar (optional)
instructions: Cut plantains into diagonal slices|Heat oil to 350°F|Fry plantain slices 3-4 minutes per side until golden and caramelized|Drain on paper towels|Serve hot, optionally dusted with cinnamon sugar

title: Mallorca Sandwich
summary: Sweet pastry sandwich with ham and cheese
description: Mallorca Sandwich is a Puerto Rican breakfast favorite made with a sweet spiral pastry filled with ham, cheese, and sometimes pineapple. It is often served with café con leche.
ingredients: 4 mallorca pastries (or croissants)|8 slices ham|8 slices Swiss cheese|2 tablespoons butter, softened|2 tablespoons pineapple jam (optional)|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Slice mallorca pastries horizontally|Spread butter on both sides|Layer ham and cheese on bottom half|Add pineapple jam if desired|Top with other half and serve immediately

title: Sofrito Base
summary: Aromatic base for Puerto Rican cooking
description: Sofrito is the aromatic base of Puerto Rican cuisine, made with cilantro, culantro, peppers, onions, and garlic. It is used in countless dishes and can be made fresh or stored.
ingredients: 1 bunch cilantro, chopped|1 bunch culantro, chopped|1 red bell pepper, chopped|1 onion, chopped|8 cloves garlic, minced|1/4 cup olive oil|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Combine cilantro, culantro, bell pepper, onion, and garlic|Blend until smooth paste forms|Heat olive oil in skillet|Add paste and cook 5 minutes, stirring constantly|Cool and store in refrigerator for up to 2 weeks

title: Tres Leches PR
summary: Sponge cake soaked in three types of milk
description: Tres Leches is a Puerto Rican dessert made with a light sponge cake soaked in three types of milk: evaporated, condensed, and heavy cream. It is topped with whipped cream.
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour|1 teaspoon baking powder|1/4 teaspoon salt|5 eggs, separated|1 cup sugar|1/3 cup milk|1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk|1 can (12 oz) evaporated milk|1 cup heavy cream|2 tablespoons sugar for topping
instructions: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt|Beat egg yolks with 1 cup sugar until pale|Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form|Fold whites into yolks, then fold in flour mixture|Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes; cool and poke holes throughout|Mix condensed, evaporated, and heavy cream; pour over cake|Chill 4 hours and top with whipped cream

title: Piña Colada
summary: Tropical rum cocktail with pineapple and coconut
description: Piña Colada is Puerto Rico's national drink, made with rum, pineapple juice, and coconut cream. It is a refreshing tropical cocktail perfect for warm weather.
ingredients: 2 oz white rum|3 oz pineapple juice|2 oz coconut cream|1 cup crushed ice|1 pineapple wedge for garnish|1 cherry for garnish
instructions: Add rum, pineapple juice, and coconut cream to blender|Add crushed ice|Blend until smooth and creamy|Pour into chilled glass|Garnish with pineapple wedge and cherry

title: Machboos Qatar
summary: Spiced rice with meat and vegetables
description: Machboos is a traditional Qatari rice dish made with fragrant basmati rice, tender meat, and aromatic spices. It is a staple of Qatari cuisine and is often served at celebrations.
ingredients: 2 lbs lamb or beef, cubed|3 cups basmati rice|1 large onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ghee|1 tablespoon tomato paste|6 cups meat broth|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat ghee and brown meat with onion and garlic|Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes|Add cumin, coriander, and black pepper|Add meat broth and simmer 30 minutes|Add rice and salt, bring to boil, then simmer covered 20 minutes until rice is tender

title: Harees Qatar
description: Harees is a traditional Qatari dish made with wheat grains and meat cooked slowly until they form a creamy porridge. It is often served during Ramadan.
ingredients: 2 cups wheat grains, soaked overnight|2 lbs lamb, cubed|1 large onion, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|4 tablespoons ghee|8 cups water|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Heat ghee and brown lamb with onion and garlic|Add wheat grains and water|Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer 2-3 hours|Stir occasionally and add water as needed|Add cumin, salt, and pepper; cook until mixture is creamy and thick

title: Balaleet Qatar
summary: Sweet vermicelli noodles with savory meat
description: Balaleet is a unique Qatari dish combining sweet vermicelli noodles with savory spiced meat. It is often served for breakfast or special occasions.
ingredients: 1 lb lamb, ground|8 oz vermicelli noodles|1 large onion, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tablespoons ghee|1 tablespoon tomato paste|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon cinnamon|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|1 teaspoon salt|2 tablespoons sugar
instructions: Heat ghee and brown ground lamb with onion and garlic|Add tomato paste, cumin, cinnamon, pepper, and salt|Simmer 15 minutes|Cook vermicelli according to package directions|Toss noodles with sugar and layer with meat mixture

title: Saloona Qatar
summary: Vegetable and meat stew with aromatic spices
description: Saloona is a traditional Qatari stew made with vegetables, meat, and aromatic spices. It is a comforting dish often served with rice.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs lamb or beef, cubed|3 potatoes, cubed|2 carrots, diced|1 onion, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tablespoons ghee|1 tablespoon tomato paste|4 cups meat broth|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat ghee and brown meat with onion and garlic|Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes|Add meat broth, cumin, coriander, and salt|Add potatoes and carrots|Simmer 45 minutes until vegetables are tender

title: Maraq Qatar
description: Maraq is a traditional Qatari meat broth flavored with aromatic spices. It is often served as a soup course or used as a base for other dishes.
ingredients: 2 lbs lamb or beef bones|1 lb lamb or beef, cubed|1 large onion, quartered|6 cloves garlic, minced|8 cups water|1 tablespoon ghee|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Heat ghee and brown meat with onion and garlic|Add water and bones|Bring to boil and skim foam|Add cumin, coriander, pepper, and salt|Simmer 2 hours until broth is rich and flavorful

title: Ghuzi Qatar
summary: Fragrant rice with roasted lamb and nuts
description: Ghuzi is a festive Qatari dish made with fragrant rice topped with roasted lamb and garnished with nuts and raisins. It is often served at celebrations.
ingredients: 3 lbs lamb, whole or large pieces|3 cups basmati rice|1 large onion, sliced|4 cloves garlic, minced|4 tablespoons ghee|6 cups meat broth|1/2 cup almonds, sliced|1/2 cup raisins|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon salt
instructions: Roast lamb at 400°F for 45 minutes until cooked through|Heat ghee and sauté onion and garlic|Add rice and toast 2 minutes|Add meat broth and cumin|Simmer covered 20 minutes until rice is tender|Top with roasted lamb, almonds, and raisins

title: Madrouba
description: Madrouba is a traditional Qatari dish made by mixing cooked rice with meat and broth until it reaches a creamy consistency. It is comfort food at its finest.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs lamb or beef, cubed|2 cups basmati rice|1 large onion, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|4 tablespoons ghee|6 cups meat broth|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Heat ghee and brown meat with onion and garlic|Add meat broth, cumin, salt, and pepper|Simmer 30 minutes|Add rice and bring to boil|Simmer covered 20 minutes, then stir vigorously until creamy

title: Muhammar Qatar
summary: Sweet rice with dates and ghee
description: Muhammar is a traditional Qatari sweet rice dish made with dates and ghee. It is often served for breakfast or as a dessert.
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice|1 cup dates, pitted and chopped|4 tablespoons ghee|3 cups water|1/4 cup sugar|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/4 teaspoon cardamom|1 pinch salt
instructions: Heat ghee and add rice, toasting 2 minutes|Add water and salt, bring to boil|Simmer covered 15 minutes|Add dates, sugar, vanilla, and cardamom|Cook 5 more minutes until rice is tender and dates are soft

title: Samboosa Qatar
summary: Crispy pastry triangles with meat filling
description: Samboosa are traditional Qatari pastries made with thin dough filled with spiced meat. They are deep-fried until crispy and golden.
ingredients: 1 lb ground lamb or beef|1 large onion, finely chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|2 tablespoons ghee|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon coriander|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|1 teaspoon salt|1 package phyllo dough|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying
instructions: Heat ghee and brown ground meat with onion and garlic|Add cumin, coriander, pepper, and salt; cook 10 minutes|Cool filling|Cut phyllo into strips and fill with meat mixture|Fold into triangles and fry in 350°F oil until golden

title: Luqaimat Qatar
summary: Sweet fried dough balls with date syrup
description: Luqaimat are traditional Qatari sweet pastries made from fried dough balls drizzled with date syrup and sprinkled with sesame seeds. They are a popular dessert.
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour|1 teaspoon baking powder|1/2 teaspoon salt|1 egg|3/4 cup water|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|2 quarts vegetable oil for frying|1 cup date syrup|2 tablespoons sesame seeds
instructions: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt|Whisk egg with water and vanilla|Combine wet and dry ingredients to form batter|Heat oil to 350°F|Drop spoonfuls of batter and fry 2-3 minutes per side until golden|Drizzle with date syrup and sprinkle with sesame seeds

title: Khabees Qatar
description: Khabees is a traditional Qatari dessert made with dates and semolina cooked together until thick and creamy. It is often served with ghee and nuts.
ingredients: 2 cups dates, pitted|1 cup semolina|1/2 cup ghee|2 cups water|1/4 cup almonds, sliced|1/4 cup pistachios, sliced|1 teaspoon cardamom
instructions: Cook dates with water until soft, about 15 minutes|Mash dates into paste|Heat ghee and toast semolina 5 minutes|Add date paste and cardamom|Cook 10 minutes, stirring constantly, until thick|Top with almonds and pistachios

title: Jireesh Qatar
description: Jireesh is a traditional Qatari dish made with cracked wheat and meat cooked together until creamy. It is often served during Ramadan.
ingredients: 2 cups cracked wheat|1.5 lbs lamb or beef, cubed|1 large onion, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|4 tablespoons ghee|6 cups meat broth|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Heat ghee and brown meat with onion and garlic|Add meat broth and bring to boil|Add cracked wheat, cumin, salt, and pepper|Simmer 45 minutes, stirring occasionally|Continue cooking until mixture is thick and creamy

title: Muhallabia Qatar
summary: Creamy milk pudding with rose water
description: Muhallabia is a traditional Qatari dessert made with milk, cornstarch, and rose water. It has a delicate, creamy texture and is often topped with nuts.
ingredients: 4 cups whole milk|1/4 cup cornstarch|1/2 cup sugar|1 teaspoon rose water|1/4 teaspoon cardamom|1/4 cup pistachios, sliced|1/4 cup almonds, sliced
instructions: Mix cornstarch with 1/2 cup cold milk|Heat remaining milk with sugar|Slowly add cornstarch mixture, stirring constantly|Cook 10 minutes until thickened|Add rose water and cardamom|Pour into serving dishes and chill|Top with pistachios and almonds

title: Thareed Qatar
summary: Bread and meat stew with vegetables
description: Thareed is a traditional Qatari dish made with pieces of bread layered with meat stew. It is a hearty, satisfying meal.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs lamb or beef, cubed|1 large onion, chopped|4 cloves garlic, minced|3 tablespoons ghee|1 tablespoon tomato paste|4 cups meat broth|2 potatoes, cubed|1 teaspoon cumin|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|4 cups bread, cubed
instructions: Heat ghee and brown meat with onion and garlic|Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes|Add meat broth, potatoes, cumin, salt, and pepper|Simmer 40 minutes until meat is tender|Layer bread cubes in serving dish|Pour meat stew over bread

title: Shawarma Qatar
summary: Marinated meat cooked on vertical spit
description: Shawarma is a popular Middle Eastern dish made with marinated meat cooked on a vertical spit. In Qatar, it is often served in pita bread with tahini sauce.
ingredients: 3 lbs lamb or chicken, thinly sliced|1/4 cup olive oil|4 tablespoons vinegar|6 cloves garlic, minced|1 tablespoon cumin|1 tablespoon coriander|1 teaspoon paprika|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: Mix olive oil, vinegar, garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper|Marinate meat for at least 4 hours|Cook on vertical spit or in oven at 400°F for 30 minutes|Slice thinly and serve in pita bread|Top with tahini sauce and vegetables

title: Karak Tea
summary: Spiced tea with milk and cardamom
description: Karak Tea is a popular Qatari beverage made with strong black tea, milk, and aromatic spices. It is often served with dates or pastries.
ingredients: 4 cups water|4 black tea bags|1 cup evaporated milk|3 tablespoons sugar|4 cardamom pods, crushed|2 cloves|1 cinnamon stick|1 pinch salt
instructions: Boil water with cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon|Add tea bags and steep 5 minutes|Add evaporated milk and sugar|Simmer 5 minutes|Strain and serve hot

title: Sago Pudding Qatar
summary: Creamy sago pearls with milk and rose water
description: Sago Pudding is a traditional Qatari dessert made with sago pearls cooked in milk and flavored with rose water and cardamom.
ingredients: 1 cup sago pearls|4 cups whole milk|1/2 cup sugar|1 teaspoon rose water|1/4 teaspoon cardamom|1/4 cup pistachios, sliced|1/4 cup almonds, sliced
